claims.claim: Islam [is] fuelling a global war/Holocaust on Christians [Twitter, 4 October 2013]
claims.notes: Pre-2015 but included for documented pattern continuity. New Statesman confirms tweet cluster: also tweeted 6 Oct: 'sharia legalises paedophilia'. Two days before publicised Quilliam 'defection'.

claims.claim: sharia legalises paedophilia [Twitter, 6 October 2013 — two days before publicised 'defection' to Quilliam Foundation]
claims.notes: New Statesman confirms tweet date (6 October 2013). Also tweeted 4 Oct: Islam fuelling 'global war/Holocaust on Christians'; 5 Oct: '2 more muslim paedos caught in Bristol'. Tweets directly contradict the Quilliam 'defection' narrative two days later.

claims.claim: Parts of the UK have become 'unrecognisable' due to the impact of mass immigration over the past decade.
claims.notes: UKIP spring conference. BBC reported. Farage said he felt 'slightly awkward' hearing other languages on a London-to-Kent train. No formal fact-check verdict; documented as reported public speech.

claims.claim: As an operator, but not as a human being, I would say Putin. The way he played the whole Syria thing. Brilliant.
claims.verdict_source: Guardian; Sky News; PA Media fact-check
claims.notes: GQ Magazine interview published by Guardian/Sky News 31 March 2014. Farage named Putin as the world leader he most admired 'as an operator'. PA Media fact-check (March 2025) confirmed comments genuine but >10 years old. Farage never formally disowned the statement. Resurfaced during 2022 Ukraine invasion and June 2024 election campaign. Sky News original: https://news.sky.com/story/nigel-farage-putin-is-leader-i-admire-most-10411596

claims.claim: [Video recorded inside Bradford mosques and others in Glasgow/Luton/East London; confronted worshippers about 'grooming gangs'; proselytised Christianity]
claims.verdict: Condemned as intimidatory and provocative; all major Christian denominations denounced Britain First
claims.verdict_source: ADL report; Hope Not Hate; Wikipedia (citing multiple press sources)
claims.platform_corrected: No — Facebook removed video content in March 2018
claims.impact: Jan 2025 grooming-gangs episode: Musk net favourability fell to -35 but Reform polling held at record highs (More in Common; YouGov Jan 2025).
claims.notes: May 2014 mosque invasions in Bradford and elsewhere. Members presented army-issue Bibles and leaflets on grooming gangs. Golding told councillors they 'had been warned'. MP George Galloway demanded police action. Led to court injunction banning Golding from mosques. Source also: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Britain_First

claims.claim: I was asked if a group of Romanian men moved in next to you, would you be concerned? And if you lived in London, I think you would be. [When asked what the difference was versus German children:] You know what the difference is.
claims.verdict_source: BBC News; ITV News; Hope Not Hate
claims.notes: LBC interview with James O'Brien, 16 May 2014. Farage's refusal to explain the difference was widely condemned as racist by Labour (Yvette Cooper, Diane Abbott, David Lammy). Farage issued partial apology then re-defended comments. ITV News also reported: https://www.itv.com/news/story/2014-05-17/farage-defends-fair-minded-romania-comments/

claims.claim: People who do not have HIV, to be frank. That's a good start. And people with a skill. [On who should be allowed to immigrate to the UK]
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: Newsweek Europe interview, October 2014. Farage said immigrants to the UK should not have HIV. Direct precursor to 2015 leaders-debate HIV claims. Documented by Hope Not Hate and Wikipedia political positions article.

claims.claim: [Launched Pegida UK in 2015, promoting the narrative that Western Europe faces an 'Islamisation' threat — framing Muslim communities as an organised political/religious invasion force]
claims.notes: Hope Not Hate case file confirms 2015 Pegida UK launch. BBC audio b06yfm8b: 'Tommy Robinson's Pegida Ambition' documents Robinson's anti-Muslim framing in this period. Pegida stands for 'Patriotic Europeans Against the Islamisation of the West'.

claims.claim: [Harassed sister-in-law of a man allegedly linked to 7/7 bombings; turned up at wrong address]
claims.verdict: Guilty — harassment; fined £325 plus £100 for wearing political uniform
claims.verdict_source: Chelmsford Magistrates' Court ruling; Tell MAMA
claims.notes: District Judge David Woollard found Golding guilty of harassing sister-in-law of 7/7-linked man (mistaken address). Fined £325 + £100 for political uniform. Court proceeding documented.

claims.claim: There is a very strong argument that the events [Charlie Hebdo Paris attack] are a result of having a fifth column living within these countries. We've got people living in these countries, holding our passports, who hate us.
claims.notes: Channel 4 News interview 8 January 2015 following Charlie Hebdo attack. Home Secretary Theresa May called the 'fifth column' reference 'irresponsible'. Deputy PM Nick Clegg called it 'firmly grabbing the wrong end of the stick'. YouTube clip: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5hIEyg_QKvc

claims.claim: Here's a fact — there are 7,000 diagnoses in this country every year of people that are HIV positive … 60% of them are not British nationals. What we need to do is to put the NHS there for British people and families, who in many cases have paid into the system for decades.
claims.notes: ITV leaders debate 2 April 2015. Full Fact: annual new HIV diagnoses were ~6,000 not 7,000; data records country of birth not nationality; 54% (not 60%) of those with reported birthplace born outside UK, with 17% unreported. Most tweeted moment of the debate. BBC reported Farage defended comments: https://www.bbc.com/news/election-2015-32176826. Plaid Cymru leader Leanne Wood: 'scaremongering'.

claims.claim: Make no mistake, these migrants are like cockroaches. They might look a bit 'Bob Geldof's Ethiopia circa 1984', but they are built to survive a nuclear bomb. They are survivors. Once gunships have driven them back to their shores, boats need to be confiscated and burned on a huge bonfire.
claims.verdict: Hate speech — language similar to pro-genocide propaganda
claims.verdict_source: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights Zeid Ra'ad Al Hussein; IPSO received 100+ complaints; 200k+ petition to sack
claims.platform_corrected: No — Sun published without correction; Hopkins questioned by police; no charge brought
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: Headline: 'Rescue boats? I'd use gunships to stop migrants'. UN compared language to pre-genocide Rwandan propaganda. Metropolitan Police investigated; no charges filed. Sun removed article later. IPSO ruling upheld complaint. Police questioned Hopkins but gave all-clear 6 months later. Source also: https://www.bbc.com/news/newsbeat-32391620

claims.claim: Show me pictures of coffins, show me bodies floating in water, play violins and show me skinny people looking sad. I still don't care.
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: Same Sun column as above. Cited alongside cockroaches quote in UN condemnation. Source: https://www.bbc.com/news/newsbeat-32391620

claims.claim: Isis are now using this [refugee] route to put their jihadists on European soil. The majority that are coming are economic migrants. We must be mad to take this risk.
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: European Parliament speech 9 September 2015 during Mediterranean migrant crisis. Claim about ISIS systematically using refugee route was later directly rebutted by Europol director Rob Wainwright (see 2016-06-07 row). BBC reported.

claims.claim: There is a 'fifth column' of Islamic extremists in the United Kingdom. British Muslims have split loyalties between their country and their faith.
claims.verdict_source: Wikipedia (political positions of Nigel Farage); Middle East Eye
claims.notes: Channel 4 documentary, approximately late 2015. Wikipedia political positions article documents claim about 'split loyalties'. Date is approximate. Part of a documented pattern of 'fifth column' rhetoric around Paris attacks and refugee crisis (see Jan 2015 and Mar 2017 rows).

claims.claim: I'd personally send every adult Muslim that has come into the EU over the past 12 months back tomorrow if I could. Fake refugees.
claims.verdict: Documented by MEND and multiple secondary sources; reflects dehumanising characterisation of Muslim migrants as fraudulent
claims.platform_corrected: Yes — Twitter later permanently banned account citing hateful conduct
claims.notes: Tweet confirmed by MEND article (2018-03-29) citing January 2016 date; also cited in CBC (2018-09-30) at https://www.cbc.ca/news/world/tommy-robinson-views-islam-1.4839120. Original tweet URL unavailable — account banned March 2018.

claims.claim: [Led Pegida UK relaunch rally in Birmingham describing Islam as an existential threat to the West and presenting Muslim immigration as invasion]
claims.verdict: Documented by multiple reputable press outlets; rally characterised by anti-Muslim content; fewer than 200 attendees
claims.verdict_source: BBC / Muslim News / Hope Not Hate
claims.notes: Pegida UK relaunch confirmed. Robinson described Islam as the enemy of the West. BBC Radio report 'Tommy Robinson's Pegida Ambition' (BBC audio b06yfm8b). Hope Not Hate case file confirms 2015 Pegida UK launch: https://hopenothate.org.uk/case-files-stephen-lennon/

claims.claim: The EU's migration policy has led to 5,000 jihadis entering the EU in the past 15 months.
claims.platform: TV (BBC Question Time and other appearances)
claims.verdict_source: The Independent (reporting Europol Director Rob Wainwright)
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: Europol director Rob Wainwright publicly rebutted: 'There is no basis in fact for this point and it is a very serious, even alarmist, conflation of migration and terrorism.' The 5,000 figure referred to European nationals who had travelled to Syria/Iraq — not migrants entering Europe. Wainwright said he had previously corrected Farage at a public event the month before. UKIP spokesman acknowledged 'misinterpretation'. The Independent 9 June 2016.

claims.claim: 508 million people — if they wish, can come to this country. We have no control. The only way we get control is to vote to leave the European Union.
claims.notes: BBC Question Time 9 June 2016. Full Fact: EU free movement applies only to EEA citizens (~440m outside UK), and only 3% of EU citizens lived in another member state in 2015. Framing implied virtually all 508m could and might come. Same broadcast: Farage claimed 'one new house every four minutes' for immigration — Full Fact rated plausible but uncertain.

claims.claim: BREAKING POINT: The EU has failed us all. [Poster depicting a long queue of non-white Syrian/Afghan refugees walking near the Croatia-Slovenia border, with no connection to UK immigration]
claims.verdict: Widely condemned as racist; reported to police; compared to 1930s propaganda
claims.verdict_source: Guardian; BBC; The Independent; Sky News; George Osborne; Nicola Sturgeon
claims.notes: Unveiled 16 June 2016, same day Labour MP Jo Cox was murdered by a far-right attacker. Photograph taken in October 2015 near Croatia-Slovenia border — no connection to UK immigration. Unison general secretary Dave Prentis reported to Metropolitan Police for inciting racial hatred. Chancellor Osborne called it 'vile' with 'echoes of [1930s] literature'. Scotland FM Nicola Sturgeon called it 'vile and racist'. 40,000-signature petition handed to police July 2016 (Sky News: https://news.sky.com/story/farage-reported-to-police-over-eu-campaign-10508329). Farage apologised only for 'the timing'. In 2019 BBC interview defended poster as having 'transformed politics'. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — reported to police, crime reference number assigned.

claims.claim: We will have done it without having to fight, without a single bullet being fired.
claims.notes: Brexit victory speech early hours of 24 June 2016, hours after Labour MP Jo Cox was shot and stabbed to death. Farage branded 'unbelievably insensitive and crass' (The Independent). He later apologised for the 'timing'. Not a factual claim subject to fact-check — logged as documented statement with major political impact.

claims.claim: [Led Britain First activists into Al-Manar Islamic Centre Cardiff; uploaded video to YouTube making allegations of extremism against mosque]
claims.verdict: Guilty of deliberate and cynical defiance of court order; sentenced to 8 weeks prison
claims.verdict_source: High Court; Tell MAMA; Reconciliation Talk (citing judge's remarks)
claims.platform_corrected: Yes — video uploaded to YouTube; subsequently removed in March 2018 platform purge
claims.notes: Golding drove 4 activists to mosque in breach of court injunction banning mosque entry without invitation. Judge said conduct was 'deliberate and cynical defiance' and 'an affront to the Muslim community'. Mosque trustee denied extremism allegations. LEGAL: Court finding. Source: http://www.reconciliationtalk.org/blog/2016/12/15/paul-golding-of-britain-first-jailed-for-deliberate-and-cynical-defiance-of-court-order-banning-him-from-mosques

claims.claim: Muslims are statistically on course to outnumber 'Europeans' this century [claim circulated via social media and interviews c.2017]
claims.verdict: Documented by BBC as a far-right myth Robinson perpetuated; demographic projections do not support this claim
claims.notes: BBC profile article (July 2019) explicitly states Robinson 'perpetuated and spread far-right myths, such as a claim that Muslims are statistically on course to outnumber Europeans this century.' Approximate date 2017 based on article context.

claims.claim: I'm sorry to say that we have now a fifth column living inside these European countries … frankly, if you open your door to uncontrolled immigration from Middle Eastern countries, you are inviting in terrorism.
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: Fox News / Hannity 22 March 2017, day of Westminster Bridge attack. Farage blamed Tony Blair's multiculturalism and said Blair had sent 'search parties' for immigrants. Parallel 'fifth column' comments on LBC. The Independent and BBC reported. Pattern of making immigration-linked claims immediately after attacks.

claims.claim: [Video shot at scene of Westminster terror attack — promoted anti-Muslim narrative following attack]
claims.verdict: Robinson filmed himself at the scene of the Westminster attack; video watched over 3 million times in 72 hours; Hope Not Hate characterises him as using terror attacks to build anti-Muslim audience
claims.notes: State of Hate 2018. Robinson added 40,042 Twitter followers in the week after the Manchester Arena attack (May 2017). After each 2017 terror attack he sharply increased his following, more than doubling his Twitter count from January to year-end.

claims.claim: [Distributed leaflets targeting Muslim men falsely believed to be defendants in Kent rape trial; confronted addresses; shouted 'dirty Muslim rapist, come out, we're not going to leave until you're gone']"
claims.verdict: Guilty — religiously aggravated harassment (1 count); sentenced to 18 weeks prison
claims.verdict_source: Folkestone Magistrates' Court March 2018; The Independent; Tell MAMA
claims.platform_corrected: Yes — Facebook removed all Britain First pages 14 March 2018 days after conviction
claims.notes: Trial at Folkestone Magistrates' Court 7 March 2018. Judge Justin Barron said actions 'demonstrated hostility towards Muslims and Islam' and used facts of case 'for their own political ends'. Fransen received 36 weeks. Court found actions could have caused rape defendants to walk free. LEGAL: Court finding. Source also: https://fra.europa.eu/en/databases/criminal-detention/node/6912

claims.claim: [Filmed inside Canterbury Crown Court during rape trial; published footage under title 'Tommy Robinson in Canterbury exposing Muslim child rapists, Police help them escape'; called defendants 'Muslim child rapists' while jury was deliberating]
claims.verdict: False — Judge Heather Norton found Robinson used 'pejorative language which prejudged the outcome of the case'; convicted of contempt of court; three-month suspended sentence
claims.notes: LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — prior contempt finding (concluded). Court transcript confirms exact language: 'Muslim child rapists' and 'Muslim paedophiles'. Suspended sentence activated in 2018. BBC: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-leeds-44287640

claims.claim: in a four week period his tweets reached 193 million people and his Facebook videos were viewed 49 million times [documented reach — not a single claim but Hope Not Hate's quantification of his broadcast reach in the period following the 2017 terror attacks]
claims.verdict: Hope Not Hate characterises Robinson as the UK's most prominent anti-Muslim activist; his content was watched 49 million times on Facebook over one four-week period
claims.notes: State of Hate 2018. Facebook videos 49m views in four weeks; tweets reached 193m people in same period. Robinson: 686,267 Facebook likes; 394,000 Twitter followers; 61,884 YouTube subscribers at time of report.

claims.claim: 22 dead - number rising. Schofield. Don't you even dare. Do not be part of the problem. We need a final solution. #Manchester
claims.verdict: Condemned as echoing Nazi terminology; led to immediate LBC sacking
claims.verdict_source: The Week; Washington Post; Change.org petition; Metropolitan Police received complaints
claims.platform_corrected: Yes — deleted and replaced with 'true solution'; LBC terminated Hopkins immediately
claims.notes: Tweet sent at 7:24am 23 May 2017, day after Manchester Arena bombing killing 22. Hopkins deleted and replaced with 'true solution'. LBC terminated her contract 'effective immediately'. Met Police confirmed receipt of complaints but did not confirm investigation outcome. Source also: https://www.washingtonpost.com/news/morning-mix/wp/2017/05/24/we-need-a-final-solution-british-columnist-tweets-then-deletes-after-manchester-bombing/

claims.claim: We need a final solution [tweet posted the morning after the Manchester Arena bombing calling for a 'final solution' — Nazi terminology]
claims.verdict: Metropolitan Police received complaints; Hope Not Hate and IRR characterise as use of Nazi genocidal terminology in anti-Muslim context; led to her dismissal from LBC
claims.verdict_source: Hope Not Hate / Institute of Race Relations
claims.notes: LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED. Hopkins deleted the original tweet and replaced it with 'true solution'. Metropolitan Police confirmed it received complaints (The Week, 26 May 2017: https://theweek.com/85034/katie-hopkins-to-leave-lbc-over-final-solution-tweet). State of Hate 2018 records this as a key incident in Hopkins's trajectory. IRR calendar entry: https://irr.org.uk/calendar_entry/katie-hopkins-reported-to-police-after-final-solution-manchester-attack-tweet/

claims.claim: The mosque where the attack happened tonight has a long history of creating terrorists & radical jihadists & promoting hate & segregation [tweeted after Finsbury Park mosque attack]
claims.verdict: False — Robinson misidentified the mosque attacked; the Muslim Welfare House (attacked) was not the Finsbury Park Mosque. Met Police confirmed misidentification.
claims.platform_corrected: No — condemned but not corrected on platform (pre-ban)
claims.notes: MEND confirms Robinson misidentified the mosque: https://www.mend.org.uk/tommy-robinson-permanently-removed-twitter/. Wikipedia cites Darren Osborne trial: Commander Dean Haydon stated Robinson's online material played 'significant role' in radicalising the attacker. Also tweeted: 'I'm not surprised at all that an attack like this has happened, I'm just surprised it's taken this long.'

claims.claim: I'm not surprised at all that an attack like this has happened, I'm just surprised it's taken this long. [tweeted same night as Finsbury Park mosque attack — one man killed, nine injured]
claims.notes: Characterised by reputable press as normalising anti-Muslim violence. Robinson's email list had sent Darren Osborne a message linking Muslims to crimes (Wikipedia, citing Lizzie Dearden, Independent, 23 Jan 2018).

claims.claim: [Appeared on Good Morning Britain holding the Quran and described it as a 'violent and cursed book']
claims.notes: Documented following Finsbury Park attack. Characterised by ISD, Hope Not Hate and Tell MAMA as anti-Muslim hate speech. No formal fact-checker verdict on theological claim.

claims.claim: [Used GB News programme to make alarmist claims about 'waves of Afghan refugees' and 'thousands of others making bogus claims to be Afghans, all descending on the UK']
claims.verdict: Hope Not Hate characterises as part of Farage mainstreaming anti-migrant narratives; the claims were unsubstantiated; reflects Farage's strategic move to use mainstream broadcast platform for far-right adjacent rhetoric
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: State of Hate 2022. Farage also criticised RNLI for helping vessels in distress in the Channel, prompting national news story and increased RNLI donations following HNH campaign. He sailed his own boat into the Channel to film migrant vessels. 49% of 2019 Brexit Party voters believed elites were involved in large-scale child trafficking (HNH/YouGov).

claims.claim: There are about six million Jewish people living in America … in terms of money and influence they are a very powerful lobby … the Jewish lobby, that has links with the Israeli government, is a strong voice in America.
claims.notes: LBC phone-in 30 October 2017. Farage agreed with caller 'Ahmed' about AIPAC/Israeli lobby influence on US elections and expanded into 'Jewish lobby' language citing 'money and influence'. Jewish Chronicle and Business Insider reported condemnation from Jewish groups. Farage dismissed criticism as 'pathetic'. Did not apologise.

claims.claim: Whites are being slaughtered in South Africa & inexplicably Appeaser May chooses to crucify herself [on visit to SA].
claims.verdict: Claim rated false — no evidence of white genocide in South Africa
claims.verdict_source: New Statesman fact-check; BBC News analysis
claims.notes: Part of sustained 'white genocide' South Africa campaign. BBC reported tweet also incorrectly stated SA government was 'seizing land'. Hopkins later detained by South African authorities at passport control in Feb 2018 for 'spreading racial hatred'. Source also: https://www.bbc.com/news/world-africa-45336840

claims.claim: God Bless you Trump! God Bless America! [Response to Trump retweeting Britain First anti-Muslim videos; simultaneously amplified videos including one falsely claiming 'Muslim migrant beats up Dutch boy on crutches']
claims.verdict: One video false — perpetrator was Dutch-born, not Muslim migrant; second video filmed in Syrian civil war 2013; third video context misrepresented
claims.verdict_source: AP Fact Check (VOA); Reuters; Dutch Embassy official statement
claims.platform_corrected: No — Twitter suspended Golding/Fransen accounts 18 Dec 2017
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: Trump retweeted 3 videos posted by Fransen to his 43.6M followers. Golding celebrated. Dutch Embassy formally told Trump perpetrator 'was born and raised in the Netherlands'. AP rated video 1 false. PM Theresa May condemned retweets; described Britain First as spreading 'hateful narratives which peddle lies'. Accounts suspended 18 Dec 2017. Source also: https://time.com/5040626/trump-britain-first-muslim-video-retweet/

claims.claim: [Three anti-Muslim videos retweeted by US President Trump from Jayda Fransen's account — one mislabelled as 'Muslim migrant beats up Dutch boy on crutches' when the attacker was not Muslim]
claims.verdict: Hope Not Hate documents this as the moment Britain First achieved global reach via Trump amplification; two of the three videos were later debunked as mislabelled
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support (68% of 2024 Reform voters; 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform — YouGov; BBC 2025 locals). Correlation, not proven single-claim causation.
claims.notes: State of Hate 2018: Jayda Fransen's page was the second fastest growing in the UK in December 2017 (after the Queen), gaining 37,582 likes in December alone. Britain First Facebook page had 1,948,000 likes — second most-liked UK politics/society page after Royal Family. Trump retweeted 3 videos. PBS confirms one video (Dutch boy/crutches) used footage from 2017 not involving a Muslim: https://www.pbs.org/newshour/politics/trump-retweets-anti-muslim-videos-from-british-far-right-leader

claims.claim: [Twitter account suspended alongside Fransen and Britain First in platform hate-speech purge following Trump retweet controversy]
claims.platform_corrected: Yes — suspended; later rejoined on alternative platform Gab
claims.notes: Twitter revised hate speech rules; suspended Golding, Fransen and Britain First accounts. Britain First directed followers to Gab. Facebook ban followed March 2018. Source: Business Insider

claims.claim: Claims while in South Africa promoting 'white genocide' conspiracy: 'The violent ethnic cleansing of white farmers by armed black gangs is infuriating & heartbreaking.'
claims.verdict: False — statistics do not support white genocide claim
claims.platform_corrected: No — Hopkins's passport confiscated by South African authorities for 'spreading racial hatred'
claims.notes: Hopkins detained at Johannesburg airport in Feb 2018; passport marked. SA authorities cited racial hatred. New Statesman analysis found farm murder rates declining and disproportionate share of murder victims are Black. Source: https://www.newstatesman.com/world/2018/05/no-katie-hopkins-there-no-white-genocide-south-africa

claims.claim: [Retweeted Paul Joseph Watson post aimed at George Soros — part of a pattern of amplifying antisemitic/far-right tropes about Soros]
claims.verdict: Hope Not Hate documents the retweet as Farage amplifying far-right anti-Soros messaging; he strongly denied the comments were antisemitic
claims.notes: State of Hate 2018. Farage retweeted Watson on 9 February. In November 2017, Farage questioned Soros and OSF alleged influence in European Parliament, stating it 'could be the biggest level of international political collusion in history'. Farage told Sky News if borders not controlled 'there may be one more great big european battle to be fought.'

claims.claim: [Britain First's Facebook page banned March 2018 after reaching 1.9 million likes — second most-liked page in UK politics/society after Royal Family; at ban it was the fastest-growing UK politics/society page]
claims.verdict: Hope Not Hate documents Britain First Facebook reach as evidence of far-right mainstreaming through social media; banned by Facebook for hate speech; Golding and Fransen also subsequently jailed for religiously aggravated harassment
claims.verdict_source: Hope Not Hate / New York Times
claims.notes: State of Hate 2018. Britain First Facebook: 1,948,000 likes (vs Labour 1,014,000; Conservatives 652,000). NYT confirms Facebook ban: https://www.nytimes.com/2018/03/14/world/facebook-ban-britain-first.html. Jayda Fransen page second fastest growing in UK (after Queen) in December 2017, gaining 37,582 likes in December alone. Britain First also banned from Twitter in February 2017.

claims.claim: 90% of grooming gang convictions are Muslims [tweeted, leading to first Twitter suspension]
claims.verdict: False — Home Office data does not support this statistic; white men are primary offenders across child sexual abuse as a whole (Jay Review 2022; CSO Hate 2025)
claims.platform_corrected: Yes — Twitter temporarily suspended account for this tweet, then permanently banned for follow-up tweet
claims.impact: Jan 2025 grooming-gangs episode: Musk net favourability fell to -35 but Reform polling held at record highs (More in Common; YouGov Jan 2025).
claims.notes: BBC confirmed this was the specific tweet triggering first suspension. Home Office and Jay Review data contradict the 90% figure. CSO Hate report (2025): https://www.csohate.org/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/Elon-Musk-X-and-Amplification-of-Islamophobia-in-the-UK-1.pdf

claims.claim: Islam promotes killing people [tweeted; Twitter permanently banned account citing hateful conduct]
claims.notes: Robinson stated publicly this was the post that led to permanent ban. MEND article 29 March 2018 confirms. Business Insider reports ban: https://www.businessinsider.com/tommy-robinson-banned-from-twitter-2018-3

claims.claim: They also want to abolish the nation state — they want to get rid of it. They want to replace it with the globalist project, and the European Union is the prototype for the new world order.
claims.notes: InfoWars interview with Alex Jones, April 2018 (date approximate per Hope Not Hate). Farage used 'new world order' and 'globalist' framing coded to antisemitic conspiracy theories. Hope Not Hate documented as part of Farage's conspiratorial rhetoric pattern.

claims.claim: [Livestreamed on Facebook outside Leeds Crown Court during grooming gang trial while jury deliberated; encouraged followers to 'harass a defendant by finding him, knocking on his door, following him'; footage viewed 250,000 times]
claims.verdict: False legal claim — two High Court judges found he had 'seriously impeded the course of justice' and encouraged vigilante action; convicted of contempt of court
claims.verdict_source: UK High Court (Old Bailey July 2019) / BBC
claims.platform_corrected: No — Facebook subsequently banned Robinson Feb 2019
claims.impact: Jan 2025 grooming-gangs episode: Musk net favourability fell to -35 but Reform polling held at record highs (More in Common; YouGov Jan 2025).
claims.notes: Court finding confirmed: Dame Victoria Sharp and Mr Justice Warby ruling July 2019. Original 13-month sentence (May 2018) quashed on procedural grounds; retried and re-sentenced 9 months July 2019. BBC: https://www.bbc.com/news/uk-england-leeds-44287640. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — prior contempt proceedings (concluded).

claims.claim: Tommy warned us about these rape gangs. You laughed and called him a racist. Tommy will die in prison at the hands of these gangs. You will laugh and say it served him right. Then you will remember YOU have a daughter #TommyRobinson
claims.notes: Verbatim confirmed from direct static HTML fetch. Posted on day Robinson was jailed for contempt of court. Hopkins permanently banned from Twitter June 2020.

claims.claim: My message to Mark Zuckerberg today: Stop telling us Facebook is a 'platform for all ideas'. The evidence shows your algorithms censor conservative opinions.
claims.notes: Verbatim confirmed from direct static HTML fetch.

claims.claim: The invasion of Ukraine is a consequence of EU and NATO expansion … It made no sense to poke the Russian bear with a stick. These are dark days for Europe.
claims.notes: Social media post on day of Russian invasion 24 February 2022. Criticised by cross-party politicians. In June 2024 BBC interview with Nick Robinson, Farage doubled down: 'We provoked this war.' Farage acknowledged it was Putin's responsibility but argued NATO/EU gave him 'justification'. Leaders of all main UK parties condemned the remarks.

claims.claim: [EU and NATO expansion amounted to 'poking the Russian bear with a stick' — war in Ukraine 'a consequence of EU and NATO expansion']
claims.verdict: Hope Not Hate documents as Farage reversing himself within 24 hours — he first attributed invasion to Putin's expansionism then reversed to blame EU/NATO; characterised as pro-Kremlin framing
claims.notes: State of Hate 2022. Farage had previously said Putin was the world leader he most admired as an operator. UKIP under his influence uploaded article in January saying 'Britain should stop meddling in Ukraine'. A day after attributing war to Putin's expansionism, he backtracked.

claims.claim: We're now spending over 10% of our national cake on the NHS every year… Even regardless of population, we've increased in percentage terms enormously under this government.
claims.verdict: Neither Farage nor Fiona Bruce was necessarily wrong — they were quoting different measures of healthcare spending. Mr Farage's 10–11% figure aligns with UK healthcare spending as a share of GDP (ONS: 11.3% in 2022).
claims.notes: Contextual clarification — verdict is neither clearly false nor clearly correct; both figures were technically valid measures

claims.claim: [The Conservatives' national service policy] would only apply to one in 36 young people.
claims.verdict: Based on current population estimates it's more like one in 26.
claims.notes: Checked as part of defence claims election round-up

claims.claim: Most people coming to the UK on visas are dependants
claims.verdict: Misleading — roughly a third of all visas issued in year ending March 2024 went to dependants; claim is correct only for work visas (~48%) not all visas

claims.post_url: (Reform UK campaign statement — BBC Verify article July 2024)
claims.verdict: No evidence — maritime law does not allow transfer to another state without that state's consent; no UK-France agreement exists; Tice claimed to have legal advice but refused to share it with BBC Verify

claims.post_url: (Paid Facebook advertisement by Reform UK — Full Fact: 'A Facebook ad from Reform UK claimed 14 million people will arrive in the UK in the next 12 years')

claims.claim: GDP wealth per capita has fallen for the last six consecutive quarters
claims.notes: Full Fact notes GDP per capita rose in Q1 2024; it had fallen for seven consecutive quarters prior. Claim made during ITV Seven Party Leader Debate and repeated on BBC Breakfast.

claims.claim: We have to provide a new home every two minutes in this country just to deal with net migration
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support: 68% of 2024 Reform voters and 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform (YouGov 2024; BBC 2025 locals). Evidence: strong correlation, not proven causation from any single claim.
claims.notes: BBC Verify: requires context and a reliable statistical source. Net migration figure does not straightforwardly translate to one home per two minutes given vacancy rates and household composition. | Also: https://fullfact.org/immigration/farage-house-every-two-minutes/

claims.claim: Half of those that've come aren't coming to work they're coming as dependants
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support: 68% of 2024 Reform voters and 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform (YouGov 2024; BBC 2025 locals). Evidence: strong correlation, not proven causation from any single claim.
claims.notes: BBC Verify: Partially correct for work visa holders (~48% dependants) but only ~33% of all visas were dependants in the year ending March 2024; dependants of skilled workers are allowed to work.

claims.claim: Any country that has a large postal ballot will always be open to fraud and intimidation and it happens in this country all the time
claims.notes: BBC Verify: Electoral Commission says no evidence of large-scale electoral fraud in last five years. Of 1462 alleged fraud cases 2019-2023 only 11 led to convictions.

claims.claim: All I do know is that man produces about 3% of the CO2 produced in the world every year and that it is nuts to call CO2 a poison
claims.notes: BBC Verify: Misleading. NASA scientists say human activity responsible for ~33% of total CO2 in atmosphere. Claim made on BBC Breakfast and Radio 5 Live.

claims.claim: You can now go shoplifting and provided you nick less than £200 worth of stuff you won't get prosecuted
claims.notes: BBC Verify: No such prosecutorial threshold exists; people have been prosecuted for stealing less than £200. | Also: https://fullfact.org/crime/shoplifting-200-prosecution-farage/

claims.claim: Labour launched their manifesto with their six key priorities, not even mentioning immigration.
claims.post_url: (Reform UK 2024 'contract' launch event — Full Fact: 'Speaking at the contract's launch, Reform UK leader and candidate for the seat of Clacton Nigel Farage said…')
claims.verdict: The word 'immigration' isn't in Labour's six 'first steps for change', but the third step pledges to 'Launch a Border Security Command'. Labour has also set out five national missions, none related to immigration.
claims.notes: Reform UK 2024 manifesto launch event

claims.claim: We have now seen six consecutive quarters of GDP per head falling.
claims.post_url: (Reform UK 2024 'contract' launch event, 13 June 2024; also repeated on BBC Breakfast 14 June 2024 and ITV Seven Party Leaders' Debate 13 June 2024)
claims.verdict: Office for National Statistics figures show that GDP per head rose in the first quarter of 2024, but fell for seven consecutive quarters before that.
claims.notes: Claim later repeated on ITV Seven Party Leaders' Debate and BBC Breakfast; fact-checked under both the manifesto round-up and economy round-up

claims.claim: [There is] record crime.
claims.verdict: Crimes recorded by the police have increased sharply in recent years, but the ONS says this isn't the best data to understand overall trends. The Crime Survey shows crime peaked in the mid-1990s and has been falling ever since.

claims.claim: Postal voting has allowed electoral fraud.
claims.verdict: There is some evidence for this in the past, but according to the Electoral Commission, 'in the past five years, there is no evidence of large-scale electoral fraud'.
claims.notes: Also: Full Fact

claims.claim: [Reform would require police officers to] complete a 2-year probationary period.
claims.verdict: Police officers already have a probation period of at least two years, so it's not clear what would change under the party's proposals.

claims.claim: [NHS has] record NHS waiting lists.
claims.verdict: This could do with some more context. Waiting lists peaked at 7.77m in Sept 2023, fallen slightly to 7.57m in April — but risen since Sunak pledged to cut them.

claims.claim: Labour launched their manifesto with their six key priorities not even mentioning immigration
claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support: 68% of 2024 Reform voters and 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform (YouGov 2024; BBC 2025 locals). Evidence: strong correlation, not proven causation from any single claim.

claims.claim: We have now seen six consecutive quarters of GDP per head falling
claims.notes: Full Fact: ONS figures show GDP per head rose in Q1 2024; it had fallen for seven consecutive quarters before that. Claim made at Reform UK 2024 contract launch.

claims.claim: Record crime
claims.notes: Full Fact: Police-recorded crime has risen but ONS says Crime Survey for England and Wales is better indicator and shows crime peaked in mid-1990s and has been falling. Published in Reform UK 2024 manifesto/contract.

claims.claim: Every two minutes, 2.6 people are coming into the country.
claims.verdict: Needs context — figure based on net migration of 685,000 in year to Dec 2023 but does not account for vacant homes or average household size
claims.notes: Unknown

claims.claim: A third of all visas go to dependants.
claims.verdict: Incorrect — approximately 33% of all visas issued went to dependants in year ending March 2024; claim conflated work visa dependant rate (~48%) with all visa categories

claims.notes: Unknown | Also: https://fullfact.org/environment/does-human-activity-only-cause-3-of-co2-emissions/

Musk amplification: on days he engaged Farage, Farage's daily audience tripled and Robinson's views doubled (AP, Aug 2025); Reform's 5 MPs drew ~50% of UK MP engagement on X (Byline Times, Feb 2025).
claims.notes: Amnesty International technical explainer, 6 August 2025. Robinson's posts: 580 million views in 2 weeks after Southport attack. 'Europe Invasion' post: 4 million views. Within 24 hours: 27 million estimated impressions for all posts speculating attacker was Muslim/migrant. Amnesty: X's 'heavy ranker' prioritises conversation regardless of harm. July 2025 UK parliamentary report established social media business models incentivised spread of misinformation after Southport murders.

claims.impact: Immigration salience is the strongest documented correlate of Reform support: 68% of 2024 Reform voters and 74% of 'immigration-top-issue' voters backed Reform (YouGov 2024; BBC 2025 locals). Evidence: strong correlation, not proven causation from any single claim.
claims.notes: Full Fact verdict: False. Dorset Police told Full Fact the figure is 'incorrect' and was not issued by them. Actual figure for relevant area is ~1%. Shared by former MEP/journalist Annunziata Rees-Mogg and columnist Allison Pearson also on GB News. Not directly by Farage but Reform-affiliated amplification.

claims.claim: Watched in Jersey City, N.J., where thousands and thousands of people were cheering as the World Trade Center collapsed
claims.notes: No evidence found in newspaper or TV archives of mass celebrations by Muslims in New Jersey on 9/11; claim defies basic logic; rated Pants on Fire

claims.claim: The Mexican government ... they send the bad ones over
claims.notes: Second time PolitiFact rated this claim Pants on Fire; immigration experts found no evidence Mexico has any policy of pushing migrants to the US

claims.claim: Don't believe those phony numbers when you hear 4.9 and 5 percent unemployment. The number's probably 28, 29, as high as 35. In fact, I even heard recently 42 percent.
claims.notes: Highest credible alternate unemployment figure was 15.6%; 42% not supported by any credible economic measure; rated Pants on Fire

claims.claim: Our African-American communities are absolutely in the worst shape they've ever been in before. Ever. Ever. Ever.
claims.notes: No comparison to slavery or Jim Crow; African Americans have made gains in life expectancy, education, and income over 50 years; poverty and unemployment near historic lows in 2016

claims.claim: That was the largest audience to witness an inauguration, period.
claims.platform: CIA headquarters speech (via press secretary Spicer)
claims.impact: Crowd size claim used to challenge media credibility early in presidency; contributed to "alternative facts" controversy
claims.notes: Claim made by Sean Spicer on Trump's behalf; Obama's 2009 inauguration drew an estimated 1.8 million vs Trump's 250,000-600,000; transit ridership data also contradicted the claim

claims.claim: Based on information from the Department of Justice, the overwhelming majority of individuals convicted of terrorism and terrorism-related offenses since 9/11 came here from outside of our country.
claims.notes: Data came from a Senate subcommittee not the DOJ; only 40 of 580 convictions were for attacks planned on US soil (7%); majority of jihadist terrorists in US were citizens or legal residents

claims.claim: The murder rate in our country is the highest it's been in 47 years.
claims.notes: Murder rate peaked in early 1990s; the uptick since 2014 represented the biggest single-year rise in 40+ years but not the highest overall rate in 47 years; national homicide rate far below its 1990s peak

claims.claim: Just found out that Obama had my 'wires tapped' in Trump Tower just before the victory.
claims.notes: FBI Director Comey testified no evidence supported the tweets; the NYT article Trump cited did not say Obama ordered wiretapping; congressional leadership of both parties found no evidence

claims.claim: This Russia thing with Trump and Russia is a made-up story. It's an excuse by the Democrats for having lost an election that they should've won.
claims.impact: PolitiFact 2017 Lie of the Year
claims.notes: FBI opened investigation on possible Trump-Russia ties in July 2016; US intelligence community concluded with confidence Russia interfered to help Trump; Democrats did not control the FBI or Senate Intelligence Committee

claims.claim: We enacted the biggest tax cuts and reforms in American history.
claims.notes: In inflation-adjusted dollars, the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act ranked fourth-largest since 1940; as a percentage of GDP it ranked seventh; Reagan's 1981 cut was 2.89% of GDP vs Trump's 0.9%

claims.claim: Looks like by April, you know, in theory, when it gets a little warmer, it miraculously goes away.
claims.impact: Downplaying of COVID-19 delayed public health preparations
claims.notes: COVID-19 did not disappear in April; pandemic continued for years; claim was part of a documented pattern of Trump minimising the threat of COVID-19

claims.claim: Anybody that wants a test can get a test. That's what the bottom line is.
claims.impact: False reassurance hampered public awareness of testing scarcity in early pandemic
claims.notes: Testing supply was limited; healthcare providers decided who got tested based on CDC criteria; multiple reports documented people unable to get tests

claims.claim: Is there a way we can do something like [disinfectant] by injection inside or almost a cleaning?
claims.platform: White House coronavirus task force briefing
claims.impact: Manufacturer Reckitt Benckiser issued emergency warning against ingesting its products; poison control centres reported spike in calls
claims.notes: AFP and multiple medical experts confirmed injecting disinfectant would be lethal; Reckitt Benckiser (Lysol/Dettol) and Clorox issued public warnings in direct response to Trump's comments

claims.claim: If you look at the one survey, the only bad survey, they were giving it to people that were in very bad shape. They were very old. Almost dead. It was a Trump enemy statement.
claims.notes: There were three studies — not one — all of which found hydroxychloroquine had no benefit against COVID-19; all studies controlled for patient health; survival rate was lower among those who took the drug

claims.claim: We have tested over 40 million people. By so doing, we show cases, 99% of which are totally harmless.
claims.platform: Fourth of July White House event
claims.notes: CDC data at the time showed cumulative case death rate of ~4.5%; hospitalisation rate was ~4%; White House provided no supporting data for the 99% figure

claims.claim: The 2020 election was rigged and stolen from the American people.
claims.platform: Various speeches rallies and social media (repeated claim)
claims.impact: Central claim driving January 6 2021 Capitol attack; used to push restrictive voting laws in multiple states; basis of 61+ lawsuits all of which failed
claims.notes: Biden won 306 electoral votes; states certified results; Congress accepted results; 60+ lawsuits rejected; CISA (Trump's own agency) called it 'most secure election in American history'; Bill Barr told Trump the claims were 'bull----'

claims.claim: Claimed 3 to 5 million immigrants voted illegally giving Hillary Clinton the popular vote margin
claims.impact: Basis for creating Presidential Advisory Commission on Election Integrity which was later disbanded
claims.notes: National Association of Secretaries of State said no evidence supported the claim; Brennan Center found only ~30 suspected noncitizen votes out of 23.5 million in jurisdictions studied; claim originated from discredited InfoWars story

claims.claim: In Springfield, Ohio, they're eating the dogs, the people that came in, they're eating the cats. They're eating, they're eating the pets of the people that live there.
claims.impact: PolitiFact 2024 Lie of the Year; sparked bomb threats against Springfield schools and city hall; inflamed anti-immigrant sentiment
claims.notes: Springfield city officials confirmed no credible reports of pets being harmed; Haitian migrants in Springfield are legally present under Temporary Protected Status and immigration parole; Reuters confirmed no evidence

claims.claim: Harris/Walz support executing newborn babies / post-birth abortion
claims.notes: Harris supports reinstating Roe v. Wade protections; neither Harris nor Walz advocate executing newborns; no Democratic politician has advocated for what Trump described

claims.claim: Vice President Kamala Harris is 'talking about bringing back the draft.'
claims.notes: Harris has never said anything about bringing back the military draft; PolitiFact found no evidence of any such position

claims.claim: The Harris-Biden Administration has been caught fraudulently manipulating Job Statistics.
claims.notes: Zero evidence of manipulation; BLS follows standard annual benchmarking process used for decades; economists across ideological spectrum confirmed no evidence of political meddling

claims.claim: $1 billion was stolen from FEMA to use it for illegal migrants. … And FEMA is now busted. They don't have any money.
claims.impact: Claim spread during Hurricane Helene disaster response; hindered public trust in FEMA relief efforts
claims.notes: FEMA migrant funding comes from CBP appropriations entirely separate from Disaster Relief Fund; Congress funds them separately; Trump's own administration in 2019 had shifted FEMA Disaster Relief Fund money to immigration enforcement

claims.claim: It has just come out that Democrats in Washington and the Democrat Governor's Office of North Carolina (Roy Cooper) were blocking people and money from coming into North Carolina.
claims.impact: Spread during Hurricane Helene response; contributed to distrust of official relief effort
claims.notes: Governor Cooper issued statement calling claim 'a flat out lie'; claim was based on a Blue Cross Blue Shield internal email urging employees not to drive to impassable roads — not a blockage of aid

claims.claim: I never mentioned wanting to end the Affordable Care Act.
claims.notes: Multiple documented instances of Trump saying he would repeal and replace the ACA including campaign pledges and presidential actions

claims.claim: Volodymyr Zelenskyy 'started' the war in Ukraine with Russia.
claims.impact: Zelensky publicly accused Trump of 'living in this disinformation space'; former VP Mike Pence publicly rebuked the claim
claims.notes: On Feb 24 2022 Russia invaded Ukraine; estimated 190,000 Russian troops massed on border before invasion; Putin premised attack on false claims; Ukraine was at peace the night before the invasion

claims.claim: The 2020 election was 'rigged' and Chris Krebs 'baselessly' dismissed 'election malfeasance.'
claims.impact: Used to justify stripping security clearance from former CISA director Krebs; part of ongoing campaign to undermine 2020 results
claims.notes: Biden won 306 electoral votes; 60+ lawsuits rejected; CISA statement said 'most secure election in American history'; Republicans including Barr and Kemp confirmed election was legitimate

claims.claim: Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia had 'MS-13' on his knuckles tattooed. … He had 'MS' as clear as you can be. Not 'interpreted.'
claims.impact: Used to justify wrongful deportation of Abrego Garcia to El Salvador's CECOT prison despite court order
claims.notes: MS-13 gang experts confirmed none of the tattoos in the image are known MS-13 signifiers; the image Trump shared on Truth Social was an altered photo; Salvadoran government photo showed a different hand; ICE acknowledged deportation was an 'administrative error'

claims.claim: Bureau of Labor Statistics employment numbers 'were rigged' when the agency revised them 'down by almost 900,000 jobs' after the 2024 election.
claims.notes: BLS revision was a standard annual benchmarking process announced months before the election; economists across political spectrum said data collection is free of political influence

claims.claim: Biden is letting millions of people from jails, from prisons, from insane asylums, from mental institutions, drug dealers pour in.
claims.notes: CBP data showed ~103,700 noncitizens with criminal convictions arrested from FY2021-FY2024 — not 'millions'; immigration expert called claim 'laughable'; Trump campaign provided no supporting evidence

claims.claim: The United States is the only country with unrestricted birthright citizenship.
claims.impact: Used as justification for executive order attempting to end birthright citizenship
claims.notes: Around three dozen countries offer automatic birthright citizenship; includes Canada Mexico and nearly all Central and South American countries; US not unique

claims.claim: We are now the only country in the world that uses mail-in voting.
claims.notes: International Institute for Democracy and Electoral Assistance found 34 countries or territories allow postal voting; Australia has had mail-in voting for a century; Canada allows it for all citizens

claims.claim: Crime in the US is 'through the roof' while global crime rates are decreasing everywhere else.
claims.notes: FBI data released March 2024 showed US homicides dropped more than 13% in 2023 — the second consecutive year of decline; contradicted Trump's claim directly

claims.claim: Based on information from the Department of Justice, the overwhelming majority of individuals found guilty of terrorism-related crimes since the events of September 11 2001 have entered our nation from abroad.
claims.notes: DOJ later confirmed it found 'no responsive records' to support Trump's assertion; New America Foundation found the large majority of jihadist terrorists in the US were citizens or legal residents

claims.claim: Is there a way we can do something like that by injection inside or almost a cleaning? Because you see it gets in the lungs and it does a tremendous number on the lungs.
claims.platform: White House coronavirus task force briefing
claims.impact: Reckitt Benckiser and Clorox issued emergency public statements; poison control calls spiked
claims.notes: AP found no medical basis for injecting disinfectants; Reckitt Benckiser stated 'under no circumstance should our disinfectant products be administered into the human body'; US surgeon general issued public warning

claims.claim: The 2020 election was stolen and rigged through widespread fraud (claim repeated hundreds of times)
claims.impact: Central claim underpinning January 6 attack on US Capitol; over 60 lawsuits failed; led to congressional investigations; drove restrictive voting laws in multiple states
claims.notes: US intelligence and law enforcement agencies found no significant fraud; AG Barr said 'there was nothing'; CISA called the 2020 election 'most secure in American history'; 50+ court cases dismissed including by Trump-appointed judges

claims.claim: Millions of migrants were entering the US from foreign prisons and mental health facilities
claims.notes: No incidents of migrants violently overtaking American communities; claim repeated hundreds of times at rallies; no evidence presented by Trump campaign

claims.claim: I won the popular vote if you deduct the millions of people who voted illegally.
claims.impact: Led to creation and later disbanding of presidential commission on election integrity
claims.notes: Brennan Center review of 42 jurisdictions with large noncitizen populations found roughly 30 suspected noncitizen votes out of 23.5 million cast; claim originated from InfoWars story; national review found only four documented voter fraud cases in 2016

claims.claim: Climate change is a hoax (repeated multiple times in speeches and tweets)
claims.platform: Rally speech (Hilton Head SC) and tweets
claims.notes: Trump tweeted in 2012 that 'global warming was created by and for the Chinese' and repeatedly called it a hoax; 97% of climate scientists agree on anthropogenic climate change; PolitiFact noted it rated the 'climate change is a hoax' claim Pants on Fire in 2014

claims.claim: The concept of global warming was created by and for the Chinese in order to make U.S. manufacturing non-competitive.
claims.notes: Trump denied making the claim at September 2016 presidential debate; PolitiFact confirmed he had made it; scientific consensus on climate change predates Trump's career and has no connection to China's manufacturing interests

claims.claim: California had a million people 'who should not have been voting' — citing a voter fraud settlement
claims.notes: The legal settlement Trump cited required LA County to remove inactive registrations — it contained no admission or evidence of 'illegal votes'; California officials called claim 'completely inaccurate'

claims.claim: On Jan. 6 2021, 274 FBI agents were 'probably acting as agitators and insurrectionists, but certainly not as law enforcement officials.'
claims.notes: No credible evidence 274 FBI agents participated as agitators at the Capitol; House Jan 6 committee found extensive evidence that the attack was carried out by Trump supporters; over 1,200 people charged for Jan 6 offences

claims.claim: Voting in California is 'rigged.'
claims.notes: No evidence presented; California election officials disputed the claim; independent audits have found no evidence of systematic fraud in California elections

claims.claim: Among Somalis in Minnesota '92% of them don't work.'
claims.notes: Statistic not supported by Census Bureau data; Somali-Americans in Minnesota have significant labour force participation; claim uses debunked or non-existent source

claims.claim: In 2016, after Donald Trump won the presidency, then-President Barack Obama was 'trying to lead a coup' with Trump's opponent Hillary Clinton.
claims.notes: No evidence that Obama or Clinton attempted a coup; standard presidential transition took place; claim inverts the documented history of the 2020-2021 period in which Trump's allies attempted to block certification of Biden's victory

claims.claim: China doesn't 'have a lot of wind farms. … Very, very few.'
claims.notes: China is the world's largest producer of wind energy; in 2024 China surpassed all countries in wind and solar power production; claim is the opposite of reality

claims.claim: Ukraine started the war with Russia / Zelensky 'started it'
claims.impact: Zelensky publicly stated Trump was 'living in this disinformation space' and that the claim came from Russia
claims.notes: Russia invaded Ukraine on Feb 24 2022; the night before Ukraine was at peace; Putin premised invasion on false pretexts; US military analysts called it the largest military operation in Europe since WWII

claims.claim: Claimed he had never told Bob Woodward that COVID-19 was deadly and that he was playing it down for the public
claims.notes: Woodward's book 'Rage' included Trump's own admission on tape: 'I wanted to always play it down. I still like playing it down.' Trump told Woodward in February 2020 that the virus was 'deadly stuff' — far more so than flu

claims.claim: Immigrants in the US illegally have caused an overall increase in violent crime
claims.notes: Multiple peer-reviewed studies including Cato Institute analysis of Texas data found immigrants commit crimes at lower rates than native-born citizens; 2018 study in Annual Review of Criminology found no significant correlation between immigration and crime

claims.claim: Vice President Kamala Harris 'is a communist. … She is really a Marxist.'
claims.notes: Harris is a centrist Democratic politician with no affiliation with communist or Marxist organisations or ideology; she supports capitalist market economy; claim has no factual basis

claims.claim: The Jeffrey Epstein files 'were made up by Comey. They were made up by Obama. They were made up by Biden.'
claims.notes: The Epstein files are genuine law enforcement and court records; Epstein was arrested by Trump's own Justice Department in 2019; the files predate both Obama and Biden administrations

claims.claim: Every boat that we knock out we save 25,000 American lives.
claims.notes: No credible public evidence that any single boat interception saves 25,000 lives; figure has no basis in published law enforcement or public health data

claims.claim: We've never had a president that solved one war, not one war.
claims.notes: Multiple presidents ended major wars: Eisenhower ended Korean War; Nixon ended Vietnam War; George H.W. Bush ended Gulf War; George W. Bush declared end of major combat in Iraq; Obama ended combat mission in Iraq

claims.claim: DOGE halts yearly payment of $2.5 million to Barack Obama for 'royalties linked to Obamacare.'
claims.notes: No such payment to Obama exists; former presidents do not receive royalties from federal laws; DOGE did not announce any such payment stoppage; claim is fabricated

claims.claim: You never heard Biden say the US has 'the strongest military anywhere in the world.'
claims.notes: Biden made statements praising US military strength on multiple documented occasions; claim is demonstrably false

claims.claim: If Harris is president I believe Israel will exist within two years from now
claims.notes: Harris denied animosity toward Israel and has long-standing documented support for the nation; no credible analyst supports Trump's claim about Israel ceasing to exist

claims.claim: The US has it [COVID-19] under control. It's going to be just fine.
claims.impact: Downplaying the pandemic contributed to delayed public health response
claims.notes: By July 2020 the US had more than 3 million confirmed cases and over 130,000 deaths; Trump was simultaneously telling Woodward privately the virus was 'deadly stuff'

claims.claim: 85% of the people that wear masks catch COVID-19
claims.notes: The CDC study Trump cited was designed to evaluate behaviours of already COVID-positive patients — not to measure mask effectiveness; CDC publicly stated the interpretation that more mask-wearers are getting infected is incorrect

claims.claim: Claimed foreigners are responsible for 'the vast majority' of terrorism convictions since 9/11 (executive order context)
claims.impact: Used as justification for travel ban executive orders
claims.notes: New America Foundation found most jihadist terrorists in US were citizens or legal residents; of 580 terrorism convictions only 40 were for attacks planned on US soil; DOJ later found 'no responsive records' to support claim

claims.claim: Claimed 2024 election results (before they occurred) proved 2020 was stolen
claims.notes: Trump claimed preliminary 2024 polling data proved 2020 results were wrong; PolitiFact found the logic was flawed; voters' choices can change between elections without indicating prior fraud

claims.claim: Greater testing is why coronavirus case counts are rising
claims.notes: The proportion of positive tests was also rising in many states — meaning more cases reflected actual spread not just more testing; hospitalisation numbers also rising independently of testing rates

claims.claim: According to data provided by the Department of Justice the vast majority of individuals convicted of terrorism and terrorism-related offenses since 9/11 came here from outside of our country.
claims.platform: Address to Joint Session of Congress
claims.impact: Used to justify Muslim-majority country travel ban executive orders
claims.notes: Data came from congressional subcommittee not DOJ; convictions included offenses committed outside the US and nonviolent crimes like fraud; data did not account for domestic terrorism; most convicted of planning attacks on US soil were US citizens

claims.claim: The Mexican government ... they send the bad ones over because they don't want to pay for them, they don't want to take care of them.
claims.notes: Trump claimed that the Mexican government has a policy of forcing criminals into the U.S. Migration experts found no evidence of such a policy, stating that movement is driven by labor demand and family networks.

claims.claim: Says crime statistics show blacks kill 81 percent of white homicide victims.
claims.notes: Trump tweeted an image from a non-existent 'Crime Statistics Bureau' claiming blacks are responsible for 81% of white homicides. Official FBI data from the preceding year showed that whites killed other whites at a rate of 82%, while blacks killed whites at a rate of only 15%.

claims.claim: The 2016 federal omnibus spending bill "funds illegal immigrants coming in and through your border, right through Phoenix."
claims.notes: The bill actually funds the U.S. Customs and Border Protection agency tasked with preventing illegal border crossings. There is no budget item for illegal immigration in the legislation.

claims.claim: Says the U.S. election system is "rigged."
claims.notes: Trump claimed the 2016 election would be rigged through massive voter fraud and cited 2012 results as evidence. PolitiFact found no evidence of widespread fraud and noted that precincts with zero votes for Romney were consistent with local demographic patterns.

claims.claim: The Obama-Clinton war on coal has cost Michigan over 50,000 jobs.
claims.notes: The 50,000 figure refers to potential jobs that might have been created by proposed projects, not actual jobs lost. Michigan does not mine coal and its entire mining sector only employs about 5,000 to 7,000 people.

claims.claim: Inner-city crime is reaching record levels. African-Americans will vote for Trump because they know I will stop the slaughter going on!
claims.notes: While some cities saw a homicide uptick in 2015, FBI data showed that violent crime in large cities had been on a steady decline since the 1990s and remained far below record levels.

claims.claim: The number of illegal immigrants "could be 3 million. It could be 30 million."
claims.notes: Trump suggested the number of undocumented immigrants could be as high as 30 million, but multiple government and independent researchers estimate the figure is consistently around 11 million. Demographers stated there is zero possibility for the number to be that high based on housing, school, and birth records.

claims.claim: Hillary Clinton and her campaign of 2008 started the birther controversy. I finished it. I finished it. You know what I mean. President Barack Obama was born in the United States. Period.
claims.notes: Trump reversed his long-held position questioning President Obama's citizenship while falsely claiming Hillary Clinton started the birther movement. PolitiFact rated this complete change of position as a Full Flop.

claims.claim: President Barack Obama was born in the United States, period.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated this a Full Flop; Trump spent years promoting the "birther" conspiracy that Obama was not born in the U.S., only to suddenly abandon the false claim in September 2016 with a brief statement while falsely blaming Hillary Clinton for starting it.

claims.claim: I did not — I did not — I do not say that. I do not say that [climate change is a hoax perpetrated by the Chinese].
claims.notes: During a 2016 presidential debate, Trump denied ever saying climate change was a Chinese hoax, but PolitiFact pointed to his own 2012 tweet stating the concept was created by and for the Chinese.

claims.claim: Of course there is large scale voter fraud happening on and before election day. Why do Republican leaders deny what is going on? So naive!
claims.notes: Trump claimed large-scale voter fraud was occurring before the 2016 election without evidence. Fact-checkers found that voter fraud is extremely rare and studies cited by his campaign referred to record-keeping issues or isolated incidents rather than widespread illegal activity.

claims.claim: Serious voter fraud in Virginia, New Hampshire and California - so why isn’t the media reporting on this? Serious bias
claims.notes: Trump alleged massive illegal voting in states he lost without providing evidence. Virginia election officials and Trump's own state campaign chairman confirmed there was no evidence of significant voter fraud.

claims.claim: Terrible! Just found out that Obama had my 'wires tapped' in Trump Tower just before the victory. Nothing found. This is McCarthyism!
claims.notes: Trump alleged that President Obama ordered surveillance on Trump Tower during the 2016 campaign. The DOJ, FBI, and Senate Intelligence Committee all stated there was no evidence or record of such wiretapping.

claims.claim: Germany owes vast sums of money to NATO & the United States must be paid more for the powerful, and very expensive, defense it provides to Germany!
claims.notes: NATO members do not pay the alliance or the US directly; instead, they commit to spending a percentage of their own GDP on their own defense. Germany's failure to meet the 2% target by 2017 does not constitute a debt owed to other nations.

claims.claim: Pre-existing conditions are in the bill.
claims.notes: While the bill's amendment nominally included coverage for pre-existing conditions, it allowed states to waive rules that prevent insurers from charging higher premiums based on health status. This could lead to prohibitively expensive coverage for people with pre-existing conditions, effectively making the guarantee meaningless.

claims.claim: We have deported about half of MS-13 since I took office.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found no basis for the 50% claim; experts said deporting half of an estimated 10,000-50,000 MS-13 members in a few months was implausible, and ICE data did not support such a figure.

claims.claim: We're pretty much at the 50 percent mark (on deportations of MS-13 gang members).
claims.notes: Trump claimed his administration had deported half of the estimated 10,000 MS-13 gang members in the U.S. ICE data showed only 2,798 total gang members deported in fiscal year 2017, a figure that includes all gangs and some months under the Obama administration.

claims.claim: The border is down 78 percent. Under past administrations, the border didn’t go down, it went up. But if it went down 1 percent, it was like this was a great thing.
claims.notes: Trump falsely claimed that border apprehensions only decreased under his administration. Official data shows significant declines under both Bush and Obama, and the 78 percent figure relies on cherry-picking the highest and lowest points of a single year.

claims.claim: I would not sign Graham-Cassidy if it did not include coverage of pre-existing conditions. It does!
claims.notes: Trump claimed the Graham-Cassidy health care bill protected coverage for pre-existing conditions. However, the bill allowed states to waive regulations, which could lead to significantly higher premiums for people with prior health issues.

claims.claim: There is "substantial evidence of voter fraud."
claims.notes: Trump claimed there is substantial evidence of voter fraud when disbanding his election integrity commission. PolitiFact found no evidence to support this and noted that studies previously cited by the administration were misinterpreted or methodologically flawed.

claims.claim: Many of these [MS-13] gang members took advantage of glaring loopholes and our laws to enter the country as illegal, unaccompanied, alien minors.
claims.notes: Trump claimed MS-13 members enter the U.S. by exploiting loopholes for unaccompanied minors. Fact-checkers found only a tiny fraction of such minors have gang affiliations and many are recruited after arrival.

claims.claim: I never said Russia did not meddle in the election.
claims.notes: Trump claimed he never denied Russian interference, but he had repeatedly dismissed reports of meddling as a 'hoax' and a 'made-up story' in various interviews and tweets.

claims.claim: I have been much tougher on Russia than Obama, just look at the facts. Total Fake News!
claims.notes: Experts found that while some hardline policies continued, Trump's personal rhetoric often undercut these actions, and he signed sanctions legislation reluctantly after veto-proof congressional support.

claims.claim: Last year we lost $500 billion on trade with China.
claims.notes: The U.S. trade deficit with China in 2017 was actually $375 billion in goods and only $336 billion when accounting for a surplus in services. Trump's figure was inflated by approximately $164 billion.

claims.claim: We've started building our wall. I'm so proud of it.
claims.notes: Trump claimed that construction on the border wall had begun, but the funding approved by Congress was specifically for the repair and replacement of existing fencing rather than the construction of new wall segments.

claims.claim: I signed the first military pay raise in 10 years.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim false; military personnel received pay raises every year during the preceding decade; Trump signed the FY2018 NDAA which included a 2.4% pay raise, not the first raise in a decade.

claims.claim: And, by the way — I know you don’t care about this — but that also includes raises for our military. First time in 10 years.
claims.notes: Trump falsely stated that the military pay raise he signed was the first in a decade. Historical records show that service members have received annual pay increases every year since 1983.

claims.claim: We are getting [MS-13 gang members] out of our country by the thousands.
claims.notes: While ICE deported over 5,000 gang members in fiscal year 2017, the agency does not track removals by specific gang affiliation, making it impossible to verify the number of MS-13 members deported. Only about 800 MS-13 members were arrested by ICE's investigations division during that same period.

claims.claim: I have watched ICE liberate towns from the grasp of MS-13 & clean out the toughest of situations.
claims.notes: Donald Trump claimed ICE has liberated entire U.S. towns from the control of MS-13, but gang experts and government data indicate no American towns have ever been under the gang's sovereignty or control.

claims.claim: ICE has liberated towns from the grasp of MS-13.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found no evidence that ICE had "liberated" entire towns from MS-13 control; while ICE conducted arrests of MS-13 members, independent experts said no U.S. town was under MS-13's grip in the way Trump implied.

claims.claim: Many countries are not paying what they should. And, frankly, many countries owe us a tremendous amount of money for many years back, where they’re delinquent, as far as I’m concerned, because the United States has had to pay for them.
claims.notes: NATO member countries do not pay money to the U.S. or a central fund; the 2% spending target refers to each nation's own domestic defense budget. There is no system of delinquency or debt for failing to meet these guidelines.

claims.claim: The United States was paying for anywhere from 70 to 90 percent of (NATO), depending on the way you calculate.
claims.notes: Trump's figures refer to the total global defense budget of the United States compared to other NATO members, rather than direct contributions to NATO. While the U.S. share of total member defense spending is about 72%, that money supports global missions beyond European defense.

claims.claim: NAFTA was a disaster. We lost thousands of plants. We lost millions of jobs.
claims.notes: While NAFTA impacted certain industries, the highest estimate of jobs displaced was around 851,700, not millions. Nonpartisan studies concluded the net effect on the U.S. economy was relatively modest compared to other factors like automation and trade with China.

claims.claim: Some of the Democrats have been talking about ending pre-existing conditions.
claims.notes: Trump claimed Democrats want to end pre-existing condition protections, but the party actually created these protections through the Affordable Care Act. Fact-checkers found zero evidence of any Democrats proposing to end such coverage.

claims.claim: Democrats let [Luis Bracamontes] into our country. ... Democrats let him stay.
claims.notes: Luis Bracamontes was deported under both Clinton and Bush; his most recent illegal entry occurred during the Bush administration. He evaded detection across multiple administrations and was not specifically released or allowed to stay by Democratic policy.

claims.claim: He challenged us. So we had, let's say 35,000 people tonight and he has 200 people, 300 people. Not too good.
claims.notes: Trump claimed Beto O'Rourke's rally drew only 200 to 300 people. Visual evidence and law enforcement estimates indicated the crowd was actually several thousand, likely around 7,000.

claims.claim: The noise (from windmills) causes cancer.
claims.notes: Trump claimed that the noise generated by wind turbines causes cancer. PolitiFact found no scientific evidence or medical studies supporting this link, and experts called the claim ridiculous.

claims.claim: This was a coup. This was an attempted overthrow of the United States government. ... This was an attempted coup.
claims.notes: Trump characterized the legal investigation into Russian interference in the 2016 election as a 'coup.' Experts clarified that a coup is an illegal seizure of power, whereas the investigation was a standard law enforcement procedure based on specific evidence.

claims.claim: We have been losing to China for many years, $500 billion a year.
claims.notes: The actual trade deficit with China was $419.2 billion in 2018, which is nearly $81 billion less than Trump claimed. He likely confused the China-specific deficit with the overall U.S. trade deficit with all countries.

claims.claim: Drug prices are coming down, first time in 51 years because of my administration, but we can get them down way lower working with the Democrats
claims.notes: While some specific data showed a temporary dip in list prices, overall drug spending continues to rise and more recent monthly indices showed prices increasing. Many of the administration's proposed reforms were not yet implemented at the time of the claim.

claims.claim: California admitted there were a million illegal votes in the 2016 presidential election.
