claims.notes: Trump mischaracterized a legal settlement regarding voter list maintenance as an admission of illegal voting. The settlement focused on removing inactive registrations and explicitly stated there was no admission of wrongdoing or fraud.

claims.claim: I was the person who saved Pre-Existing Conditions in your healthcare,
claims.notes: Trump claimed to have saved pre-existing condition protections despite repeatedly attempting to repeal the Affordable Care Act which established them. His administration also supported a lawsuit aimed at overturning the entire law and its consumer protections.

claims.claim: I was pleased to announce last year that, for the first time in 51 years, the cost of prescription drugs actually went down.
claims.notes: While drug price growth has slowed or stabilized in some sectors, data shows that the majority of prescription drugs continue to see price increases. The CPI data cited by Trump ignores high-priced specialty drugs and reflects list prices rather than actual consumer costs.

claims.claim: I always felt this [COVID-19] was a pandemic, long before it was called a pandemic.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim Mostly False; Trump had previously compared COVID to the flu, said it would disappear like a miracle, and expressed optimism that it was under control, contradicting his claim of always treating it as a pandemic.

claims.claim: We've done more tests in eight days than South Korea has done in eight weeks.
claims.notes: Trump's claim was inaccurate based on available testing data from both countries, and it ignored that South Korea's per capita testing rate was significantly higher than that of the U.S.

claims.claim: In eight days, we have done more [coronavirus] testing than South Korea has done in eight weeks.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the comparison Mostly False; South Korea's testing program was far more systematic and targeted than the U.S. effort; per-capita, South Korea's testing was still superior and more effective at containing the virus.

claims.claim: Those models that you're mentioning are talking about without mitigation.
claims.notes: Trump claimed that academic projections of COVID-19 deaths did not factor in mitigation efforts. The University of Washington model specifically accounts for mitigation like social distancing in its forecasts.

claims.claim: Mail-In Voting will lead to a RIGGED ELECTION. There will be massive voter fraud.
claims.notes: Reuters reported this was the first tweet Twitter ever fact-checked, adding a label "Trump makes unsubstantiated claim that mail-in ballots will lead to voter fraud." The same post on Facebook had 170,000 reactions and was shared thousands of times before being left up.

claims.claim: He spied on my campaign, and got caught!
claims.notes: Multiple investigations, including a DOJ review and a bipartisan Senate report, found no evidence that President Obama or the White House influenced the FBI's investigation into Russian interference. The investigations concluded the FBI had sufficient cause to open the inquiry without improper political motivation.

claims.claim: This is going to be a fraud like you’ve never seen
claims.notes: Trump claimed widespread fraud in mail-in voting during the first 2020 debate. AP found mail voting has proven secure in many states and that the specific irregularities Trump cited were mischaracterized or accidental.

claims.claim: I had to close the greatest economy in the history of our country.
claims.notes: While unemployment reached historical lows, GDP growth was average compared to prior presidents and other metrics like wages were modest. Economists noted that the economy benefited from trends starting under the previous administration and saw a widening deficit.

claims.claim: Many people every year, sometimes over 100,000, and despite the Vaccine, die from the Flu... Are we going to close down our Country? No, we have learned to live with it, just like we are learning to live with Covid, in most populations far less lethal!!!
claims.notes: Trump exaggerated annual flu deaths which average 35,900 and incorrectly claimed COVID-19 is less lethal despite it being roughly 10 times deadlier.

claims.claim: The World Health Organization just admitted that I was right. Lockdowns are killing countries all over the world. The cure cannot be worse than the problem itself. Open up your states, Democrat governors.
claims.notes: Trump claimed the WHO changed its stance to agree with him on lockdowns, but the WHO official's comments were taken out of context; the organization's position remained that lockdowns should only be a secondary measure.

claims.claim: We saved 2 million people
claims.notes: The 2.2 million figure comes from a model of a worst-case scenario where no one changed their behavior and no government action was taken. Experts noted that it is unrealistic to claim all those lives were saved by specific administration actions as the pandemic was still ongoing and some level of individual precaution was expected.

claims.claim: If we hadn't done anything at all with COVID, we would have lost 2 million people or more.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim false; the 2 million figure was an early model projection with no interventions; experts noted that any administration would have taken some mitigating actions, and Trump personally downplayed COVID repeatedly.

claims.claim: We are rounding the turn (on coronavirus). We are rounding the corner.
claims.notes: Trump claimed the U.S. was rounding the corner on COVID-19, but data showed cases, hospitalizations, and positivity rates were actually increasing at the time.

claims.claim: We are rounding the turn on the coronavirus. We are rounding the corner.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated this False; at the time Trump made this claim repeatedly in late October 2020, new COVID-19 cases were rising sharply to record levels, with a third wave underway; daily cases would reach all-time highs within days.

claims.claim: If somebody's terminally ill with cancer (in the U.S.), and they have COVID, we report them (as a COVID death). And you know, doctors get more money and hospitals get more money. Think of this incentive.
claims.notes: Trump suggested that hospitals inflate COVID-19 death counts for financial incentives. PolitiFact found that death certification follows international WHO guidelines and there is no evidence that Medicare payment increases have led to fraud.

claims.claim: doctors get more money if someone dies from COVID. You know right?, our doctors are very smart people. What they do is they say 'I'm sorry, but you know everybody dies of COVID.'
claims.notes: Trump claimed doctors misrepresent causes of death for financial gain. AP found no evidence of this fraud and noted that excess mortality data suggests the death toll is likely an undercount.

claims.claim: Our doctors get more money if somebody dies from COVID.
claims.notes: Trump alleged that doctors inflate COVID-19 death numbers for financial gain. PolitiFact found that doctors do not receive higher payments for these diagnoses and face legal penalties for falsifying death records.

claims.claim: If you count the legal votes, I easily win. If you count the illegal votes, they can try to steal the election from us.
claims.notes: Trump alleged the election was being rigged through 'illegal' mail-in votes. AFP found no evidence for these claims, noting that both in-person and mail-in ballots are legal and that officials confirmed no widespread fraud occurred.

claims.claim: We will win this and I will not allow the Democrats to silence your voices. We were getting ready to win this election. Frankly we did win this election.
claims.notes: AFP fact-checked Trump's Nov. 5–6 2020 public claims; vote counting was ongoing and Trump had not won; Biden ultimately received 306 electoral votes vs. Trump's 232, and courts dismissed over 60 subsequent fraud challenges.

claims.claim: Figures regarding improper vote counting in a Georgia county were 'true and correct' to the best of his knowledge.
claims.notes: A federal judge found that Trump signed a sworn verification for a court filing containing specific fraud numbers his own lawyers had previously informed him were inaccurate. The judge concluded that Trump and his legal team intended to advance false claims to delay the January 6 vote.

claims.claim: No president has ever had a better first three years in terms of the economy.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated False the claim Trump led the greatest economy in history; his first three years had solid but not record-breaking GDP growth, and many economists placed his economic record below several post-WWII presidents.

claims.claim: On January 6th, 2021 the crowd was peaceful and the attack on the Capitol was carried out by Antifa and the radical left, not Trump supporters.
claims.notes: Reuters found the Antifa claim false; law enforcement officials, the bipartisan House Select Committee, and criminal convictions confirmed that Trump supporters carried out the Jan. 6 Capitol attack; no credible evidence of Antifa involvement was found.

claims.claim: In 2020 the ballots that were counted "were fake ballots."
claims.notes: Trump claimed during a Fox News interview that ballots in the 2020 election were fake. PolitiFact found no evidence of widespread fake ballots, noting that security protocols and audits ensure ballot authenticity.

claims.claim: The 2020 election was stolen and the legal case against him for attempting to overturn results in Georgia is a "travesty of justice."
claims.notes: Trump repeatedly claimed the 2020 election was fraudulent despite 60 court losses and multiple audits confirming Biden's win. Experts and officials found no evidence of widespread fraud that could have changed the outcome.

claims.claim: When I took office, 'the auto industry was on its knees gasping its last breath.' Trump administration 'tariffs and taxes saved the American auto industry from extinction.'
claims.notes: The auto industry was actually rescued by the Bush and Obama administrations after the 2008 recession. Trump's tariffs on steel and aluminum increased manufacturing costs and his policies did not result in net industry growth.

claims.claim: In actuality, there is no evidence Joe Biden won the 2020 election.
claims.notes: Trump shared an anonymous report claiming a lack of evidence for Biden's victory. Numerous audits, court cases, and election officials have confirmed the results are accurate and verified.

claims.claim: Food costs 40%, 50%, 60% more than it did just a few years ago.
claims.notes: While food prices have risen by about 20% during Biden's first three years, Trump's figures of 40% to 60% are double to triple the actual rate. The price increases he cited actually correspond to timelines spanning 12 to 16 years.

claims.claim: Joe Biden doesn't have an immigration policy — he has an open-border policy.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim false; the Biden administration deported or removed millions of migrants and requested billions for border enforcement; an "open border" policy would permit free entry without legal consequence.

claims.claim: Biden has implemented a formal policy that illegal aliens who intrude into the United States are granted immunity from deportation.
claims.notes: Trump claimed Biden has a formal policy granting immunity to illegal immigrants; however, the administration has deported, removed, or returned individuals about 3.8 million times. While the DHS prioritizes certain removals due to resource constraints, it does not grant legal immunity.

claims.claim: Crime is down in Venezuela by 67% because they're taking their criminals and sending them to the United States.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the 67% figure was not supported by the cited sources; experts noted that Venezuelan crime data is unreliable and there is no evidence that Venezuela's government deliberately sent criminals to the United States.

claims.claim: Trump says he wants to deport ‘the worst of the worst,’ specifically violent criminals including murderers, rapists, and child predators.
claims.notes: Government data indicates that 71.7% of those in ICE custody have no criminal convictions, and among those convicted, only a small fraction committed violent offenses. Expert analysis suggests there is no evidence to support the claim that migrants are responsible for a surge in violent crime.

claims.claim: Joe Biden “is letting millions of people from jails, from prisons, from insane asylums, from mental institutions, drug dealers pour in.”
claims.notes: Trump claimed millions of immigrants are being released from foreign prisons and mental institutions into the U.S. Federal data shows approximately 103,700 noncitizens with criminal convictions were arrested between 2021 and 2024, which is far below the claimed 'millions.'

claims.claim: The U.S. had the 'greatest economy in history' during his presidency.
claims.notes: Economic growth averaged 2.67% during Trump's first three years, which is less than the 4% average under Bill Clinton. The pandemic-induced recession also led to massive job losses before he left office.

claims.claim: He's destroying Medicare because all of these people are coming in, they're putting them on Medicare. They're putting them on Social Security. They're going to destroy Social Security.
claims.notes: Trump falsely claimed that Biden is putting incoming migrants on Medicare and Social Security. Federal law prohibits unauthorized noncitizens from these benefits, and many immigrants contribute to these funds through taxes without eligibility.

claims.claim: Kamala Harris was "Indian all the way, and then all of a sudden, she made a turn and she went, she became a Black person."
claims.notes: Trump falsely claimed that Harris only recently began identifying as Black. Harris, the daughter of Jamaican and Indian immigrants, has long identified as a Black woman and a person of South Asian descent.

claims.claim: My Jan. 6 speech on the White House Ellipse drew a crowd as big as Martin Luther King's "I Have a Dream" speech on the Washington Mall.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated this False; the 1963 March on Washington drew an estimated 250,000 people; crowd scientists estimated Trump's Jan. 6 Ellipse rally at 30,000–50,000 people, well below MLK's audience.

claims.claim: His Jan. 6, 2021, speech on the White House Ellipse drew the "same number of people," as the 1963 March on Washington where Martin Luther King Jr. gave his "I Have a Dream" speech.
claims.notes: Trump claimed his January 6 crowd was equal to or larger than MLK's 1963 crowd. While the 1963 march drew an estimated 250,000 people, the House select committee estimated Trump's crowd at approximately 53,000.

claims.claim: There’s been a 43% increase in violent crimes since I left office.
claims.notes: While one DOJ survey showed a 43% increase between 2020 and 2022, FBI data and preliminary 2023-2024 reports show violent crime has actually declined. The claim uses selective, lagging data and ignores more recent trends and the primary FBI crime reporting system.

claims.claim: Vice President Kamala Harris ‐cast the tiebreaking votes that caused the worst inflation in American history, costing a typical American family $28,000.‐
claims.notes: Inflation peaked at a 40-year high but not an all-time record, and economists attribute the rise primarily to supply chain issues and the Ukraine war. The $28,000 cost figure is a credible cost estimate but fails to account for offsetting wage growth during the same period.

claims.claim: Harris cast the tiebreaking vote bringing in 10.5 million immigrants in a short period of time.
claims.notes: Reuters noted that "border czar" was never Harris's official role; she led diplomatic efforts on root causes of Central American migration, not border enforcement; she did not cast a vote to "bring in" immigrants.

claims.claim: Laken Riley was killed by an illegal alien — and these are the people Kamala Harris let into our country.
claims.notes: The Washington Post found that the murder suspect entered the U.S. in 2022 under the Biden-era parole program but was released — not a policy decision by Harris directly; the broader implication that Harris had personal authority over border releases was misleading.

claims.claim: Hundreds of thousands of unaccompanied migrant children who entered the U.S. from Mexico are unaccounted for or lost.
claims.notes: The claim misrepresents a DHS OIG report that criticized ICE for failing to track court notices for 291,000 children and 32,000 who missed hearings. Experts clarify this is a tracking and paperwork issue, not evidence that the children are physically missing.

claims.claim: The U.S. government fails to safeguard our wonderful, law-abiding American citizens while offering sanctuary and protection to dangerous criminals, many of whom have illegally crossed our borders from various countries, including those from prisons and mental health facilities.
claims.notes: Trump repeated a claim that foreign countries are emptying prisons and mental health facilities to send people to the U.S. AP found no substantiated evidence for this claim.

claims.claim: We are the only country in the world that does this with the birthright
claims.notes: Around 30 other countries, including Canada, Mexico, and most of Central and South America, also provide birthright citizenship. While rare in Europe and East Asia, the U.S. is not the only country with this policy.

claims.claim: The United States currently has the highest energy costs of any country in the world.
claims.notes: AP found the claim false; according to World Bank data, many European nations and others have significantly higher electricity prices than the United States; U.S. average residential electricity prices are among the lower rates for developed nations.

claims.claim: By contrast, our ideological opponents tried to replace it [America First] with something called "the new world order."
claims.notes: AP found Trump appropriated the phrase "new world order" — coined by President George H.W. Bush, a Republican — to vilify political opponents; the phrase has been used by both parties to describe post-Cold War international cooperation, not a replacement for American interests.

claims.claim: We are removing the worst criminal aliens, the most dangerous people on the planet.
claims.notes: AP found that ICE data showed a large proportion of people deported in the early Trump second-term had no criminal records or only minor offenses like traffic violations, contradicting the "worst of the worst" characterization.

claims.claim: The U.S. House Select Committee that investigated the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol "deleted and destroyed all of the information that they collected over two years."
claims.notes: The House Select Committee publicly released an 845-page final report and more than 100 transcripts of testimony that remain available online. While some sensitive video recordings were not archived for security reasons, the vast majority of evidence was preserved.

claims.claim: The January 6 committee deleted "all" evidence.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim false; the House Select Committee transferred records to the National Archives; the Archives confirmed it received all committee materials and they remain accessible.

claims.claim: We're deporting the worst of the worst — killers, rapists, the most violent criminals.
claims.notes: AP found that data from ICE shows the majority of deportees had no criminal records or had minor offenses; Trump's framing of deporting only the "worst of the worst" violent criminals did not match the statistics on who was actually being removed.

claims.claim: Ukraine... should have never started it [the war with Russia].
claims.notes: Donald Trump claimed that Ukraine started the war with Russia three years ago. Evidence confirms that Russia launched a massive, unprovoked invasion into Ukraine in February 2022, which Vladimir Putin announced as a 'special military operation.'

claims.claim: We have the most powerful military anywhere in the world, and nobody is even close.
claims.notes: Washington Post found Trump omitted that Biden significantly increased the defense budget and that NATO allies have also upgraded capabilities; independent indices consistently rank the U.S. military #1 regardless of who is president.

claims.claim: We have ended the invasion at our southern border. The border is now the most secure in history.
claims.notes: Washington Post found that while border encounters declined sharply in early 2025, claiming the border is "the most secure in history" lacks historical basis; some crossings continued and claiming an "invasion" has been "ended" overstates the situation.

claims.claim: Automobile plants are already starting to be built in the United States because of my tariffs.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found that the auto plant announcements cited by Trump were mostly plans already in progress before his tariffs took effect; no new plants had broken ground specifically because of the tariffs at the time of the claim.

claims.claim: The United States is taking in almost $2 billion a day in tariffs.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found that daily tariff collections were significantly below $2 billion; CBP data showed average monthly tariff revenue that, when divided by days, did not approach this figure, and analysts found the claim substantially overstated.

claims.claim: Tariffs will pay off the entire federal deficit.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found tariff revenues, projected at roughly $100-300 billion annually, fall far short of the $1.8 trillion federal deficit; paying off the deficit through tariffs alone would be arithmetically impossible.

claims.claim: The U.S. is “taking in almost $2 billion a day in tariffs.”
claims.notes: Trump claimed the U.S. was collecting $2 billion daily in tariffs, but Treasury Department records showed only $192 million. The $2 billion figure appeared to be a projection based on past trade volumes that failed to account for how tariffs typically reduce imports.

claims.claim: The U.S. has a $1 trillion trade deficit with China.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the U.S. goods trade deficit with China was approximately $279 billion in 2024 — less than $300 billion, far short of $1 trillion; Trump has repeatedly exaggerated this figure.

claims.claim: Prescription drug and pharmaceutical prices will be reduced, almost immediately, by 30% to 80%.
claims.notes: Trump's executive order involves a 30-day period for price targets and potential months or years of rulemaking, which contradicts the claim that prices would drop almost immediately.

claims.claim: Those crosses along the road are burial sites of white farmers who were killed. Those cars are stopped to pay respect to their family members who were killed.
claims.notes: AFP found the footage was from a 2020 protest where crosses marked a memorial along the road following a farm murder — not gravesites; the convicted killers were sentenced to life imprisonment in 2022.

claims.claim: There is a genocide of white farmers in South Africa. [Presenting image of Red Cross workers] Here are burial sites of white farmers who are being buried.
claims.notes: AFP found that an image Trump displayed showing Red Cross workers in hazmat suits was actually from a video of responders handling victims of a jailbreak atrocity in the Democratic Republic of Congo, not South Africa.

claims.claim: This is the biggest tax cut in history.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated Mostly False; the "Big Beautiful Bill" extended existing 2017 TCJA cuts rather than creating all-new cuts; as a share of GDP, the tax reduction did not rank as the largest in history.

claims.claim: Washington, D.C., has 41 homicides per 100,000 people, “No. 1 that we can find anywhere in the world.”
claims.notes: Trump cited outdated 2023 data and incorrectly claimed D.C. had the highest homicide rate in the world; at least 49 other cities had higher rates that year. Furthermore, D.C.'s homicide rate has decreased significantly in 2024 and 2025.

claims.claim: The Smithsonian Institution includes "nothing about success, nothing about brightness, nothing about the future."
claims.notes: PolitiFact visited three Smithsonian museums and found them full of exhibits about American achievements, technological ingenuity, military history, and pop culture; the National Museum of African American History focuses heavily on Black achievements.

claims.claim: Chicago is in chaos, and a video shows immigration agents and law enforcement responding to the 'mess' in the city.
claims.notes: Trump shared a White House video claiming to show current law enforcement raids in Chicago. AFP found the footage was actually outdated and filmed in Florida, Texas, and several other states, some dating back to 2023.

claims.claim: #BREAKING: DOGE halts yearly payment of $2.5 million to Barack Obama for ‘royalties linked to Obamacare,’
claims.notes: The claim that the Department of Government Efficiency stopped royalty payments to Barack Obama originated from a satirical website called the Dunning-Kruger Times. The Affordable Care Act does not pay royalties to Obama, and the story was fiction.

claims.claim: My tariffs are helping to slash the deficit this year by more than 25%.
claims.notes: The federal deficit decreased by only 2.3% in fiscal year 2025, significantly less than the 25% claimed. Analysis shows the deficit actually increased during the months Trump was in office in 2025 compared to the same period in 2024.

claims.claim: Over one year ago, we were a "dead" economy. Now we are the "hottest" country in the world.
claims.notes: AP found this misleading; when Trump took office the U.S. GDP had grown 2.8% in 2024, the strongest among wealthy nations except Spain, contradicting his claim of inheriting a "dead" economy; Q1 2025 GDP contracted for the first time in three years.

claims.claim: The United States previously required 72 jabs for children. Europe only requires 11.
claims.notes: AFP found that the "72 jabs" figure misrepresents the maximum doses through age 18 as mandatory injections; CDC recommendations are not federal mandates, and the comparison used the highest U.S. figure against the lowest European country's schedule.

claims.claim: Ending fraud, waste and abuse could balance the federal budget.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim false; the federal deficit is roughly $1.8 trillion, while independent estimates of government fraud, waste and abuse run in the tens of billions, far below what would be needed to balance the budget.

claims.claim: I settled eight wars.
claims.notes: AP found the claim significantly exaggerated; the conflicts Trump cited include a temporary 12-day ceasefire between Israel and Iran and unresolved disputes; no formal peace treaties have been ratified for most.

claims.claim: Biden-era inflation was the worst in 48 years, maybe the worst ever in my opinion.
claims.notes: AP found this incorrect; while Biden-era inflation peaked at 9.1% in June 2022, the U.S. experienced inflation over 13% in 1980 and estimates suggest it neared 20% during World War I, both higher than Biden-era peaks.

claims.claim: The dry fire hydrants during the Los Angeles wildfires were caused by California routing water to the Pacific Ocean to protect a tiny little fish.
claims.notes: AP found the claim misleading; local authorities stated the dry hydrants resulted from the municipal system's inability to handle the disaster's scale; water Trump later released went to a dry lake basin 100+ miles from Los Angeles, not to the city.

claims.claim: Among Somalis in Minnesota, '92% of them don’t work.'
claims.notes: Trump claimed that 92% of Somalis in Minnesota are unemployed. Official Census data shows that the actual non-working rate is approximately 28%, which is lower than the rate for Minnesotans overall.

claims.claim: The crime rate now is the lowest it's been since 1900. That's 125 years.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found that while the murder rate is at a multi-decade low, claiming it's the lowest since 1900 is unverifiable because pre-1960 crime data used inconsistent methodology and cannot reliably be compared to modern statistics.

claims.claim: We came into Minneapolis and within a few days, crime went down by 50%. They had the highest crime in the nation.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found both parts false; Minneapolis did not have the highest crime rate nationally and official crime data showed no 50% drop within days of the immigration enforcement surge; the city's crime rate had been declining before the operation.

claims.claim: The 2015 Iran nuclear agreement gave Iran the right to have top-of-the-line nuclear weapons.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim false; the JCPOA explicitly required Iran to limit uranium enrichment and reduce its stockpile, extending its nuclear "breakout" time; multiple experts confirmed the deal granted Iran no "right" to nuclear weapons.

claims.claim: In the past nine months, zero illegal aliens have been admitted to the United States.
claims.notes: AP found this misleading; while border encounters have dropped significantly and no migrants are being released while awaiting court proceedings, the claim omits that some are detained and others are expelled, not that zero have been admitted.

claims.claim: Our economy is roaring like never before.
claims.notes: AP found this inaccurate; after-tax real incomes increased only 0% in 2025, the smallest annual growth since 2022, declining from the 2.2% rise seen in Biden's final year in office.

claims.claim: Mail-in voting means mail-in cheating.
claims.notes: Trump claimed mail-in voting equates to cheating despite extensive studies showing that mail-in fraud is extremely rare and does not affect election outcomes. Trump himself cast a mail-in ballot in a Florida special election just after making the claim.

claims.claim: Haitian immigrants in Springfield Ohio have had their pets abducted and eaten - "Reports now show that people have had their pets abducted and eaten by people who shouldn't be in this country."
claims.impact: PolitiFact's 2024 Lie of the Year. Claim caused multiple bomb threats against Springfield hospitals, schools, and government buildings. Spread to 67 million debate viewers when Trump repeated it Sept 10.
claims.notes: Verified via PolitiFact Lie of the Year article. Vance's staffer was informed by a city official on the same day that comments were 'baseless.' Springfield police confirmed no credible reports. REVIEW: High electoral-political consequence - boosted anti-immigrant campaign narrative.

claims.claim: "If I have to create stories so that the American media actually pays attention to the suffering of the American people then that's what I'm going to do."
claims.verdict: Self-admission that he fabricated stories; cited by PolitiFact as part of the 2024 Lie of the Year
claims.impact: Vance's own admission cited in PolitiFact's 2024 Lie of the Year and widely reported.
claims.notes: Stated on CNN State of the Union Sep 15 2024. PolitiFact cites this as Vance's acknowledgment that he 'created stories' to attract media. Cross-verified by AP: https://apnews.com/article/vance-haitians-springfield-ohio-pets-false-claims-1c4c8a06ca7d0e1328ba6b9c1b5ca7ff

claims.claim: "Murders are up by 81% because of what Kamala Harris has allowed to happen to this small community [Springfield Ohio]."
claims.notes: Stated on CNN State of the Union Sep 15 2024. PolitiFact: Vance cherry-picked 2021-2023 window (5 to 9 murders = 80% not 81%); FBI data 2013-2022 shows no clear trend; Springfield had 13 homicides in 2018 under Trump. Republican Clark County prosecutor stated in 21 years he had seen no murders involving Haitians. Mostly False.

claims.claim: "In Springfield Ohio there has been a massive rise in communicable diseases. Communicable diseases like HIV and TB have skyrocketed."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: overall communicable disease rate (excl. COVID-19) in Clark County dropped in 2023 to its lowest since 2015. TB rose by one case (3 to 4); HIV preliminary 2023 data shows increase but epidemiologist told PolitiFact the change is 'not statistically significant.' Mostly False.

claims.claim: Says immigrants with temporary protections in Springfield Ohio are "illegal immigrants."
claims.impact: Claim made at VP debate; CBS moderator corrected Vance on air and his mic was cut. High national visibility.
claims.notes: PolitiFact: immigration experts confirmed both humanitarian parole and Temporary Protected Status are lawful statuses under US immigration law. 'Neither program lets people stay permanently but while the temporary protection is in place they are not here illegally.' False.

claims.claim: "We have 320,000 children that the Department of Homeland Security has effectively lost. Some of them have been sex trafficked."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: the Aug 2024 DHS OIG report said 32,000 children failed to appear for court and 291,000 had not been issued a Notice to Appear - it did not say children were 'lost' or cite sex-trafficking figures. Covers FY2019-2023 spanning two administrations. Also fact-checked by AP: https://apnews.com/article/fact-check-misinformation-migrant-children-missing-7ab0cea2fd2238346197429e952baa8b

claims.claim: "Iran which launched this attack has received over $100 billion in unfrozen assets thanks to the Kamala Harris administration."
claims.notes: AFP: the $100bn in frozen Iranian assets were released under the 2015 Obama-era nuclear deal; Harris was California AG at the time and had no role. In 2024 a $6bn Biden deal remained frozen after Oct 7 Hamas attacks per US officials. Also rated False by AP: https://apnews.com/article/debate-walz-vance-misinformation-fact-check-a3f67b1fdef425038dfac9267861247c

claims.claim: "We've got 20, 25 million illegal aliens who are here in the country."
claims.verdict: False - not supported by available data
claims.notes: AFP and Migration Policy Institute: DHS estimate as of Jan 2022 was 11 million; MPI estimate 11.3 million as of mid-2022. MPI: 'None of them is anywhere close to 20 to 25 million.' Also rated False by AP debate fact-check and PolitiFact VP debate roundup.

claims.claim: "There's a Federal Reserve study that really drills down on the connection between increased levels of migration especially illegal immigration and higher housing prices."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: the source was a blog post by Minneapolis Fed president Neel Kashkari - not a peer-reviewed study - that said immigration's long-run inflation effect is 'unclear.' It did not single out illegal immigration. Experts say housing shortage not immigrants is the primary driver. Mostly False.

claims.claim: "As I read the Minnesota law that [Walz] signed into law it says that a doctor who presides over an abortion where the baby survives the doctor is under no obligation to provide lifesaving care to a baby who survives a botched late-term abortion."
claims.notes: AP and PolitiFact: Walz's 2023 law updated language to 'care for the infant who is born alive'; retained 'An infant who is born alive shall be fully recognized as a human person and accorded immediate protection under the law.' Infanticide is illegal in all US states including Minnesota. False.

claims.claim: "What [Harris has] actually done instead is drive the cost of food higher by 25%, drive the cost of housing higher by about 60%."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: grocery prices rose 22% not 25% since Biden/Harris took office; Case-Shiller home price index rose 38% not 60%. Half True - both figures overstated.

claims.claim: "Kamala Harris let in fentanyl into our communities at record levels."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: fentanyl seizures have increased but the vast majority is smuggled through official ports of entry by US citizens or Mexican nationals with visas - not attributable to undocumented border crossings. Mostly False.

claims.claim: Donald Trump "peacefully gave over power on January the 20th as we have done for 250 years in this country."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: ignores Trump spending months claiming the election was stolen; telling supporters on Jan 6 to march to the Capitol; the violent mob that attacked the Capitol and delayed certification. Mostly False.

claims.claim: "So there's an application called the CBP One app where you can go on as an illegal migrant apply for asylum or apply for parole and be granted legal status" - characterised CBP One as the "Kamala Harris open border wand."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: CBP One was launched under Trump in 2020; users apply from outside the US at official ports of entry and have not crossed illegally. The app is a scheduling tool not an asylum or parole grant. Mostly False.

claims.claim: Says former President Donald Trump did not lose the 2020 election - "Did Donald Trump lose the election? Not by the words that I would use."
claims.impact: Reinforced election-denial messaging for the Trump-Vance ticket weeks before the Nov 2024 election.
claims.notes: PolitiFact: Biden won 306 electoral votes to Trump's 232; states certified results; 60+ lawsuits dismissed; Republican election officials confirmed results; Trump's own AG Barr called claims 'bull----.' Determining a presidential election winner is not a matter of 'word choice.' Pants on Fire!

claims.claim: Donald Trump "served as president for four years without pursuing his political rivals" while Harris "has weaponized the Department of Justice."
claims.notes: Washington Post (Glenn Kessler): identified at least a dozen examples of Trump demanding probes of political rivals including Clinton, Obama, Biden, Comey, McCabe and others. All inquiries fell short due to lack of evidence. Rated False.

claims.claim: "During the Trump administration we built 12,000 factories."
claims.notes: WaPo (Glenn Kessler): figure refers to BLS 'establishments in private manufacturing' - over 80% employ five or fewer workers; includes bakeries and home businesses. Claim was debunked during Trump's own presidency (repeated 15 times). Biden's record: net gain of ~39,000 such establishments vs ~18,000 under Trump's full term. Four Pinocchios. Stated on NBC Meet the Press Aug 25 2024.

claims.claim: "She [Harris] made the pivotal decision in favor of the so-called Inflation Reduction Act which allocated many of our resources to China" - the IRA is a "green energy scam" that ships jobs to China.
claims.notes: WaPo (Glenn Kessler): the IRA was explicitly designed to compete with China's dominance in green energy manufacturing by incentivising domestic US production. Treasury data showed the law was bringing manufacturing investment back to the US. Rated False. Rally in Eau Claire Wisconsin Aug 7 2024.

claims.claim: "[Vice President Harris] cast her vote to maintain NAFTA the trade agreement that transferred American employment opportunities to Mexico."
claims.notes: WaPo (Glenn Kessler): Harris was California AG when NAFTA was renegotiated and became USMCA; she did not vote to 'preserve' NAFTA. Harris is also a free-trade sceptic. Rated False. Rally in Glendale Arizona Jul 31 2024.

claims.claim: "How did we get to a point where Kamala Harris is calling on ending the child tax credit?"
claims.notes: PolitiFact: Harris has never called to end the child tax credit - on the contrary the Biden-Harris administration expanded it in 2021 and Harris repeatedly pushed Congress to make the expansion permanent. PolitiFact: 'Not only wrong but ridiculous.' Pants on Fire! Context: Vance was defending his 'childless cat ladies' remark made on Fox News in 2021.

claims.claim: Housing is expensive "because we flooded the country with 30 million illegal immigrants" and because of a housing shortage.
claims.notes: PolitiFact: Pew/DHS estimates put undocumented immigrants at 12-14 million as of 2023 - less than half of 30 million cited. Expert (Univ Washington): unauthorized immigration adds less than 0.5% to home costs vs 46% from mortgage rate increases. 'The contribution of unauthorized immigration to housing affordability problems is about 1/100 the magnitude of the impact of higher interest rates.' Mostly False. Interview on Fox News with Sean Hannity Nov 13 2025.

claims.claim: "Our Social Security system reveals that 40 percent of those reaching out are engaging in fraudulent activities. Consequently this implies that the 60 percent who genuinely require their Social Security payments are left waiting."
claims.notes: WaPo (Glenn Kessler): the 40% fraud figure was wildly inaccurate and misrepresented internal Social Security Administration data. Headline describes it as a 'whopper.' Rated False.

claims.claim: "Political violence, it's just a statistical fact that it's a bigger problem on the left."
claims.notes: PolitiFact: CSIS study of 750 terrorist attacks 1994-July 2025 found left-wing attacks outnumbered right-wing only in the first half of 2025. For the preceding 30+ years right-wing attacks predominated. Left-wing attacks killed 13 victims in the past decade vs 112 for right-wing. Stated on The Charlie Kirk Show podcast Oct 15 2025. Mostly False.

claims.claim: Second Springfield post (Sep 10 debate day): "Do you know what's confirmed? That a child was murdered by a Haitian migrant who had no right to be here." (Referring to Aiden Clark's death.)
claims.impact: Post received approximately 16 million views per Washington Post analysis.
claims.notes: PolitiFact: Aiden Clark's parents explicitly stated their son 'was not murdered. He was accidentally killed by an immigrant from Haiti.' The Haitian driver was convicted of involuntary manslaughter and vehicular homicide - not murder. Vance's use of 'murdered' contradicts the family's own public statement and the court verdict. Also corroborated by WaPo: https://www.washingtonpost.com/politics/2024/09/19/day-by-day-how-jd-vance-tweeted-misinformation-about-springfield/

claims.claim: "[Harris] used two programs to wave a wand and to say we're not going to deport those people here" - and "if Kamala Harris waves the wand illegally and says these people are now here legally, I'm still going to call them an illegal alien."
claims.notes: PolitiFact and immigration experts: TPS and humanitarian parole are statutory programmes established by Congress - not unilateral acts by Harris. TPS for Haiti was first established in 2010 under Obama. PolitiFact: Haitians under these programmes are legally present. False.

claims.claim: Months ago, I raised the issue of Haitian illegal immigrants draining social services and generally causing chaos all over Springfield, Ohio. Reports now show that people have had their pets abducted and eaten by people who shouldn't be in this country. Where is our border czar?
claims.impact: PolitiFact named this claim series its 2024 Lie of the Year
claims.notes: Springfield city manager confirmed the same day that the claims were baseless. No credible reports of pets being stolen or eaten were received by local police. Vance's staffer was told this before he posted.

claims.claim: Donald Trump had two choices. He could have destroyed the program [ACA], or he could actually build upon it and make it better so that Americans didn't lose a lot of health care. He chose to build upon a plan, even though it came from his Democratic predecessor.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found Trump cut ACA outreach funding by millions, enabled short-term plans that undermined ACA markets, and vocally backed congressional efforts to repeal the law. The record directly contradicts Vance's claim.

claims.claim: How did we get to this place where Kamala Harris is calling for an end to the child tax credit?
claims.impact: Widely cited in campaign coverage July-August 2024
claims.notes: PolitiFact found Harris has consistently supported expanding the child tax credit; the Biden-Harris administration expanded it in 2021 via the American Rescue Plan. Vance's claim is the opposite of reality.

claims.claim: Anything that you lose on the tariff from the perspective of the consumer, you gain in higher wages, so you're ultimately much better off.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found 94-95% of economists across four decades of surveys agreed tariffs reduce general economic welfare. Multiple academic studies confirm consumers bear most of the cost with no offsetting wage gains.

claims.claim: If you go back to the Trump presidency, we had 12,000 factories that were built during Donald Trump's presidency.
claims.notes: WaPo found the figure refers to small BLS manufacturing establishments — over 80% employ five or fewer people and include bakeries and tailors. Biden's record on the same metric was substantially better. Trump had repeated this debunked claim 15 times during his presidency.

claims.claim: [Harris] wants to take away your ability to eat red meat.
claims.notes: AFP found no evidence Harris supports banning red meat. Her 2019 statement said she would support revising dietary guidelines — not outlawing food. The Dietary Guidelines do not outlaw products.

claims.claim: Donald Trump salvaged The Affordable Care Act, better known as Obamacare, claiming it had been collapsing.
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the Trump administration repeatedly tried to repeal the ACA and asked the Supreme Court to overturn it in 2020. The court rejected the action in 2021. Trump cut ACA outreach and enrollment assistance throughout his term.

claims.claim: Vice President Harris became the appointed border czar.
claims.notes: AP confirmed Harris was tasked with diplomatic engagement on root causes of Northern Triangle migration — not border enforcement. DHS Secretary Mayorkas led immigration enforcement. The 'border czar' label was a Republican framing Harris explicitly rejected.

claims.claim: If we actually care about getting cleaner air and cleaner water, the best thing to do is to double down and invest in American workers and the American people. And unfortunately, Kamala Harris has done exactly the opposite.
claims.notes: AP found the Biden administration made $370 billion available for climate policy through the IRA — the largest-ever federal investment in domestic clean energy manufacturing and utilisation.

claims.claim: We are the cleanest economy in the entire world.
claims.notes: WaPo found the US ranked 17th globally on environmental health per the Yale/Columbia Environmental Performance Index in 2024. The US placed 27th on air quality and 9th on water and sanitation.

claims.claim: Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia was a convicted MS-13 gang member.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found Abrego Garcia has never been convicted of any crime per his attorney and court records. Two federal judges ruled government evidence of gang affiliation was unsupported; it relied on clothing and a since-fired officer's information.

claims.claim: Under the previous administration the cost of a median-price home in America more than doubled, and that was just in four years.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found four government/private metrics show home prices alone rose 18-37% under Biden — far short of doubling. Only when elevated mortgage rates are factored in does the total cost roughly double. PolitiFact rates this Mostly True as misleading without that critical context.

claims.claim: Democrats are threatening to shut down the entire government because they want to give hundreds of billions of dollars of health care benefits to illegal aliens.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found immigrants in the US illegally are largely ineligible for Medicare, Medicaid and ACA marketplace coverage. Democrats' proposal would restore coverage for legal immigrants losing access under Republicans' new tax law — not create new benefits for undocumented people.

claims.claim: Right now we're focused on Minneapolis because that's where we have the highest concentration of people who have violated our immigration laws.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found 78 US metro areas have a higher concentration of unauthorized immigrants than Minneapolis. The Minneapolis metro area undocumented share (2.4%) is well below the 4.1% national figure. Vance provided no evidence when asked.

claims.claim: Kamala Harris wants to give $25,000 to illegal aliens to buy American homes.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found Harris's first-time homebuyer assistance proposal did not specify that undocumented immigrants would qualify. Vance added the phrase 'illegal aliens' with no factual basis; Harris's campaign explicitly stated undocumented immigrants would not be eligible.

claims.claim: Did Donald Trump lose the election? Not by the measure I would use. ... I don't think Donald Trump lost the 2020 election.
claims.impact: Highly significant — denial of 2020 election result by VP nominee in final weeks of 2024 campaign
claims.notes: PolitiFact found Trump's own Attorney General, election officials in all 50 states, 60+ courts, the Electoral College, and Congress certified Biden's 2020 victory. No credible evidence of fraud sufficient to change the outcome was produced.

claims.claim: Under Donald Trump, housing price growth slowed to about 1-2% mainly because of negative net migration and the administration's efforts to remove undocumented immigrants.
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found home price growth slowed to ~1.6% but the deceleration began in early 2024 — months before Trump took office. Economists attribute the trend to high prices, elevated mortgage rates, demographic factors and buyer caution. Immigration played no demonstrated major role.

claims.claim: The Scottish government began distributing letters to citizens whose houses lay within so-called Safe Access Zones, warning them that even private prayer within their own homes may amount to breaking the law.
claims.notes: DW Fact Check/AFP found Scotland's law bans protest activities outside abortion clinics within 200m — not prayer at home. The Scottish Parliament member who introduced the bill called Vance's claim 'shocking and shameless misinformation'.

claims.claim: I look to Brussels, where EU commissars warn citizens that they intend to shut down social media during times of civil unrest the moment they spot what they've judged to be hateful content.
claims.notes: DW Fact Check found the EU Digital Services Act permits platform suspension only in extreme cases after all other remedies are exhausted — not simply upon spotting 'hateful content'. Vance omitted the multi-step due process required by the DSA.

claims.claim: "We're one of the seven nations in the world that allow abortion on demand at 20 weeks."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "The CBO says (the Build Back Better Act) is $3 trillion of deficit spending."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "Under the Affordable Care Act, three states get 35% of the money, folks. Can you name them? I'll help you: California, New York and Massachusetts. They're 22% of the population."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: Graham repeatedly stated in 2016 and 2018 that a Supreme Court vacancy should not be filled in a presidential election year — then in 2020 reversed course and supported filling Justice Ginsburg's seat immediately before the election.
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Contributed to major Senate fundraising surge for Democratic challenger Jaime Harrison in South Carolina. Galvanised opposition to Amy Coney Barrett's confirmation.

claims.claim: "The House of Representatives is abandoning more than a century's worth of precedent and tradition in impeachment proceedings and denying President Trump basic fairness and due process accorded every American."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "Block-granting welfare worked like a charm" — used to justify the Graham-Cassidy healthcare bill.
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "The phone records will be in the hands of the phone companies with hundreds of people available to look at the records, versus 20 or 30 people in the government."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "The labor participation rate is at an all-time low."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "If you took every penny of the 1 percent, including their dog, you wouldn't even begin to balance the budget."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "We're taking the military budget under sequestration cuts down to the smallest Army since 1940, the smallest Navy since 1915."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "Top U.S. intelligence officials have told every member of Congress, including the president, we're about to be attacked in a serious way because (of) the threat emanating from Syria and Iraq."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "People come here to have babies. They come here to drop a child. It's called 'drop and leave.'"
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "The average student will be spending $1,700 to $1,800 more during the life of their loan because of this surcharge."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "The Geneva Convention did not apply, until 2005, to the war on terror."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: "The U.S. sends $300 billion overseas to buy oil from people who hate our guts."
claims.impact: fact-checked

claims.claim: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz signed "legislation giving free college … to illegal immigrants."
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the claim is broadly accurate but too sweeping: Walz signed tuition aid bills covering students who are in the US illegally only if they meet several conditions (3+ years in Minnesota schools, income under $80k). Graham omitted key eligibility restrictions and the program is not described as "free college." No engagement figures published by PolitiFact.

claims.claim: Under the Affordable Care Act, three states receive 35% of the funding folks. … So, why do they obtain 35% of the funds when they represent merely 22% of the populace? That's how they structured the law; the more you expend, the more you gain.
claims.notes: AP Fact Check rated the claim misleading. Graham implied that Democratic lawmakers rigged funding in the ACA for their home states. AP found this false: higher premiums in those states — driven by local cost-of-living factors, not political design — account for the difference. South Carolina's low share is largely because it declined to expand Medicaid, where the federal government covers 90% of costs.

claims.claim: On confirming a Supreme Court justice nominee in a presidential election year [Graham stated in 2016 and 2018 that a vacancy should not be filled in an election year; in September 2020 he reversed and said he would support filling the vacancy immediately].
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated Full Flop on the Flip-O-Meter. In March 2016, Graham explicitly said: "If there's a Republican president in 2016 and a vacancy occurs in the last year of the first term, you can say Lindsey Graham said let's let the next president … make that nomination. And you could use my words against me, and you'd be absolutely right." He reiterated the position in October 2018. He then reversed within days of Justice Ginsburg's death.

claims.claim: Block-granting welfare worked like a charm.
claims.notes: Washington Post awarded Two Pinocchios. Graham used the 1996 welfare block-grant as a model for Graham-Cassidy. Fact Checker found that while caseloads fell, poverty did not decline; CBO data showed many families in need fell outside the TANF safety net; states were not held genuinely accountable for outcomes. Graham glossed over significant negative ramifications.

claims.claim: I am tired of sending $300 billion overseas to buy oil from people who hate our guts.
claims.notes: FactCheck.org rated misleading. The $302 billion figure cited is the total cost of all US oil imports in 2014 — including from Canada (~1.24 billion barrels) and Mexico (307 million barrels), countries with favorable views of the US (68% and 66% approval respectively). Only a fraction (under $61 billion) came from Russia, Iraq and Saudi Arabia. The phrase 'people who hate our guts' is therefore a significant exaggeration.

claims.claim: Russia has an economy the size of Italy.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated Mostly True. Measured by nominal GDP, Graham is correct — Russia's GDP is comparable to Italy's. However, measured by purchasing power parity (PPP), Russia's economy is substantially larger. The claim is accurate under one widely used metric but misleading under another equally legitimate one.

claims.claim: Nobody wants to displace a willing American worker, but I can tell you in South Carolina there are certain jobs, like in the meatpacking industry, that as an employer you can advertise all day long every day of the week and you're not going to get that workforce.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated Mostly True. Evidence supports genuine labor shortages in South Carolina meatpacking and agriculture. However, Graham's statement lacked important context: experts noted that low wages and harsh conditions in those industries are a significant reason US workers avoid them — a policy-relevant factor he omitted.

claims.claim: There was a time when someone like Scalia and Ginsburg got 95-plus votes.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated True. Justice Antonin Scalia was confirmed 98–0 in 1986; Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg was confirmed 96–3 in 1993. Graham's statement is accurate. Included here as a True check for completeness of Graham's full PolitiFact record.

claims.claim: Joe Biden voted against the first Gulf War. He opposed the surge. He wanted to partition Iraq.
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated True. All three claims about Biden checked out: he voted against the 1991 Gulf War authorization, opposed the 2007 surge of troops, and co-sponsored the Biden-Gelb plan for a soft partition of Iraq into autonomous regions. Included here for completeness of Graham's full PolitiFact record.

claims.claim: Without the widespread voter fraud, out-of-state voters, mail-in ballots from dead people, and 'discovery' of hidden ballots, we all know that President Trump wins our state in a landslide.
claims.impact: Greene voted against certifying Biden's 2020 electoral votes on January 6 2021. Georgia was certified for Biden after three recounts; no evidence of widespread voter fraud found by state officials or courts.
claims.notes: PolitiFact round-up article (2021-02-02) documents and rates this claim Pants on Fire. Date of claim approx December 14 2020 per article text.

claims.claim: I know we're not a blue state. I know for a fact that President Trump won here in Georgia. I feel it 1,000%.
claims.impact: Stated the day before Biden's inauguration. Biden won Georgia by 11779 votes confirmed by two machine counts and one hand recount. Statement rated Pants on Fire.
claims.notes: Rating explicitly noted as Pants on Fire in PolitiFact article 2021-02-02. Said on OANN on Jan 19 2021.

claims.claim: Reports from Whitfield County, GA that Dominion machines are flipping votes. This is exactly the kind of fraud we saw in 2020.
claims.impact: Spread during 2024 early voting period; Georgia state officials and Dominion publicly rebutted the claim. County elections supervisor confirmed single incident was user error; no machines removed from service.
claims.notes: Verified directly at PolitiFact URL. Also confirmed as false by AFP Fact Check (https://factcheck.afp.com/doc.afp.com.36KL244) on the same date.

claims.claim: Patents dating back to the 1800s show that people can control the weather.
claims.impact: President Biden publicly called the claim "beyond ridiculous" during Oct 9 Hurricane Milton response briefing. Claim spread amid Hurricane Helene/Milton disaster relief. Multiple meteorologists confirmed no technology exists to control or create hurricanes.
claims.notes: Full text of fact-check verified at PolitiFact URL. Greene repeated claim multiple times from Oct 3-9 2024.

claims.claim: Zelenskyy said he wants America's 'sons and daughters to go die in Ukraine.'
claims.impact: Zelenskyy's actual statement was about NATO mutual-defence obligations if Russia invaded a NATO member country — he did not say he wanted Americans to die in Ukraine.
claims.notes: Verified directly at PolitiFact URL.

claims.claim: Prosecutor Jack Smith "only targets Republicans."
claims.impact: During Smith's tenure as DOJ public corruption chief two Democratic senators — John Edwards and Bob Menendez — were charged. Claim rated False.
claims.notes: Verified directly at PolitiFact URL.

claims.claim: Illinois received about $5.1 billion at an elementary school there that used it for equity and diversity.
claims.impact: The $5.1 billion was federal pandemic aid distributed to all Illinois schools statewide; it was not sent to one school nor used solely for equity and diversity.
claims.notes: Verified directly at PolitiFact URL. Claim made during House Oversight hearing on COVID-19 spending.

claims.claim: Ukraine was the No. 1 donor to Hillary Clinton when she was running for president.
claims.impact: Clinton's 2016 campaign filed no donations from Ukraine or Ukrainian nationals. The Clinton Foundation confirmed it has never received funding from the Ukrainian government.
claims.notes: Verified directly at PolitiFact URL. Made at America First Rally in The Woodlands TX on Feb 19 2022.

claims.claim: Says Sens. Lisa Murkowski, Susan Collins and Mitt Romney 'are pro-pedophile.'
claims.impact: Claim arose in context of three Republican senators supporting Ketanji Brown Jackson's Supreme Court nomination. PolitiFact found all three had track records opposing child exploitation.
claims.notes: Verified directly at PolitiFact URL.

claims.claim: Your first question is a violation of my HIPAA rights. You see, with HIPAA rights, we don't have to reveal our medical records, and that also involves our vaccine records.
claims.impact: HIPAA only restricts health care providers and insurers; it does not bar reporters or employers from asking about vaccination status. Multiple legal experts confirmed the claim is incorrect.
claims.notes: Verified directly at PolitiFact URL. Said at press conference July 20 2021 when asked about vaccination status.

claims.claim: The FDA should not approve the covid vaccines. There are too many reports of infection & spread of #COVID19 among vaccinated people. These vaccines are failing & do not reduce the spread of the virus & neither do masks.
claims.impact: Twitter suspended Greene's account for one week under its COVID-19 misleading information policy (4th violation). Multiple public health experts told PolitiFact vaccines were highly effective at preventing severe illness and death.
claims.notes: PolitiFact article directly quotes and examines the Aug 9 2021 tweet. Twitter enforced 7-day suspension. Sourced as misleading by Twitter platform label.

claims.claim: On PBS, 'they now have drag queen storytime instead of good old Sesame Street.'
claims.impact: Sesame Street's 55th season continued airing on PBS. The drag queen segment was a 2021 digital project by a local NYC PBS station for the NYC Dept of Education — never nationally distributed. PBS CEO confirmed the segment "was not intended for national distribution" and "was never broadcast."
claims.notes: Verified at PolitiFact URL (published April 1 2025). Claim made on Newsmax March 25 2025 before House DOGE hearing on PBS/NPR funding.

claims.claim: [Speculated in a Facebook post that a laser beam from space may have started the Camp Fire in California, linking PG&E, the Rothschild family, and a proposed high-speed rail project.]
claims.impact: The Camp Fire (2018) killed 85 people. Cal Fire attributed the blaze to electrical transmission lines. Greene's deleted Facebook post was reported by Media Matters. PolitiFact describes the theory as bogus.
claims.notes: Date approximate (YYYY-MM-01); post made in November 2018. REVIEW: post invokes Rothschild family — antisemitic trope noted by SF Chronicle and Forbes. PolitiFact rates claim bogus in 2021-02-02 round-up.

claims.claim: [In an October 2017 article, speculated whether the Las Vegas massacre was orchestrated as part of a plot to dismantle Second Amendment rights, writing: 'Now there is another source that says that could be the very motive of the Las Vegas Massacre.']
claims.impact: The FBI closed its Las Vegas investigation in January 2019 finding no clear motive and no evidence of a Second Amendment conspiracy. 58 people were killed in the October 1 2017 shooting.
claims.notes: Date approximate (YYYY-MM-01); article published October 2017. PolitiFact describes the theory as bogus in 2021-02-02 round-up.

claims.claim: [In a Facebook comment on the Parkland school shooting, agreed with another commenter who called it a false flag event, writing 'Exactly' and suggesting the school resource officer was 'paid to do what he did and keep his mouth shut since it was a false flag planned shooting.']
claims.impact: The Parkland shooting killed 17 students and staff on February 14 2018. PolitiFact named false-flag smears of Parkland its 2018 Lie of the Year.
claims.notes: Date approximate (YYYY-MM-01); post made in May 2018 and later deleted. Greene subsequently told Congress in 2021 that school shootings are real.

claims.claim: [In a Facebook post speculated that: 'It's odd there's never any evidence shown for a plane in the Pentagon' in reference to the September 11 2001 attacks, and separately promoted the theory that MS-13 gang members working for the Obama administration murdered DNC staffer Seth Rich.]
claims.impact: Visual and forensic evidence confirms American Airlines Flight 77 struck the Pentagon on 9/11. Seth Rich's murder remains unsolved; DC Metro Police described it as a likely botched robbery. No credible evidence links MS-13 or the Obama administration to his death.
claims.notes: Date approximate (YYYY-MM-01). Both claims documented in PolitiFact 2021-02-02 round-up. Greene later stated in a 2021 House floor speech: ""9/11 absolutely happened. I do not believe that it's fake.""

claims.claim: COVID-19 is not dangerous for non-obese people and those under 65.
claims.impact: This tweet resulted in a 12-hour Twitter suspension per the HIPAA fact-check article's background. COVID-19 caused severe illness and death across all age groups; children and healthy adults were also hospitalised and died from the disease.
claims.notes: Tweet preceded the HIPAA press conference. PolitiFact's HIPAA fact-check (2021-07-21) references the prior tweet that led to the 12-hour ban. Separate claim from the August 9 vaccines-failing tweet.

claims.claim: 202,377 more votes cast than voters voting in Pennsylvania!
claims.impact: State officials and the Associated Press confirmed there were fewer votes cast than registered voters in Pennsylvania. The figure cited by Greene was traced to a data error by a Republican state legislator.
claims.notes: Rated as wrong/false in PolitiFact 2021-02-02 round-up. Associated Press also debunked the figure (AP: ""There were not more votes than voters in Pennsylvania"" Dec 29 2020).

claims.claim: Says an image shows the Highland Park shooting suspect 'in jail or rehab or a psychiatric center' before the shooting.
claims.notes: PolitiFact found the image was demonstrably doctored; a digital forensics expert confirmed "clear signs of manipulation." AP Fact Check independently verified the image was fabricated.

claims.claim: 'I was present [in] the chamber unlike A — Representative Ocasio-Cortez — who feigned her outrage with yet another fabrication, just another fabrication that circulates widely.'
claims.notes: WaPo's video team found no evidence Greene was on the House floor that day. AOC never claimed to be in the Capitol chamber — she was in her Cannon House Office Building office, not the Capitol itself.

claims.claim: [Compared COVID-19 mask mandates and vaccination requirements to Nazi persecution of Jews, saying they were 'exactly what the Nazis did' and that the Nazi Party was socialist.]
claims.notes: WaPo documented that while the Nazi party used 'socialist' in its name, it was virulently anti-socialist and suppressed socialist/communist parties. Comparing COVID health measures to Holocaust persecution was factually baseless.

claims.claim: "Yes they can control the weather. It's ridiculous for anyone to lie and say it can't be done."
claims.notes: PolitiFact rated the claim Pants on Fire. X Community Note was added clarifying hurricanes cannot be artificially created or steered. Meteorologists and atmospheric scientists rejected the claim entirely.

claims.claim: [Tweeted on and after January 6 claiming Capitol rioters were Antifa members dressed as Trump supporters, not Trump supporters themselves.]
claims.notes: PolitiFact found no credible evidence that Antifa organized or participated in the Jan. 6 Capitol attack. FBI investigations and subsequent prosecutions identified hundreds of rioters as Trump supporters with no Antifa affiliation. | Topical fact-check: PolitiFact attributed this Antifa/Capitol claim to its original source, not MTG individually; MTG amplified it. URL verified live (HTTP 200).

claims.claim: [Amplified false claims that the Uvalde, Texas Robb Elementary School shooter was transgender or a 'transsexual leftist illegal alien.']
claims.notes: PolitiFact found no evidence shooter Salvador Ramos was transgender or a non-citizen. The false claim originated from a fabricated tweet and misidentified photos of an unrelated transgender artist. | Topical fact-check (facebook-posts): covers the Uvalde-shooter-transgender claim MTG amplified; no MTG-specific article exists. URL verified live (HTTP 200).

claims.claim: [Promoted the anti-vaccine documentary 'Died Suddenly,' which falsely claims COVID-19 vaccines are killing large numbers of people and are linked to 'Great Reset' conspiracy theories.]
claims.notes: Lead Stories found the documentary's central claims — that COVID-19 vaccines cause mass sudden deaths — were not proven. Medical experts noted it selectively attributed deaths to vaccines without controlling for baseline death rates. | Lead Stories topical fact-check of the 'Died Suddenly' film MTG promoted; no MTG-specific article exists. URL verified live (HTTP 200).

claims.claim: [Claimed that Representatives Ilhan Omar and Rashida Tlaib were not legitimate members of Congress because they were sworn in on the Quran rather than the Bible.]
claims.notes: PolitiFact confirmed the U.S. Constitution (Art. VI) explicitly prohibits any religious test for office. No specific text is required for the oath of office; an affirmation without any book is also legally valid. | Topical fact-check (viral-image): covers the Quran swearing-in claim MTG repeated; no MTG-specific article exists. URL verified live (HTTP 200).

claims.claim: [Tweeted about 'Antifa driven trans-terrorism' after the Nashville Covenant School shooting, linking the LGBTQ+ community to terrorism without evidence.]
claims.notes: PolitiFact consulted domestic extremism experts who found no evidence of a growing trend of transgender radicalization or violence. Research indicates trans people are more likely to be victims of violent crime than perpetrators.

claims.claim: "We are witnessing a communist takeover of our judicial system to target political enemies and destroy their lives. It's despicable."
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found the claim ignores that Proud Boys leader Enrique Tarrio was convicted by a jury of peers and sentenced by a Trump-appointed federal judge — not by Biden administration officials as Greene implied.

claims.claim: "New Orleans terrorist attacker is said to have come across the border in Eagle Pass TWO DAYS AGO!!! Shut the border down!!!"
claims.notes: FactCheck.org confirmed the New Orleans attacker, Shamsud-Din Jabbar, was a U.S. Army veteran born in the United States. Fox News, which initially reported the false 'border crossing' claim, later issued a correction.

claims.claim: "The Special Counsel's report exposing that Joe Biden's mental decline is so severe that he can not stand trial means he is unfit for office."
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found Special Counsel Hur's report said no such thing. The decision not to charge Biden was based on the full evidentiary context, not a finding of mental incapacity. Greene and others distorted the report's language.

claims.claim: "This is a map of hurricane affected areas with an overlay of electoral map by political party shows how hurricane devastation could affect the election."
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found zero evidence anyone attempted to steer Hurricanes Helene or Milton toward Republican-voting areas. The post implied deliberate weather manipulation to affect the 2024 election; no such technology exists. Community Note added on X.

claims.claim: [Told reporters after Devon Archer's closed-door testimony that Archer said he heard Hunter Biden speak to Joe Biden 'more than 20 times about their business deals.']
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found the publicly released transcript showed Archer said Biden only exchanged pleasantries with business associates and never discussed business deals. Greene's characterisation directly misrepresented Archer's testimony.

claims.claim: [Retweeted a post claiming 'Biden Announced The US TaxPayers Will Now Be PAYING The Pensions & Welfare For The People Of Ukraine' and called on Congress to impeach Biden over this.]
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found Biden's April 2022 remark about Ukraine aid covering some pensions had been publicly known for months and was not the new outrage the post implied. The original claim misrepresented the scope and purpose of the aid package.

claims.claim: [Embraced and promoted the 'pure blood' conspiracy theory, using the term to describe unvaccinated people and implying blood transfusions from vaccinated donors pose a danger to unvaccinated recipients.]
claims.notes: FactCheck.org's SciCheck found the 'pure blood' concept is 'patently false.' Blood transfusions do not transfer COVID-19 vaccine immunity or mRNA. Blood banks do not screen or separate donations based on vaccination status.

claims.claim: "Many women have reported problems with their menstrual cycles after taking the vaccines. And women have reported miscarriages as well. We need answers and we will be asking."
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found vaccines do not harm fertility or cause miscarriages. Minor temporary menstrual cycle changes are documented in peer-reviewed literature but are not clinically significant. Greene retweeted a Project Veritas video amplifying this misinformation.

claims.claim: "Lasers.. CBS, 9 years ago, talked about lasers controlling the weather."
claims.notes: FactCheck.org found lasers cannot control or create a hurricane. The CBS segment referenced concerned small-scale atmospheric research with no applicability to hurricane-scale weather systems. Community Note context same as Oct 3 post.

claims.claim: [In an online video, claimed Obama used MS-13 gang members to assassinate DNC staffer Seth Rich: 'Seth Rich was murdered by two MS-13 gang members. That's what I mean by dirty work.']
claims.notes: PolitiFact documented this in its 2021 review of Greene's pre-Congress conspiracy claims. Seth Rich's murder remains unsolved; DC police found no evidence of MS-13 involvement. The Obama-MS-13 conspiracy was debunked by multiple investigators.

claims.claim: [In a 2019 Facebook video, expressed agreement that Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg had been replaced by a body double: 'I do not believe that was Ruth, no. I don't think so.']
claims.notes: PolitiFact documented this in its 2021 review of Greene's pre-Congress conspiracy activity. Ginsburg continued to serve on the Supreme Court, was seen publicly, and her staff confirmed her identity. No evidentiary basis for the claim existed.

claims.claim: The COVID-19 vaccine is the "deadliest vaccine ever made."
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Widely cited in anti-vaccine campaigns; claim made to Louisiana state lawmakers considering a school vaccine mandate.

claims.claim: "COVID-19 is targeted to attack Caucasians and Black people. The people who are most immune are Ashkenazi Jews and Chinese."
claims.platform: Dinner in New York City (video recording)
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Caused widespread condemnation including from Kennedy's own family; made during his 2024 presidential primary campaign.

claims.claim: "DARPA is spraying the skies with chemicals that are in jet fuel."
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: PolitiFact: DARPA did not respond; Kennedy's office provided no evidence; atmospheric science experts confirmed no such programme exists; claim builds on widely debunked chemtrails conspiracy. Verified via direct fetch.

claims.claim: "Everybody can get" the COVID-19 vaccine.
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Statement made during Kennedy's confirmation-related testimony as sitting HHS Secretary.

claims.claim: "90% of health care spending treats chronic disease."
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: PolitiFact: The statistic misrepresents a 2017 Rand Corp report; the 90% figure covers all spending on people who have any chronic condition, not spending on treatment of chronic disease specifically. Verified via direct fetch.

claims.claim: "The CDC is not an independent agency. It is a vaccine company [...] The CDC owns over 20 vaccine patents. It sells about $4.6 billion of vaccines every year."
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Health Feedback: CDC does not sell vaccines; $4.6 billion figure is the CDC's budget to purchase and distribute vaccines for free to children; CDC holds patents on technology it licences to third parties but this does not make it a vaccine company. Verified via direct fetch. Date estimated from publication context (YYYY-MM-01 per spec).

claims.claim: "As long as you take an Emergency Use [vaccine], you can't sue them. Once they get approved, now you can sue them—unless they can get it recommended for children"; "Under the Vaccine Act, you cannot sue a vaccine company for injuring you, no matter how negligent they were."
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Health Feedback: People can sue vaccine manufacturers for childhood vaccine injuries after exhausting the VICP; Kennedy himself filed multiple suits against Merck over Gardasil. Verified via direct fetch. Date estimated as YYYY-MM-01 per spec (published Nov 2022).

claims.claim: Gardasil (HPV vaccine) is associated with "autoimmune diseases and menstrual cycle problems and fertility problems."
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Science Feedback: Multiple large studies including a 2.5-million-person meta-analysis found no consistent evidence of increased risk of autoimmune or neurological diseases from HPV vaccine. Date estimated as YYYY-MM-01 per spec. Verified via direct fetch summary.

claims.claim: Event 201 (October 2019 pandemic simulation) is evidence that officials "knew that the COVID-19 pandemic was already happening or was about to begin."
claims.impact: fact-checked
claims.notes: Health Feedback: Event 201 was organised months before any known COVID-19 cases; scenario involved a fictitious coronavirus; Johns Hopkins confirmed the scenario was purely fictional; coronaviruses had long been recognised as pandemic risks. Date estimated YYYY-MM-01 per spec. Verified via search result summary.

claims.claim: Kennedy refused to state that vaccines do not cause autism; pointed to a single methodologically flawed paper as credible evidence of a vaccine-autism link.
claims.impact: fact-checked
