claims.claim: Congress shared a doctored/deepfake video of Home Minister Amit Shah saying he will scrap SC/ST/OBC reservations
claims.impact: During 2024 Lok Sabha election campaign.
claims.notes: BOOM found the Amit Shah video was doctored using old editing tools (not AI deepfake) and was decontextualised — Shah was speaking about removing only the Muslim OBC quota, not all SC/ST/OBC reservations. Malviya shared original video to counter the claim, but framing the edits as 'deepfake' was itself misleading.

claims.claim: Serial fake news peddler 'Vikrama Sharma' affiliated to NAMO Facebook page — admitted BJP IT cell provides him content; PMO India Report Card Twitter handle followed by Malviya
claims.impact: During 2019 Lok Sabha election campaign.
claims.notes: Quint documented that Vikrama Sharma — who had spread fake news including Balakot body count and ABVP Kerala fake victory claim — said: 'The IT cell provides me content which I disseminate through Facebook and WhatsApp.' His account was followed by Amit Malviya.

claims.claim: Coordinated campaign to claim Shaheen Bagh women were paid Rs 500 each — amplified across BJP IT cell handles, Times Now, Republic and OpIndia
claims.impact: During Delhi assembly election campaign.
claims.notes: Joint Alt News–Newslaundry investigation traced video to Kusmi Telecom shop in Pul Prahladpur. Times Now broadcast with disclaimer; OpIndia published supportively. MyNation (BJP-linked) asserted women were paid. Shop owner eventually confirmed video was shot there but the claims were unreliable.

claims.claim: New CCTV footage of wanted gangster Guddu Muslim, spotted in Odisha
claims.notes: Multiple channels ran CCTV clip of Sheikh Hamid Mohammad (a Muslim man from Sohela, Odisha, at a mosque during Ramzan) as gangster Guddu Muslim. Anchor Subhankar Mishra on Aaj Tak said 'This is the latest video of gangster Guddu Muslim.' The man had no connection to the Umesh Pal murder case. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal misidentification of named individual.

claims.claim: New video of Guddu Muslim surfaced; shooter was seen in Odisha
claims.notes: ABP News and ABP Ganga broadcast the same Odisha CCTV clip as footage of gangster Guddu Muslim. Footage was of an innocent man visiting a mosque in Ramzan. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal misidentification of named individual.

claims.claim: Guddu Muslim seen in Odisha — video of April 11 2023
claims.notes: News18 India anchor Anand Narsimhan claimed the clip showed Guddu Muslim at his last known location. News18 later deleted its tweet. Footage was of an unrelated man at a mosque. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal misidentification of named individual.

claims.claim: Gangster Guddu Muslim spotted in Odisha — new CCTV footage
claims.notes: TV9 Bharatvarsh ran the same unrelated Odisha CCTV clip as Guddu Muslim footage. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal misidentification of named individual.

claims.claim: Gangster Guddu Muslim spotted in Odisha CCTV
claims.notes: News Nation broadcast the unrelated Odisha CCTV clip as Guddu Muslim footage. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal misidentification of named individual.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia has banned loudspeakers in mosques during Ramzan
claims.notes: Alt News found Saudi Arabia restricted the number of loudspeakers to four but did not ban them; the claim of a 'ban' was false. CNN-News18 was among the amplifiers alongside ABP and India TV. Claim drew from a misreading of a Saudi Ministry of Islamic Affairs circular.

claims.claim: Old photo shared as massive traffic jam near Atal Tunnel after Christmas 2022
claims.notes: Alt News found the circulating photo was from January 2022, not Christmas 2022. Times of India and Hindustan Times used it in coverage of Himachal Pradesh tourist rush without verification.

claims.claim: Old photo shared as massive traffic jam near Atal Tunnel after Christmas 2022
claims.notes: Same as above — Hindustan Times published the year-old image as current evidence of post-Christmas tourist rush near Atal Tunnel.

claims.claim: New video of Delhi violence: man seen shooting at police in Maujpur during Delhi riots
claims.notes: Alt News found the clipped video was from 25 February, not new, and showed a member of the pro-CAA mob firing at Muslims in Vijay Park — not at police. Times Now called it the '4th video of attack on police.' Longer video and on-ground reporters confirm the opposite. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal violence context.

claims.claim: Breaking news: China accepts 30 soldiers have been killed during the Ladakh standoff — names read out from Global Times
claims.notes: Times Now anchors Rahul Shivshankar and Navika Kumar read out 30 names of supposed dead Chinese soldiers citing Global Times; Alt News found Global Times had published no such list; source was a WhatsApp forward. Tweets later deleted.

claims.claim: Photos of 106 PLA tombstones reveal extent of Chinese casualties in June 15 Galwan clash
claims.notes: The images showed the Kangxiwa military cemetery containing graves of PLA soldiers killed in the 1962 Sino-Indian war. The oldest Google Earth imagery used by India Today dates to 2011, years before the Galwan clash. Times Now added 'PM Modi was right about Galwan grit' framing.

claims.claim: 40 PLA soldiers killed in Galwan clashes — images of graves as proof
claims.notes: Aaj Tak claimed 40 PLA soldiers died and showed satellite images of the Kangxiwa cemetery (1962 war graves, photographed in 2011) as evidence. A defence expert on the same show noted graves had been present since at least December 2019.

claims.claim: Exclusive: Pakistani Air Force attacking Panjshir Valley; Pakistan army supporting Taliban against Northern Alliance
claims.notes: Republic TV aired ARMA 3 gameplay as 'exclusive' evidence of Pakistani airstrikes. Fact-checker Mohammed Zubair traced footage to a January 2021 YouTube ARMA 3 video. Republic later deleted the content and issued a non-apology attributing it to source 'Hasti TV'.

claims.claim: Pakistani air force attacking Panjshir — video footage of Pakistani jet over the valley
claims.notes: TV9 Bharatvarsh aired the same ARMA 3 clip attributed to Hasti TV. Alt News noted TV9 had also broadcast the same ARMA 3 clip the previous year as visuals of a MiG-25 shot down in the Armenia-Azerbaijan conflict.

claims.claim: Exclusive: Military mobilisation in Beijing; elite forces deployed at Xinhua Gate; Xi Jinping under house arrest — coup in China
claims.notes: Republic Bharat broadcast a 10-minute segment based on Der Spiegel journalist Georg Fahrion's explicitly sarcastic Twitter thread about Beijing. Fahrion publicly mocked the channel: 'Two things are infinite, the universe and man's stupidity.' Content deleted from YouTube and Facebook.

claims.claim: UPSC Jihad: Muslims are infiltrating India's civil services with unfair advantages in age limit, attempts, cut-offs, coaching, and interviews
claims.notes: Alt News documented six specific false claims. Supreme Court called show 'insidious' and 'a great disservice to the nation'; I&B Ministry cautioned the channel for 13 code violations. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active Supreme Court proceedings at time of broadcast.

claims.claim: Maulana Qadri issued a fatwa against Priya Varrier, saying she appears to him during prayers — an insult to Islam
claims.notes: Aaj Tak's Halla Bol programme (anchor Anjana Om Kashyap) built its entire opening segment around a tweet from '@TiimesHow', a clearly labelled parody account. No fatwa was issued; a police complaint (not a fatwa) was filed in Hyderabad by Muslim youth. Alt News noted this was not the first such incident.

claims.claim: Massive gold discovery in Sonbhadra, UP: India to become world's second-largest gold reserve nation with 3,350 tonnes
claims.notes: Aaj Tak called India 'phir sonay ki chidiya' (golden bird again). GSI Director General clarified no such discovery existed; the exploration was from 1998-2000 and identified 160 kg of extractable gold from 52,806 tonnes of ore — not 3,350 tonnes. Outlets conflated ore with refined gold.

claims.claim: Sonbhadra gold discovery: hillock that will change India's fortunes — 3,350 tonnes
claims.notes: ABP News described the discovery in similar terms. GSI confirmed the exploration was decades old and extractable gold was only 160 kg.

claims.claim: Indian Navy has taken action and attacked Karachi port — all-around attack on Pakistan; Lahore and Karachi suffering heavy losses (Operation Sindoor)
claims.notes: Anchor Anjana Om Kashyap broadcast claim of naval attack on Karachi; two visuals used were unrelated old footage. Karachi Port Trust confirmed port was 'operating normally and securely' the following morning. No Indian Navy action on Karachi was confirmed.

claims.claim: Karachi port has been demolished by the Indian Navy (Operation Sindoor)
claims.notes: Zee News anchor stated 'Karachi port ko tabah kar diya hai nausena ne.' No Indian Navy action on Karachi was confirmed; Karachi Port Trust issued a public denial.

claims.claim: Multiple explosions heard at Karachi port — all-out attack against Pakistan; heavy damage to Karachi port due to Indian strike
claims.notes: TV9 Bharatvarsh broadcast claim of attack on Karachi port; image used in report was from a 2023 naval drill (ship featured was INS Vikramaditya, not INS Vikrant). No confirmed attack.

claims.claim: INS Vikrant action in Karachi; several explosions at Karachi port last night — Indian Navy attacked Karachi (Operation Sindoor)
claims.notes: ABP News anchor claimed 12 explosions were 'reportedly' heard in Karachi. No confirmed attack; Karachi Port Trust denied any incident.

claims.claim: 4-year-old Iron Dome footage presented as live video of Indian air defence foiling Pakistani air attack in Jaisalmer
claims.notes: Anchors Anjana Om Kashyap and Shweta Singh broadcast footage claiming it showed Pakistani air attack foiled in Jaisalmer 'a short while ago.' Alt News traced the clip to NSFchannel on YouTube, May 11 2021 — Israel's Iron Dome. At least 10 channels aired the same footage.

claims.claim: 4-year-old Iron Dome footage presented as live video of Indian air defence foiling Pakistani air attack in Jaisalmer
claims.notes: ABP News broadcast the same 4-year-old Iron Dome footage as live Jaisalmer footage. Same finding as row above.

claims.claim: 4-year-old Iron Dome footage presented as live video of Indian air defence foiling Pakistani air attack in Jaisalmer
claims.notes: News18 broadcast same 4-year-old Iron Dome footage as live Jaisalmer India-Pakistan conflict footage.

claims.claim: 4-year-old Iron Dome footage presented as live video of Indian air defence foiling Pakistani air attack in Jaisalmer
claims.notes: India TV broadcast same 4-year-old Iron Dome footage as live Jaisalmer footage during Operation Sindoor.

claims.claim: 4-year-old Iron Dome footage presented as live video of Indian air defence foiling Pakistani air attack in Jaisalmer
claims.notes: News Nation broadcast same 4-year-old Iron Dome footage as live Jaisalmer footage during Operation Sindoor.

claims.claim: Footage presented as India's air defence destroying Pakistani jets and missiles during Operation Sindoor (on debut DD News show)
claims.notes: On his first DD News show, Chaudhary showed Iranian missiles targeting Israel's Nevatim airbase (Oct 2024) as India-Pakistan conflict footage. The same DD India YouTube channel had the original video with correct caption since Oct 2024. The problematic section was later silently deleted.

claims.claim: Explosion near the Pentagon — blast reported near US Department of Defense
claims.notes: Republic and other Indian channels aired an AI-generated image of a 'Pentagon explosion' originating from a QAnon-aligned account amplified by a fake Bloomberg account. Arlington Fire & EMS explicitly denied any explosion. Republic later issued a correction.

claims.claim: Explosion near the Pentagon — blast reported near US Department of Defense
claims.notes: Zee News published and then removed a video report on the AI-generated 'Pentagon explosion' without correction. Same AI-generated image as row above.

claims.claim: Pakistan bombing Panjshir valley — footage of Pakistani airstrikes on resistance fighters
claims.notes: Zee Hindustan broadcast the same ARMA 3 gameplay video as 'Pakistan bombing Panjshir' footage sourced from Hasti TV. Later deleted.

claims.claim: Asaduddin Owaisi danced in celebration after Hindu Samaj leader Kamlesh Tiwari was murdered
claims.notes: ANI had uploaded the video on October 18 but the event was clearly captioned as October 17 — the day before Tiwari was killed. Sudarshan News's programming head admitted the error was due to 'confusion' but Chavhanke refused to retract. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal incitement context.

claims.claim: Video of sword-wielding people chanting slogans calling to kill RSS workers at Muharram procession
claims.notes: Alt News found the video was morphed — anti-RSS audio chants were added to original video of a Muharram procession from Dehri, Bihar. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — fabricated audio to incite communal violence.

claims.claim: Only Muslims were given jobs in government-owned Pawan Hans Limited; Hindu students discriminated against
claims.notes: Alt News investigation found recruitment was open and based on merit; the claim was propaganda based on incomplete data. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — communal framing.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia to teach Ramayana and Mahabharata in schools under Vision 2030
claims.notes: Saudi Vision 2030 makes no mention of Ramayana or Mahabharata in school curricula. The source tweet (by Nouf Marwaai) was about a private school social studies test with a passing reference to Indian epic poetry — not a national curriculum change. Marwaai herself told Alt News the coverage misrepresented her tweet.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia to teach Ramayana and Mahabharata in schools under Vision 2030
claims.notes: Same false claim as above; ABP Education published the story without verification.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia to teach Ramayana and Mahabharata in schools under Vision 2030
claims.notes: Same false claim; multiple outlets including Republic TV, TV9 Hindi and News18 picked up the story. Saudi Embassy did not issue a national curriculum change announcement.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia to teach Ramayana and Mahabharata in schools under Vision 2030
claims.notes: Same false claim; News18 among the outlets that published without contacting the Saudi Embassy or the source.

claims.claim: New JDU posters of Nitish Kumar 'Nitish Sabke Hain' put up in Bihar amid political crisis
claims.notes: ABP News anchor explicitly described the posters as 'new' during the Bihar political crisis; Alt News found the image was from a BBC Marathi article dated November 2020 (during Bihar elections). ABP Bihar's own 2020 footage showed the same poster.

claims.claim: Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has surrendered; Islamabad is under attack by Indian forces (Operation Sindoor)
claims.notes: Zee News claimed Sharif had surrendered and that Islamabad was under attack. No credible source from either country confirmed these claims. Karachi Port Trust and Pakistan's ISPR denied. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — war-time false claims regarding foreign head of government.

claims.claim: Islamabad is under attack by Indian forces; Shehbaz Sharif's house targeted (Operation Sindoor)
claims.notes: Sudarshan News among outlets claiming Islamabad and PM's residence were under attack. No official Indian or Pakistani source confirmed. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — war-time false claims.

claims.claim: Top Lashkar terrorist Qari Mohammad Iqbal killed in Operation Sindoor strike at Kotli, Pakistan
claims.notes: Multiple mainstream outlets including CNN-News18, ABP News, Republic and Zee News claimed Qari Mohammad Iqbal was killed in Kotli. No official Indian or Pakistani source confirmed the claim.

claims.claim: BSES gave jolt to Jama Masjid: mosque electricity cut for non-payment of Rs 4 crore
claims.notes: Alt News found the story was fake; BSES itself had already tweeted clarifications. Republic TV deleted its tweet and video without apology. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — defamatory communal framing of named religious institution.

claims.claim: Arundhati Roy said '70 lakh Indian army cannot defeat Azadi gang in Kashmir' — quote attributed to non-existent interview
claims.notes: Alt News traced the fake quote to obscure Pakistani website timesofislamabad.com and Postcard News. Arnab Goswami hosted prime-time debate calling Roy a 'one-book whiner wonder' on the basis of fabricated quote. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — defamation of named individual.

claims.claim: India Today: Indian Navy attacked Karachi port — INS Vikrant leading extreme action (Operation Sindoor)
claims.notes: India Today tickers flashed 'Indian Navy attacks Pakistan's Karachi' and 'Exclusive.' Screengrab from viral video used. Karachi Port Trust confirmed it was operating normally. Image later swapped for generic INS Vikrant photo without correction.

claims.claim: AI-generated images of US actor Dwayne Johnson performing aarti inside a Hindu temple are real photographs
claims.notes: Images were AI-generated by Ahmedabad-based photographer Bhargav Valera; The Rock's distinct tattoos are absent in the viral images.

claims.claim: CCTV footage of a man in a white shirt at a mosque in Odisha shows wanted gangster Guddu Muslim
claims.notes: Man in CCTV footage is Sheikh Hamid Mohammad from Bargarh district going to a mosque during Ramzan; falsely identified as Guddu Muslim on air. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED (named innocent individual as criminal).

claims.claim: CCTV footage of a man in a white shirt at a mosque in Odisha shows wanted gangster Guddu Muslim
claims.notes: Same false identification as Aaj Tak; innocent Odisha man Sheikh Hamid Mohammad filmed during Ramzan prayers; also carried by News18 India; TV9 Bharatvarsh; News Nation and others. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: CCTV footage of a man in a white shirt at a mosque in Odisha shows wanted gangster Guddu Muslim
claims.notes: Multiple mainstream channels falsely broadcast innocent man as criminal fugitive during Ramzan; broadcaster has not issued correction. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Video of a train crossing a bridge is footage of the trial run of a train on the Chenab Railway Bridge in Jammu and Kashmir
claims.notes: Video is of Beipanjiang Railway Bridge (Shuibai) in Guizhou province China. BJP national spokesperson tweet had 17000 views. Also shared by former BJP MP Boora Narsaiah Goud and industrialist Anand Mahindra who later deleted his retweet.

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia has banned loudspeakers in mosques during Ramzan
claims.notes: Saudi Islamic affairs ministry circular only regulated loudspeaker volume and broadcast duration; it did not ban loudspeakers. Also spread by OpIndia; Major Surendra Poonia; Panchjanya and India TV. (translated from Hindi)

claims.claim: Clipped video shows Uddhav Thackeray referring to Mughal emperor Aurangzeb as his brother
claims.notes: Full video shows Thackeray quoting what BJP supporters say about Aurangzeb; clip reversed meaning. BJP MLA Maharashtra tweet garnered 450000 views. (translated from Hindi) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Photo shows Rahul Gandhi meeting the producer of the BBC documentary India: The Modi Question six months earlier in London
claims.notes: Photo shows Gandhi with Labour MP Jeremy Corbyn and Sam Pitroda during May 2022 London visit; BBC documentary producer is Richard Cookson with no connection to Corbyn. Former Deputy CM J&K and BJP Andhra Pradesh general secretary Vishnu Vardhan Reddy both spread the claim.

claims.claim: Photo shows massive traffic jam near Atal Tunnel after Christmas 2022 tourist rush in Manali
claims.notes: Image is from January 2022; originally posted by photographer Yash Handa; shared as current by Times Of India; Hindustan Times; Dainik Bhaskar and TV9 Bharatvarsh.

claims.claim: Photo shows massive traffic jam near Atal Tunnel after Christmas 2022 tourist rush in Manali
claims.notes: Same old January 2022 image misattributed to Christmas 2022 tourist rush near Manali; also carried by Dainik Bhaskar and TV9 Bharatvarsh.

claims.claim: Shahi Imam of Delhi Jama Masjid Ahmad Bukhari has joined the BJP
claims.notes: Video showed Imam sharing stage with BJP MP Harsh Vardhan at a Jama Masjid toilet-restoration event; not a BJP membership event. Note: actor spread a false rumour about a Muslim religious figure; not a BJP-aligned claim.

claims.claim: Girls at a Tamil Nadu tribal school are throwing dupattas from windows in protest against North Indian and patriarchal school uniforms
claims.notes: Students were welcoming author Geetha Ilangovan at their school; no protest took place. Note: Ashok Swain is a liberal academic; included for neutrality per spec.

claims.claim: Photos of an injured woman are of a Hindu communist girl named Ankita Vijay beaten by a Muslim man named Abdul
claims.notes: Woman is Malayalam actress Anicka Vikhraman; injuries inflicted by former partner Anoop Pillai; no Muslim involvement. VHP member Sadhvi Prachi and @shripushpendra1 both spread the false communal claim. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Photos of an injured woman are of a Hindu communist girl named Ankita Vijay beaten by a Muslim man named Abdul
claims.notes: Right-wing media personality amplified the same false communal claim about Malayalam actress Anicka Vikhraman; ex-journalist known for anti-Muslim content. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: A Muslim youth misbehaved with a Hindu traffic policeman in Churu Rajasthan and threatened to bring him to the minister's house
claims.notes: Churu Police and media reports confirmed the accused was Narendra Singh; not a Muslim. BJP leader and former North Delhi Mayor spread the communal false framing.

claims.claim: Video from 2020 shows effigy of PM Modi wrapped in the Indian tricolour being paraded in Punjab; linked to recent Ajnala police station violence in 2023
claims.notes: Video is from December 2020 Lohian Khas Punjab; effigy wrapped in saffron jacket not tricolour; unrelated to February 2023 Ajnala protests. Note: Ashok Swain is a liberal academic; included for neutrality.

claims.claim: Video of passports found discarded on a roadside proves that thousands of illegal Muslims were settled near Bet Dwarka in Gujarat
claims.notes: Video is from March 2018 Sirsa district Haryana; passports belonged to workers defrauded by a defunct visa agency; SHO Om Prakash confirmed no Muslim settler angle. VHP member Sadhvi Prachi spread with inflammatory caption.

claims.claim: Old video shows a disgruntled cinema-goer complaining about Shah Rukh Khan's film Pathaan as the film is a flop
claims.notes: Video is a 2022 Brahmastra film reviewer's clip shared in jest; woman's remarks were not about Pathaan. Sudarshan News journalist shared during right-wing boycott campaign. Tweet had 461500 views.

claims.claim: Photo of an iron pillar in Bharatpur shows the Qutub Minar iron pillar with names of Hindu ancestors of the Mughal emperors
claims.notes: Image is of Loha Stambha of Jawahar Burj in Bharatpur Rajasthan; recently erected by Maharaja Brijendra Singh; names are of Bharatpur rulers not Mughal ancestors.

claims.claim: Clipped video shows Uddhav Thackeray calling Mughal emperor Aurangzeb a son of Mother India and praising him
claims.notes: Full video shows Thackeray quoting BJP supporters' rhetoric sarcastically; clip removed context. Also shared by @PunekarSays; @kalnemibasu and other right-wing handles. (translated from Hindi)

claims.claim: Video of a massive crowd inside a mall in Bihar's Bhagalpur shows audience craze for Shah Rukh Khan's Pathaan
claims.notes: Video is of a July 2022 midnight shopping sale at Lulu Mall Kochi Kerala; unrelated to Pathaan release.

claims.claim: Two videos of violent assaults show Bajrang Dal members attacking people on Sonipat-Panipat highway in Haryana
claims.notes: Both clips are from Punjab: first is personal-enmity assault in Sunam Sangrur; second is gang-related finger-mutilation in Mohali. No Bajrang Dal involvement confirmed by police.

claims.claim: Congress leaders at the Raipur Plenary Session were welcomed with heavy gold chains not garlands
claims.notes: Garlands were handcrafted from bamboo and special leaves by tribal artisans from Bastar; CM Bhupesh Baghel confirmed this publicly. (translated from Hindi)

claims.claim: Video of a man beating a child shows a Muslim man attacking a Hindu child in India
claims.notes: Video is from Pakistan and shows a man beating his own son; no Hindu-Muslim communal dimension exists. (translated from Hindi) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Viral video of a large pile-up on the Yamuna Expressway is a recent incident
claims.notes: Video is from 2017 and recirculates periodically; no specific political actor identified. Date_claimed approximated from fact-check publication.

claims.claim: Photos from PM Modi's 2013 election rally in Jodhpur are from his 2018 BJP pre-poll rally showing massive crowd enthusiasm
claims.notes: Two of three images were from 2013 rally; third was from 2018 Kisan Hunkar Rally by independent MLA Hanuman Beniwal. Bagree's tweet was later deleted.

claims.claim: Photo of a damaged school bus shows Congress supporters targeting innocent school children during Bharat Bandh protests
claims.notes: Bus belonging to Sanskriti school Pune was damaged on September 9 — a day before the bandh — by unrelated miscreants; school management confirmed no political connection.

claims.claim: Rajasthan government's 2021-22 budget allocates Rs 100 crore exclusively to one Muslim dargah in Jodhpur
claims.notes: Rs 100 crore is total budget for Religious Tourist Circuit shared across Hindu; Jain; Sikh and Muslim sites (31 projects across all four faiths). Cropped ad misleadingly used. (translated from Hindi)

claims.claim: Clipped video of Rahul Gandhi proves he plans to redistribute wealth exclusively to Muslim minority communities
claims.impact: Lok Sabha 2024 election campaign
claims.notes: Full Congress Manifesto launch speech included Dalits; Tribals; OBCs and poor general-caste people; references to other communities edited out. Spread multiple times during Lok Sabha 2024 campaign.

claims.claim: Andhra Pradesh NDA government has permanently abolished the Waqf Board
claims.notes: AP government revoked a 2023 GO due to legal challenges and non-functionality; Waqf Board itself was not abolished; government committed to reconstituting it. Post had 270000 views and 2500 reshares.

claims.claim: A newspaper headline shows a Hindu girl was stabbed to death by a Muslim man
claims.notes: Times of India publicly stated the headline was distorted and threatened legal action. Alt News compilation documents multiple serial falsehoods by Bagree since 2016. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Photos show a huge crowd at BJP's Modi rally in Jodhpur on 3 December 2018
claims.notes: The Quint WebQoof reverse image search confirmed two images dated to 2013 rally and third from Kisan Hunkar Rally. Bagree's tweet was deleted. Cross-referenced with Alt News fact-check of same claim.

claims.claim: 14000 kg of dog meat was brought from Jaipur to Bengaluru and sold to restaurants in the city
claims.notes: Karnataka Food Safety Department and Home Minister G Parameshwara confirmed meat was Sirohi-breed goat not dog. False claim originated from cow vigilante Puneet Kerehalli and amplified by MrSinha_ with Muslim communal framing. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Video shows a Muslim maulana posing as a doctor and inappropriately touching female patients while supposedly treating them
claims.notes: Video is a scripted awareness drama produced by Bangladeshi media company Peace Multimedia; title card visible in full video. Post reached 30 lakh views and 3200 reshares; post was not deleted.

claims.claim: Cricketer Rishabh Pant's car accident occurred in a Rohingya-dominated area and locals looted his belongings instead of helping him
claims.notes: Police confirmed Pant was rescued by Haryana Roadways Bus operators who alerted police; saved his life; and returned his valuables; claim of Rohingya looting was entirely false. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Video shows a Muslim cleric spitting on food before serving it to customers
claims.notes: Alt News found the video does not show spitting on food; context was misrepresented by Poonia who has been fact-checked multiple times. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Rajasthan government's 2021-22 budget allocates Rs 100 crore exclusively to one Muslim dargah in Jodhpur
claims.notes: Cropped version of a Congress members' ad used to imply state funds exclusively for one dargah; actual budget covers four religious communities. Also spread by Rishi Bagree. (translated from Hindi)

claims.claim: Rajasthan government allocated Rs 100 crore exclusively for one dargah in the 2021-22 budget
claims.notes: The Quint WebQoof found Rs 100 crore covers Religious Tourism Circuit for Hindu; Jain; Sikh and Muslim sites; not exclusively one dargah. Also independently verified by Alt News and Factly.

claims.claim: Muslim civil service aspirants receive special age relaxations; more exam attempts; lower qualifying marks and exclusive coaching — constituting UPSC Jihad infiltration of Indian bureaucracy
claims.notes: All six checked claims were false; UPSC rules apply by SC/ST/OBC category not religion; IPS Association described broadcast as communal and irresponsible journalism. Supreme Court stayed broadcast. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Clipped video of Rahul Gandhi shows him making the same demand for a union of states as Amritpal Singh
claims.notes: Gandhi spoke about Indian federalism and the constitutional phrase Union of States; entirely different context from Amritpal Singh's separatist demands. (translated from Hindi)

claims.claim: Video shows journalist Abhisar Sharma bribing a village elder with a currency note to speak against PM Modi
claims.notes: Full video shows Sharma handing back a newspaper clipping the elderly man had given him; not cash. Sharma clarified publicly and threatened legal action against spreaders.

claims.claim: Congress 2024 manifesto is a Muslim appeasement document equivalent to a Muslim League manifesto planning to redistribute Hindu wealth
claims.impact: Lok Sabha 2024 election campaign
claims.notes: Congress manifesto contains no mention of wealth redistribution to Muslims; multiple false claims about manifesto amplified by MrSinha_ multiple times during 2024 Lok Sabha campaign.

claims.claim: 95% of street crime is committed by people who are immigrants or of immigrant origin
claims.notes: Fact-checker found the figure unverifiable; it does not appear in any official statistics. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 9 million unemployed, 15 million poor in France
claims.notes: AFP found Le Pen's unemployment and poverty figures were heavily exaggerated versus official INSEE and ILO data. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 52% of rapes committed in Paris in 2014 were committed by foreigners — the study says no such thing
claims.notes: AFP showed Le Pen misrepresented the source study which did not support her claim. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: You acquire French nationality with just five years of discontinuous residence
claims.notes: AFP established the legal requirements for naturalisation are far stricter; the claim is false. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Immigration figures are multiplying each year and borders are a sieve
claims.notes: AFP found Le Pen exaggerated both public concern polling and actual immigration growth statistics. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Violent acts in villages have exploded
claims.notes: AFP found Le Pen used figures from unofficial categories not recognised by the Interior Ministry's official statistics. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Homicides have multiplied by four in fifteen years in France
claims.notes: AFP found the claim is false; homicide statistics do not support a four-fold increase over that period. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Macron doubled coal production / coal-based electricity
claims.notes: AFP found the claim misleading; coal power rose briefly during an energy crisis but was not 'doubled' by policy. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Multiple false or misleading claims during the 2017 presidential debate — including on immigration numbers, Islamist link to Macron, and Swiss agricultural tariffs
claims.impact: High — 2017 presidential run-off debate
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs identified 19 inaccurate or false claims by Le Pen during the between-rounds presidential debate. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: France will share its UN Security Council seat with Germany under the Aix-la-Chapelle Treaty
claims.notes: Treaty text does not provide for any sharing of the Security Council seat; the claim is false. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: A migrant freshly arrived can receive more than a retiree who worked all their life
claims.notes: AFP and Les Décodeurs showed asylum seeker allowance (max ~440 €/month) is well below minimum pension (868 €/month). (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Amedy Coulibaly was given French nationality at 18 — he was in fact born French
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs found this is a persistent false claim by Le Pen; Coulibaly was born on French soil with French nationality. (Translated from French) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — attribution of terrorism/crime to named individual.

claims.claim: Multiple claims in 2022 presidential run-off debate — including on Macron coal policy, immigration, EU, and energy costs
claims.impact: High — 2022 presidential run-off debate
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs fact-checked major claims from both candidates in the 2022 run-off debate; multiple Le Pen claims found false or misleading. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The euro caused a price explosion; Swiss agricultural tariffs prove protectionism works; Islamist connection to Macron
claims.impact: High — 2017 presidential campaign
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs identified multiple false claims in Le Pen's TF1 interview ahead of the presidential run-off. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: All fichés S could be expelled; crime statistics show violence has exploded
claims.impact: High — 2017 first-round debate
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs found the fiché S expulsion claim legally inapplicable, and crime stats claims exaggerated. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: French is the second most spoken language in the world
claims.notes: AFP Factuel / Objectif Désinfox coalition: French is not the second most spoken language globally. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Mali still has an ambassador in France
claims.notes: Mali's ambassador was recalled in 2020; the claim is false. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Slightly torn ballot papers are null votes — proof of electoral fraud
claims.impact: High — post-presidential election
claims.notes: Interior Ministry confirmed slightly torn papers do not automatically constitute null votes; fraud accusation is false. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The RN demanded a recount after the second round of the presidential election
claims.impact: High — post-presidential election
claims.notes: RN communications director confirmed to AFP the party fully accepted the results and made no recount request. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: France has the highest number of Covid deaths in Europe
claims.notes: AFP official tally placed the UK ahead of France and Italy in European Covid death toll at the time of the claim. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Is it normal that a migrant freshly arrived can receive more than a retiree who worked all their life?
claims.notes: AFP showed asylum seeker daily allowance (max 440 €/month) is well below minimum old-age solidarity allowance (868 €/month). (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 40% of the minimum old-age pension is given to foreigners who have never contributed in France
claims.notes: AFP found no national statistics on nationality of all beneficiaries exist; the 40% figure conflates 'born abroad' with 'foreign national'; 90% of recipients have worked and contributed. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Social housing is 'funded by the French' and should be reserved for them
claims.notes: Social housing financing is mixed-source; EU law prohibits nationality-based priority allocation. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: France admits legal immigrants each year equivalent to the population of Lyon
claims.notes: AFP found Bardella's figure overstated actual first-permit issuances and mixed legal categories. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: One gratuitous assault every 44 seconds in France; 120 knife attacks per day
claims.notes: AFP showed Bardella used mismatched data sources and confused victims with incidents to inflate figures. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 93% of thefts in Île-de-France transit are committed by foreigners — decontextualised
claims.notes: AFP found the figure covered a very narrow sub-category; it does not represent overall transit theft. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 330,000 people entered the EU illegally in 2022
claims.notes: Frontex data counted crossings, not individual people; one person can generate multiple crossings. AFP found actual person-based count was closer to 188,000. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Justice is pursuing me to silence me over my Trappes remarks
claims.notes: AFP found Bardella was facing a private defamation claim, not state prosecution; framing of political persecution was misleading. (Translated from French) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active litigation.

claims.claim: Foreign students have a 75% failure rate at French universities and the majority stay in France
claims.notes: AFP found no official 75% failure rate statistic exists; 'majority staying' also unsupported by data. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The overall cost of immigration to France is unsustainably high
claims.notes: AFP found cost-of-immigration figures relied on partial methodologies that excluded fiscal contributions. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 77% of rapes and sexual assaults in Paris are committed by foreigners
claims.impact: High — European Parliament campaign debate
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs found the figure covered only 36 people (street rapes in Paris) vs 55,000+ sexual violence cases nationally; 13% nationally involved foreign suspects. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Abolishing the AME (state medical aid for undocumented migrants) would save 1.6 billion euros; the AME gives free healthcare to all undocumented foreigners
claims.impact: High — 2024 legislative election campaign
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs found the AME is not free healthcare for all undocumented people; the savings figure and scope of entitlement were both incorrect. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Two-thirds of people over 60 in France are on RSA, unemployed or receiving disability pension; pension reform costs the state money
claims.impact: High — 2024 legislative election campaign
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs found the two-thirds figure was unsubstantiated; no study supports the claim that repealing the pension reform saves public money. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Energy bills can be cut 30% immediately by lowering VAT on fuels including petrol and diesel
claims.impact: High — 2024 legislative election campaign
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs found VAT cuts on petrol and diesel would breach EU directives and expose France to penalties; Bardella omitted this. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Sexual violence has exploded due to immigration in France
claims.notes: AFP and criminologists found no evidence sexual violence statistics are driven by immigration. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Two-thirds of 500,000 annual immigrants are Muslim; fertility rates of Maghrebi and African women are 3–4 children per woman while France average is 1.68
claims.notes: AFP found the Muslim-share figure unsupported; fertility rates for immigrant women are substantially lower than claimed. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The immigrant population makes more and more children — the curves of native and immigrant fertility are crossing
claims.notes: AFP and INSEE showed the fertility curves have not crossed; immigrant women's fertility is declining, not rising. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 80 to 90% of under-18s in the Paris suburbs are of Maghrebi or African origin
claims.notes: Objectif Désinfox coalition: figure not corroborated by any official census data. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 22% of newborns in France have Muslim first names according to INSEE
claims.notes: INSEE does not publish religious or ethnic breakdowns of first names; the figure is fabricated. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Pétain protected the Jews of France during the Occupation
claims.notes: AFP Factuel confirmed Zemmour's recurring claim has been consistently refuted by historians. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Many large companies have fled Saint-Denis because of insecurity
claims.notes: Claim was not corroborated by any business data; companies contacted denied it. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: De Gaulle gave Algeria independence to prevent mass immigration to France
claims.notes: Historians dismissed this as an unsupported extrapolation from declassified documents. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: French is the second most spoken language in the world
claims.notes: French ranks lower; the claim has been made repeatedly by multiple RN/Reconquête figures. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Twelve French cities are among the fifteen most criminal in Europe
claims.notes: AFP found the ranking relies on subjective user reviews on a website, not official crime data; multiple researchers called it unreliable. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: France Télévisions spent 46 million euros on reception costs in 2024 — 126,000 euros per day
claims.notes: The 46 million figure covers all travel, mission and reception costs combined; reception costs alone were 3.2 million euros. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Foreign students have a 75% failure rate at French universities; the majority stay in France
claims.notes: No official 75% failure-rate figure exists in ministry data; the majority-stay claim is also unsupported. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Only 2% of hospitalisations in 2020 were Covid — hospitals were not overwhelmed
claims.notes: AFP and hospital federations explained the 2% is a national annual average masking severe regional and temporal peaks; the figure was selectively extracted from a 26-page report. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 92% of healthcare workers have not had their Covid booster dose
claims.notes: Only 8% had received the Omicron-adapted booster (4th dose); most had received earlier boosters. Philippot conflated non-uptake of specific variant booster with general non-vaccination. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: A vaccine side effects study proves a link between Covid vaccines and deaths
claims.notes: AFP found the study cited showed pharmacovigilance signals, not causal proof of vaccine-linked deaths. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The state will take 500 euros from every French person's savings to fund Ukraine
claims.notes: No such proposal existed; the claim was based on a misreading or distortion of a French government discussion on unused accounts. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The Munich Security Conference chairman cried from frustration at J.D. Vance's speech — edited video
claims.notes: AFP found the video was selectively edited; the chairman's demeanour did not constitute crying in the full footage. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Fake photo showing Philippot posing with migrants in Spain was shared as real
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the photo was doctored; Philippot was not in Spain at the time. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Vaccines have killed approximately 1,000 people in France — pharmacovigilance reports prove it
claims.notes: AFP found Collard misread pharmacovigilance reports; signals are not confirmed causal deaths; full context was omitted. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: AI-generated photos of an X/Twitter office raid circulate as real
claims.notes: AFP confirmed images were AI-generated and did not show any real event. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Nationality and residence permits are given automatically in France; there are no more expulsions
claims.notes: AFP established permits are never automatic; 15,950 expulsions were carried out in 2020 according to Interior Ministry. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Eleven or twelve French cities are among the 15 most criminal cities in Europe
claims.notes: AFP found the ranking used subjective user reviews, not official statistics; multiple criminologists dismissed it as unreliable. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 2024 European election: bulletins for certain lists were missing from polling stations — proof of fraud
claims.notes: AFP and electoral law specialists confirmed parties are responsible for supplying their own ballots; absence is not fraud. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: An infographic claiming NFP received half as many votes as RN in the second round of 2024 legislative elections
claims.notes: AFP found the figures in the viral infographic did not match official Interior Ministry results. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Votes disappeared or were not counted for Marine Le Pen on election night 2022
claims.notes: AFP found no evidence of vote manipulation; Conseil constitutionnel validated results. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 2017 presidential first round was rigged — partial results table showed Macron at 1.59% before jumping to 24%
claims.notes: AFP and Interior Ministry confirmed the table is fabricated and does not come from official sources; AFP archive footage from 2017 shows Macron leading throughout. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 500,000 immigrants per year arrive in France — figures used to support 'submersion' narrative
claims.notes: AFP and researchers warned the figure and framing relied on methodology from a partisan think tank (OID) that researchers called systematically biased. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: There is a project to place wind turbines off every coast except in front of Le Touquet
claims.notes: AFP found this claim about coastal wind-farm planning was inaccurate and misleading. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The Dominion company manages French election vote-counting; the 2022 presidential election was rigged by electronic voting machines
claims.impact: High — pre-election period
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs confirmed Dominion has no contract with the French state; the sole voting machine model used is French-certified. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Pétain protected the Jews of France — historical revisionism defended repeatedly
claims.notes: AFP Factuel detailed how the claim contradicts documented Vichy collaboration in deportations. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 400,000 people in France present the same profile as the Rambouillet terrorist — potential terrorists
claims.notes: Note: Attributed to multiple figures including Valeurs actuelles article. AFP found the researcher whose work was cited publicly denounced the 'manipulation'; only ~10,000 jihadist profiles existed. (Translated from French) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — attribution of terrorism risk to population group.

claims.claim: The Constitutional Council cannot block a referendum that bypasses Parliament on 'national preference'
claims.notes: Constitutional lawyers told AFP this interpretation is contested and highly likely unconstitutional. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The Constitution can be revised by referendum without going through Parliament
claims.notes: Constitutional scholars confirmed this is impossible under current constitutional arrangements. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The word 'Joyeux Noël' was banned by the ecologist mayor of Besançon
claims.notes: AFP confirmed no such ban existed; the claim originated from a misrepresentation of an internal council communication. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 96% of unaccompanied minor migrants intercepted in Paris are actually adults — headline used as amplifier of misleading statistic
claims.notes: AFP found the 96% figure applied only to the subset of cases where identity was formally verified by three specific countries; it does not represent all unaccompanied minors in Paris. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Biden 'boasted of having set up the most vast and inclusive electoral fraud organisation in history'
claims.notes: AFP found the headline and article amplified a truncated video; Biden was discussing a get-out-the-vote helpline, not admitting fraud. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: School-age EVARS sex-education guide contains explicit sexual definitions intended for primary school children
claims.notes: AFP found the definitions read on air were from a teen-targeted website, not the primary-school EVARS resource. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The May 2026 heatwave is not exceptional — Pascal Praud dismisses climate scientists' warnings as manipulation
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the heatwave was statistically unprecedented; scientists publicly rejected Praud's claims as discrediting scientific consensus. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Raoult on CNews: polio vaccine causes 30 times more polio than the natural virus — context omitted
claims.notes: AFP found the statistic is technically true but misleadingly implies vaccine-derived cases are a new burden, when in fact wild polio has fallen 99.99% and derived cases are a known managed phenomenon. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 40% of minimum pension (minimum vieillesse) goes to foreigners who have never contributed
claims.notes: Multiple RN figures incl. Laurent Jacobelli and Le Pen repeated this; AFP found no national-level nationality data on all beneficiaries; the 40% figure confuses 'born abroad' with 'foreign national'; 90% of recipients have contributed. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Macron arrived at 100% of electronic voting machines in 2022 — Le Pen was beaten by machines
claims.notes: AFP found Le Pen won in 9 of the 63 communes with electronic voting; Macron did not sweep 100% of machine votes. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Maghrebi and African immigrant women have fertility rates of 3–4 children compared to France's 1.68 average — great replacement is underway
claims.notes: INSEE showed immigrant women's fertility has declined; differential with non-immigrants is shrinking; Zemmour's figures were inflated. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Slightly torn ballot papers are null votes and indicate organised electoral fraud in the 2022 elections
claims.notes: Interior Ministry confirmed torn ballots were not automatically null; the fraud narrative was false. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 500 parrainages signatures for presidential candidates are required by the Constitution
claims.notes: The 500-signature requirement is set in law, not the Constitution. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The Rassemblement National is the 'first party of France' after the 2022 legislative first round
claims.notes: AFP noted that while RN had the highest single-party vote, the left coalition NUPES received more combined votes. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Yaël Braun-Pivet was elected Assemblée nationale president 'thanks to the RN'
claims.notes: AFP found RN withdrew its candidate enabling Braun-Pivet's election at second round; a third round without RN would also have confirmed her given coalition maths. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 2 million Le Pen votes disappeared on election night 2022 according to results dashboards
claims.notes: AFP confirmed final Conseil constitutionnel figures were consistent throughout; no votes disappeared. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The RN is the only political movement that is growing
claims.notes: AFP noted the left coalition NUPES also grew significantly; RN's growth claim ignores combined left performance. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Valeurs actuelles video amplified as showing a Génération Identitaire protester giving a Nazi salute
claims.notes: AFP found the clip was a freeze-frame misrepresentation; the full video showed no Nazi salute. This check also implicates Valeurs actuelles as the media origin. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Violence statistics prove violent acts have exploded in villages — the figures are from official sources
claims.notes: Interior Ministry told AFP the category 'violent acts on public roads' used by Le Pen does not exist in official statistics. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 500,000 immigrants arrive in France every year — supporting the 'submersion' narrative
claims.notes: Same AFP June 2026 report: researchers identified Bardella and other RN figures using biased OID think-tank methodology to inflate immigration figures. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: There is a classement of the most criminal European cities showing French cities at the top
claims.notes: Ranking is based on non-representative subjective user reviews; AFP and criminologists rejected it as unreliable. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: My court case is political, not judicial — the conviction is motivated by the desire to eliminate me from politics
claims.notes: AFP provided context that conviction in the Parlement européen assistants case followed 9 years of independent judicial investigation. (Translated from French) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active court proceedings.

claims.claim: Many specialist institutions for disabled children have been closed
claims.notes: Claim not corroborated by official data on specialist facility numbers. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Marine Le Pen was always pro-nuclear — denying her past opposition to nuclear energy
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs confirmed Le Pen expressed opposition to nuclear for safety reasons during the 2022 campaign; Bardella's denial is misleading. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: RN members claim minimum vieillesse is being given to foreigners who never contributed — repeating the 40% statistic across multiple appearances
claims.notes: AFP identified the claim was repeated by Laurent Jacobelli, Marine Le Pen, and others. The 40% figure was based on a misread Cnav study covering 'born abroad' not 'foreign nationals'. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Abrogating the pension reform would have a positive economic effect on public finances
claims.notes: Les Décodeurs found no study supports net savings from returning to 62 retirement age; only cuts to the social volet could generate savings. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Marion Maréchal used OID immigration statistics acknowledged to be based on flawed methodology
claims.notes: AFP June 2026 report named Maréchal as a figure who publicly acknowledged using OID data that researchers found systematically biased. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: RN demanded a vote recount after the 2022 presidential second round
claims.notes: RN publicly stated to AFP it fully accepted the results; French law does not permit ordinary recounts. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Justice is pursuing Bardella for denouncing Islamism in Trappes — to silence him politically
claims.notes: AFP found the case was a private defamation action, not state prosecution; characterisation as political persecution was misleading. (Translated from French) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active litigation.

claims.claim: 77% of rapes and sexual assaults committed in Paris are by foreigners — Attal/Bardella EU debate
claims.impact: High — 2024 European Parliament election campaign
claims.notes: AFP found the 77% figure covers only 36 people intercepted for street rapes in Paris — out of 55,000+ national sexual violence cases; 13% of nationally elucidated sex crimes nationally involved foreign suspects. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 75% of beef served in school canteens across France is imported
claims.impact: High — 2024 European Parliament election campaign
claims.notes: Agriculture minister and industry body Interbev told AFP the claim is false; most school canteen beef is French-sourced. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Greenhouse gas emissions in France have not fallen under Macron's government
claims.impact: High — 2024 European Parliament election campaign
claims.notes: AFP found Citepa data shows a 5.8% fall in emissions in 2023; the claim contradicts official figures. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The net cost of immigration to French public finances is 40 billion euros per year
claims.notes: The economists whose studies were cited told AFP the 40 billion figure does not come from their work; they described it as a 'fallacious interpretation'; actual estimates are below 0.5% of GDP. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Student visas are the first pathway for immigration into France
claims.notes: AFP found while student visas are the largest single permit category since 2022, the framing misleadingly implies most student permit holders become permanent immigrants. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: A 'first militant campaign' for Chirac in 1995 is part of Zemmour's biography — implies mainstream conservatism
claims.notes: AFP found Zemmour's claimed Chirac-RPR connection in 1995 was unverifiable; his narrative sought to position Reconquête as natural heir to the right. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Marine Le Pen was never against nuclear energy — it is false to say she opposed it
claims.notes: AFP confirmed Le Pen expressed opposition to nuclear on safety grounds during the 2022 presidential campaign; Bardella's denial is false. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Viral infographic claims Le Pen won on all electronic voting machines in 2022 — Macron only won due to machine fraud
claims.notes: AFP found Le Pen won 9 of 63 machine-vote communes; no evidence of fraud confirmed by Conseil constitutionnel. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Votes disappeared for Marine Le Pen on election night 2022 according to broadcast dashboards
claims.notes: AFP found the dashboards showed incremental counting updates not disappearing votes; final Conseil constitutionnel totals were consistent throughout. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Jews of France were protected by Pétain — Vichy saved more Jews than it deported
claims.notes: AFP Factuel confirmed historians universally reject this claim; Vichy actively participated in roundups and deportations including of French Jewish citizens. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Frontex counted 330,000 people entering the EU illegally in 2022
claims.notes: AFP confirmed with Frontex that the 330,000 figure counts crossings, not individuals; OIM counted 187,993 actual persons. Bardella made no distinction. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Morocco, Tunisia and Egypt are being integrated into the Erasmus+ programme — harming France
claims.notes: AFP found Bardella's claim about Erasmus+ expansion to North Africa misrepresented the programme's scope and existing partnership frameworks. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Foreign students from Morocco, Tunisia and Egypt impose a 1 billion euro cost on France and mostly remain
claims.notes: AFP found the 1 billion figure from the Cour des comptes excludes the economic contributions of foreign graduates; the 'majority stay' claim is not supported by DGEF data. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: France's 2017 presidential election was rigged — partial results showed Macron at 1.59% before jumping to 24%
claims.notes: AFP and Interior Ministry confirmed the table is of unknown provenance; AFP archive footage from election night shows Macron in clear lead throughout. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: 400,000 people in France are potential terrorists like the Rambouillet attacker — citing misrepresented researcher
claims.notes: Valeurs actuelles article by Philippe de Villiers cited researcher Hakim El Karoui who publicly denounced a 'manipulation' of his work; the 300–400k group are not jihadists. (Translated from French) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — attribution of terrorism risk to named population.

claims.claim: 95% of street crime (délinquance de rue) is attributable to immigrants or people of immigrant origin — repeated in multiple appearances
claims.notes: AFP found the category 'délinquance de rue' does not exist in official statistics; the Interior Ministry's own statistics service confirmed no such measurement exists. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Homicides in France have multiplied by four over fifteen years
claims.notes: AFP found homicide statistics from SSMSI do not support a four-fold increase; the claim uses selective and inconsistent data. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: There are no more expulsions in France — foreigners in irregular situations are never removed
claims.notes: AFP confirmed 15,950 expulsions were carried out in 2020; the decline was due to Covid-related travel disruption, not policy failure. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The cost of immigration to France is 40 billion euros net per year — cited in European election context
claims.notes: Same AFP check: CEPII and OCDE economists explicitly told AFP the 40 billion figure is a 'fallacious interpretation' of their research; the actual fiscal impact is far smaller. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Many specialist institutions for disabled children have been closed by successive governments
claims.notes: AFP found the claim is not corroborated by official data on specialist institutions. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: There is a classement showing France has the most criminal cities in Europe
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the ranking is based solely on subjective user opinions on Numbeo, not official crime data; criminologists called it unreliable. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Immigration statistics produced by the OID institute are reliable and show France is being 'submerged' by immigration
claims.notes: AFP and researchers found the OID (funded by ultra-conservative donor Pierre-Edouard Stérin) uses systematically biased methodology; Bardella is one of the main amplifiers of its outputs. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: There has been an explosion of violent acts in villages — backed by official statistics
claims.notes: AFP showed Le Pen confused 'small towns' with 'villages' and used a data category the Interior Ministry says does not exist in official crime recording systems. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: There is one gratuitous (unprovoked) assault every 44 seconds in France
claims.notes: AFP found Bardella misinterpreted national statistics by conflating different offence categories and conflating victims with incidents. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Sex education definitions in the EVARS primary school guide include explicit adult content — broadcast on air
claims.notes: AFP confirmed definitions were from a teen-focused website not the primary-school EVARS document; the error was amplified by the CNews broadcast without correction. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: A new study proves Covid vaccines are causally linked to excess deaths in France
claims.notes: AFP consulted pharmacologists who confirmed the pharmacovigilance study shows signals, not causal proof; Philippot's presentation omitted all caveats. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The Covid hospitalisation statistics published by the ATIH prove hospitals were never overwhelmed — only 2% of all hospitalisations were Covid
claims.notes: AFP and two hospital federations explained the 2% is a smoothed annual national average masking acute regional peaks; the actual ICU Covid burden peaked at 11% nationally. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Only 8% of French healthcare workers have had any Covid booster — showing massive vaccine rejection
claims.notes: The 8% referred to uptake of the Omicron-specific 4th dose only; the vast majority of healthcare workers had received earlier boosters. AFP confirmed Philippot conflated the categories. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: France Télévisions spends 126,000 euros per day on 'réception' costs — used to demand privatisation
claims.notes: AFP found the per-day figure was calculated from the 46 million total covering all travel/mission/reception; actual reception costs were 3.2 million, giving about 8,770 per day — 15 times less. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The French state plans to seize 500 euros from every French person's savings account to finance Ukraine
claims.notes: AFP found no such proposal existed in any government document; the claim misrepresents a discussion about dormant accounts. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Munich Security Conference chairman Christoph Heusgen broke down in tears of frustration at J.D. Vance's speech — viral edited clip
claims.notes: AFP found the video was selectively cut to suggest a breakdown; in full context no tears were visible. Philippot shared it with an inflammatory caption. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: AI-generated images of a police or government raid on X/Twitter headquarters shared as if real
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the images were AI-generated and corresponded to no real event. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Pharmacovigilance reports show Covid vaccines have killed around 1,000 people in France
claims.notes: AFP found pharmacovigilance signals are not confirmed causal deaths; the reports include unverified correlations and the methodology was misrepresented. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: The cost of foreign students to France is 1 billion euros — a net loss
claims.notes: AFP found Bardella and Maréchal cited the Cour des comptes gross cost figure without the economic benefits identified in the same report; the net assessment is far more nuanced. (Translated from French)

claims.claim: Germany has the highest taxes of all OECD countries. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Weidel claimed Germany has the highest taxes of all OECD countries during live X conversation with Elon Musk. OECD data shows Germany is not in first place across any of the measured household types.

claims.claim: [The state] doesn't provide for security, so we have skyrocketing criminal rates here. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The claim of 'exploding' crime rates is a recurring AfD narrative. Official PKS statistics show crime figures fluctuated but did not 'skyrocket'. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: claim attributes general threat to state negligence.

claims.claim: Germany is the only industrial country that unplugged the nuclear power plant. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Italy shut down all nuclear plants by 1990 following Chernobyl referendum. Multiple EU states including Denmark, Estonia, Greece, Ireland, Luxembourg also operate without nuclear power.

claims.claim: Nuclear energy is carbon free. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Life-cycle emissions from uranium mining, fuel processing and waste storage mean nuclear power is not CO2-free. IPCC estimates 4–100 g CO2-equivalent per kWh over the full lifecycle.

claims.claim: He [Hitler] was a communist and he considered himself as a socialist. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Widespread coverage during Bundestagswahl campaign
claims.notes: Weidel's claim that Hitler was a communist contradicts all historical consensus; Hitler explicitly stated NSDAP was not socialist in 1928 and communists/social democrats were among his first victims. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: historical relativisation of Nazism.

claims.claim: 57%, almost 60% of the people coming into our country through an open border, they throw away their papers. (translated from German)
claims.notes: 52% of first-time applicants Jan–Oct 2024 could not present ID documents, but this does not mean they deliberately destroyed papers. Many reasons include documents seized by smugglers, stateless individuals, war destruction.

claims.claim: [Germany] switched off the last nuclear power plant [after energy prices skyrocketed]. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The nuclear phase-out was decided in 2002 and reaffirmed in 2011; the final April 2023 shutdown was a months-long postponement, not a crisis decision. Prices had already fallen by shutdown date.

claims.claim: He [Hitler] was not conservative. He was this socialist-communist type. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Broad media coverage during election campaign
claims.notes: Historian Werner Suppanz (Uni Graz): 'Eindeutig nicht [socialist].' Hitler declared NSDAP was not socialist in 1928; communists and social democrats were systematically persecuted after 1933. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Angela Merkel opened the German borders for illegal immigration in 2015. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Borders were not closed in 2015; stationary border controls in the Schengen area had long been abolished. ARD legal correspondent Kolja Schwartz: 'grundfalsch, weil es schon seit Jahren keine geschlossenen Grenzen mehr gibt.'

claims.claim: 150 EU bureaucrats are listening to this conversation to enforce the Digital Services Act – it is pure censorship. (translated from German)
claims.notes: EU Commission spokesperson Thomas Regnier: 150 staff work across all DSA enforcement, not specifically monitoring this livestream. Only 2–3 were assigned to it. The DSA targets illegal content removal, not censorship.

claims.claim: Germany is the only industrial country that unplugged its nuclear power plants. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Italy shut down all nuclear plants by 1990; Belgium and Spain also plan phase-outs. Multiple EU countries have never operated nuclear plants.

claims.claim: Germany has the highest energy prices worldwide. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Global Petrol Prices data: Germany ranks 15th for industrial electricity (June 2024), 3rd for households; 13th–14th for gas. Verivox analysis of 147 countries: Germany ranks 9th on electricity, 21st when adjusted for purchasing power.

claims.claim: Atomic power is CO2-neutral. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Federal Ministry for Environment confirms nuclear power is 'weder CO2-frei noch die CO2-ärmste Art der Energieerzeugung'. Full lifecycle emissions from uranium mining, fuel processing and waste storage are significant.

claims.claim: In Hessen the beautiful Reinhardswald with tree stands over 200 years old is simply being felled to build wind turbines. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Only ~0.15% of the 20,000 ha Reinhardswald forest was approved for 18 turbines. The area is not an ancient primeval forest in the sense Weidel implied. The claim exaggerates the scope of clearing.

claims.claim: Germany is a net importer of French nuclear power and has the highest energy prices worldwide. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Germany imported ~16 TWh from France in 2024 but also exported ~3 TWh. On energy prices: Germany is not globally highest across all categories.

claims.claim: The CO2 footprint of nuclear power plants is zero. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Umweltbundesamt 2025: nuclear lifecycle emissions ~55 g CO2-equivalent/kWh. IPCC range 4–100 g. Not zero.

claims.claim: We have for example 215,000 Syrians who are under an obligation to leave the country – that is the size of a major city. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Direct impact on Bundestagswahl campaign narratives
claims.notes: BAMF data: only ~11,000 Syrian nationals were under obligation to leave as of 30 June 2025; of those, 9,997 held a Duldung (suspension of deportation). Total ausreisepflichtig persons were 226,506 but not exclusively Syrians.

claims.claim: The IPCC has not proven that climate change is man-made – there is no mono-causal proof. (translated from German)
claims.notes: IPCC Sixth Assessment Report states unequivocally: 'Human activities have warmed the atmosphere, ocean and land.' Scientific consensus on anthropogenic climate change is overwhelming.

claims.claim: Half of Bürgergeld recipients are foreigners. They have never paid into this social system. And the other half, three quarters of them have a dual passport – they have a migration background. (translated from German)
claims.notes: ~47–48% of Bürgergeld recipients are foreign nationals, not 'half'; the 'three-quarters dual passport' claim cannot be verified as the Bundesagentur für Arbeit does not record dual citizenship. Many foreign recipients have previously paid into the system.

claims.claim: Remigration is simply compliance with law and order; under our asylum law, Article 18, anyone who comes to us from a safe third country must be turned back. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Legal expert Anuscheh Farahat: the provision is 'dead law' superseded by EU Dublin-III Regulation. Administrative Court Berlin confirmed Germany cannot turn back asylum seekers at the border if the Dublin procedure applies.

claims.claim: We are the only [AfD] fraction that is transparent [regarding executive pay]… the salary of the executives was raised to the usual level of other fractions. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The AfD pay increases were not announced by the party itself but revealed by T-Online. AfD supplement rates were in some cases above those of other parliamentary groups. AfD's claim of being uniquely transparent is false.

claims.claim: When citizens hoist German flags, the domestic intelligence service [Staatsschutz] investigates. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Police spokesperson in relevant district confirmed no investigation into flag-hoisting incidents. Displaying the German national flag is explicitly legal. Weidel's claim derived from a Swiss outlet ('Die Weltwoche') which was itself incorrect.

claims.claim: When we are in power, we will tear down all wind power plants. Down with these windmills of shame! (translated from German)
claims.notes: Wind power supplies ~31% of German electricity. Tearing down all turbines would cause blackouts and massively raise prices. Weidel later retracted, claiming she meant only turbines in Reinhardswald – but no turbines exist there.

claims.claim: People are being killed on the streets every day in Germany. (translated from German)
claims.notes: PKS 2023: 214 solved murders and 342 manslaughters for the whole year – not daily. Most occur between known parties, not as random street crime.

claims.claim: [Data in circulating graphics show] Ukrainians receive 59% of Bürgergeld, Germans only 5.3%. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The figures represent proportion of each nationality receiving Bürgergeld, not share of total spend. More than half of all Bürgergeld recipients (~2.8 million) are German nationals.

claims.claim: Not a single penny from the Sondervermögen [special fund] has been invested in the Bundeswehr so far. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The Bundeswehr procurement office confirmed the €100 billion Sondervermögen is 'practically fully committed' in contracts. ~87% bound, €13 billion remaining under further contracts planned.

claims.claim: A video of Tino Chrupalla exposed massive electoral fraud involving postal votes and care homes [in the 2026 Baden-Württemberg state election]. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The clip dated September 2024 at Saxony/Thuringia election press conference, not the 2026 Baden-Württemberg election. Chrupalla made general unproven allegations about ballot security; he presented no evidence of fraud.

claims.claim: The 2012 risk analysis of a coronavirus pandemic was hidden from the Bundestag and there is no trace of it in the Bundestag archives. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The report (Drucksache 17/12051, 2013) is publicly available in the Bundestag archive. It was not a prediction or cover-up, but a hypothetical scenario analysis. Gauland correctly cited figures but wrongly implied they exactly matched COVID-19 lethality.

claims.claim: A photo of the anti-AfD demonstration in Hamburg is a Photoshop lie – hundreds of demonstrators were placed 'on' the Alster lake. (translated from German)
claims.notes: dpa confirmed the photo was not manipulated. Fraunhofer Institute expert: the apparent anomaly is explained purely by perspective/viewing angle of the photo.

claims.claim: The AfD as the strongest party has a right to the post of Thuringian parliament president. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The constitution grants the strongest faction only the right to nominate a candidate, not to have that candidate elected. The election remains a free vote of all deputies. Thuringia Constitutional Court confirmed this interpretation.

claims.claim: The results of the Thuringia state election were manipulated. (translated from German)
claims.impact: LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: ongoing legal implications at time
claims.notes: Höcke's claims of manipulation were repeatedly debunked by electoral authorities. No evidence of systemic fraud was found. The narrative echoes broader election-denial disinformation.

claims.claim: When we have governmental responsibility in Germany, we will dismantle all the wind industry installations in Germany, completely. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Wind power is Germany's largest electricity source. Full dismantlement would require mass expropriations, cause blackouts and dramatically raise prices — the opposite of AfD's stated goals on energy security.

claims.claim: Murders and rapes are increasing in Germany. The decrease in overall crime is only due to fewer bicycle thefts – saying Germany is safer is fake news. (translated from German)
claims.notes: PKS 2017: overall crime at lowest since 1992 (5.76M cases). Violent crime fell 2.4%. Murders and sexual crimes figures do not support von Storch's claim of general escalation. Interior Minister Seehofer had explicitly stated 'Germany has become safer'.

claims.claim: A wind turbine is economically most useful when it is not turning. All wind turbines must go to get reliable, cheap energy prices again. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Prof. Katrin Schaber (Hochschule Biberach): 'The statement is false, it is exactly the opposite.' When large amounts of wind/solar feed into the grid, electricity prices fall.

claims.claim: 102 Germans were murdered by immigrants in 2018 – an increase of 685 per cent over 2017 (13). (translated from German)
claims.notes: AfD omitted BKA footnote: 75 wounded survivors of the December 2016 Breitscheidplatz terror attack were recorded in 2018 statistics as 'completed homicide victims' due to registration modalities. They are alive. The 685% figure is therefore fabricated.

claims.claim: Man-made climate change cannot yet be scientifically proven. Climate change has always existed and always will. (translated from German)
claims.notes: 99.94% of peer-reviewed climate scientists agree on anthropogenic climate change per 2016 meta-study. The Federal Government confirmed this consensus in a parliamentary response. IPCC reports are unambiguous.
