claims.claim: For years the number of violent offences has been rising – especially from children under 14 – frequently with a migration background. (translated from German)
claims.notes: PKS data shows the proportion of suspect children in total suspects remains roughly stable. Experts confirm migration background itself is not a causal factor; socioeconomic circumstances (poverty, education) are the leading determinants.

claims.claim: A Bild newspaper article reports that AfD politician Maximilian Krah owes more than €80,000 in child support. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Bild confirmed it published no such article. The post circulated with a forged Bild header. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: false attribution to named individual and media outlet.

claims.claim: Over a million Ukrainians in Germany remain in Bürgergeld – 'almost all Ukrainians in Germany stay in Bürgergeld'. (translated from German)
claims.notes: 672,511 Ukrainians (of 1.27 million in Germany) received Bürgergeld – approximately half, not 'almost all'. Münzenmaier did not respond to Correctiv's request for comment.

claims.claim: Ukrainian protected persons may travel abroad for up to six months without their protection status lapsing – meaning they can draw welfare while living elsewhere. (translated from German)
claims.notes: While status does not lapse after 6 months abroad, Bürgergeld is paid for only up to 3 weeks of absence. BMAS confirmed this rule applies to all recipients, not only Ukrainians.

claims.claim: Ukrainian refugees in Germany can retire up to ten years earlier than Germans, without having made contributions. (translated from German)
claims.notes: BMAS confirmed: 'There is no separate retirement age for Ukrainians living in Germany and none is planned.' Pension rights require prior contributions under standard German law.

claims.claim: The German government drew up a coronavirus pandemic crisis scenario in 2012 and there is no trace of it in the Bundestag archives – suggesting a cover-up. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Drucksache 17/12051 is publicly available in Bundestag archive. Report was a hypothetical scenario, not a suppressed plan. Video spread more than 16,300 times on Facebook.

claims.claim: Videos on TikTok show large pro-AfD demonstrations. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Multiple clips were actually footage from Coldplay concerts and anti-far-right protests. Audio of football fan chants was overlaid post-production. AFP confirmed the demonstrations shown were anti-extremism rallies.

claims.claim: A video shows ballots in Leipzig electoral district 152 without AfD entries – evidence of election fraud. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Leipzig city spokesman, LKA Saxony and public prosecutor confirmed the ballots were fabricated. The numbered rows in video begin at '2', not '1' as required by law; AfD appears in row 1 on all official sample ballots.

claims.claim: A video shows AfD postal ballots being opened and destroyed in Hamburg. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Electoral authorities confirmed the footage showed forged materials. Even the AfD candidate called the video a 'Fake' after inspection.

claims.claim: The Bundesrat voted for the AfD [in December 2025]. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Bundesrat spokesperson: 'The statement is obviously false.' The Bundesrat elects no parties, only persons for specific offices. No such vote took place. Basic Law Article 51 confirms the AfD has no Bundesrat representation.

claims.claim: Alice Weidel praised China in a viral video, saying China is the only country that can force the US to the negotiating table. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Reverse video search shows the clip is from an August 2024 AfD rally in Thuringia. In the relevant section Weidel discussed Afghan deportations, not China. The claim was fabricated in Chinese-language social media posts.

claims.claim: A TikTok table shows the electoral positions of all parties; the AfD's positions are correctly represented. [Weidel and other AfD politicians shared the table, which attributed false positions to CDU, SPD, Greens, FDP and Linke] (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundestagswahl 2025 campaign impact
claims.notes: In more than half of categories, false positions were attributed to CDU, SPD, Greens, FDP and Linke. Only AfD positions were broadly accurate. AfD-affiliated politicians including Elena Roon spread the misinformation.

claims.claim: In Thuringia polling, the AfD is currently at 41 per cent. (translated from German)
claims.notes: At the time of the claim Thuringia polls showed AfD at 30%, not 41%. The figure was inflated. AfD subsequently won 32.8% in the September 2024 election.

claims.claim: AI-generated female influencer profiles on TikTok and X represent genuine German women who support the AfD. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundestagswahl 2025 campaign impact
claims.notes: Multiple accounts posing as young female AfD voters were confirmed AI-generated through technical analysis of lip-sync, hair artefacts, and robotic motion. AfD and affiliated agencies known to use such tactics.

claims.claim: A tagesschau video shows the Bundeswehr rehearsing the inauguration ceremony for Alice Weidel as new Federal Chancellor. (translated from German)
claims.notes: tagesschau editor-in-chief Marcus Bornheim confirmed the video is AI-generated and not a real tagesschau broadcast. Reverse image search showed original footage from June 2024 'tagesschau in einfacher Sprache' episode.

claims.claim: Alles für Deutschland [SA slogan used at rallies to incite audience repetition] (translated from German)
claims.impact: LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED
claims.notes: BGH confirmed two Landgericht Halle convictions from May and July 2024 (fines of €13,000 and €16,900) for deliberately using a banned SA-era slogan. Höcke claimed ignorance of the phrase's Nazi origin. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: The photo of the Hamburg anti-AfD demonstration was digitally manipulated to make the crowd appear larger. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Fraunhofer Institute media forensics expert confirmed no manipulation; visual discrepancy is purely due to viewing angle and perspective. dpa publicly denied any manipulation of its photo.

claims.claim: Knife crime has risen over the past ten years and we have an imported migrant problem. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Knife crime has only been uniformly recorded in PKS since 1 January 2024; historical comparisons are methodologically invalid. PKS does not record migration background. Criminological research identifies socioeconomic factors, not migration, as primary determinants.

claims.claim: The demonstration against me during the interview was financed with taxpayers' money via NGOs. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Organisers (Zentrum für Politische Schönheit) confirmed no state funding. The demonstration was unregistered, funded by donations. Police spokesperson confirmed no advance state coordination.

claims.claim: Merkel has broken all her election promises – he [Merz] is a Lügenkanzler [liar-chancellor]. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv found the assessment of broken promises premature – several Merz pledges on migration and the Gebäudeenergiegesetz were still in process. The 'all promises broken' framing was unsubstantiated at time of claim.

claims.claim: In Germany we had an influx of almost 7 million people – those are the officially counted ones. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Federal Statistical Office confirms ~7 million total migration inflows since 2015, but Weidel conflated legal skilled-worker migration with asylum seekers, creating a misleadingly homogenous picture of undocumented mass arrival.

claims.claim: Atomic power can be restarted quickly and cheaply, and returning to nuclear would lower energy prices. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Fraunhofer Institute: nuclear restart would not lower prices – it is a 'weit verbreiteter Irrglaube'. Operators (EnBW CEO Michels) stated dismantling is 'irreversibel'. Regulatory, staffing and fuel procurement timelines mean 1–5+ years minimum.

claims.claim: A court has ruled that the Ukraine war is over. (translated from German)
claims.notes: No court ruling to this effect exists. No credible source cited. Correctiv found no evidence of any court decision regarding the end of the Ukraine war.

claims.claim: Germany was 'begging' for French nuclear power in summer 2022 – implying catastrophic dependence after nuclear phase-out. (translated from German)
claims.notes: In summer 2022 Germany was actually a net exporter of electricity, including to France, which was struggling with reactor maintenance issues. The claim reverses the actual flow.

claims.claim: Electricity from wind is unstable 'Flatterstrom' that cannot work as a reliable baseload. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Bundesnetzagentur confirms grid security is guaranteed until 2031 despite rising renewables. Renewables actually increase grid resilience by reducing fossil fuel import dependency. December 2024 Dunkelflaute was caused by market conduct, not structural insecurity.

claims.claim: You need 1,300 wind turbines to replace a single nuclear power plant. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Weidel used minimum-rated outdated turbines. With modern 6 MW land turbines, ~595–725 needed; with offshore turbines only 250–318. Land between turbines is usable for agriculture and other purposes.

claims.claim: The fact that the FAZ published a report on the Höcke verdict before it was delivered proves the outcome was pre-determined. (translated from German)
claims.impact: LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: concerns a criminal conviction
claims.notes: FAZ confirmed it accidentally published a pre-prepared 'conviction' version at 17:45 before the verdict at ~19:00. Court press officer confirmed the panel had not yet retired to deliberate at that point. FAZ mistake ≠ predetermined outcome.

claims.claim: A viral Tiktok video shows Annalena Baerbock crying during Alice Weidel's speech. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv confirmed the video was manipulated using AI. The original footage showed Baerbock in a different context. | DATE REVIEW: fact-check article date differs from claim date — verify before publication.

claims.claim: A TikTok post claims a man raised on-camera evidence of election manipulation at the 2024 European Parliament vote in Potsdam. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Police and city of Potsdam rejected the allegations as invented. The video appears to be satire repurposed without context markers as serious election fraud accusation.

claims.claim: A Bild article reports the founding of a Muslim Party of Germany ('Muslimische Partei Deutschlands'). (translated from German)
claims.notes: Bild confirmed no such article was ever published. The post used a forged Bild article header.

claims.claim: Videos show the Greens stormed an AfD party congress in December 2024. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The clips actually show a tumult at an AfD event in December 2018. AFP found no evidence the disrupters in any footage were Greens supporters.

claims.claim: Videos on social media show large crowds at pro-AfD demonstrations before the 2025 Bundestagswahl. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundestagswahl 2025 campaign impact
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the crowds were from Coldplay concerts, anti-extremism protests and other unrelated events. Audio of football chants was dubbed over in post-production.

claims.claim: Nothing has yet been invested from the Bundeswehr special fund. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Bundeswehr procurement office confirmed ~€87 billion of the €100 billion fund already bound in contracts; specific purchases include F-35 jets (~€10 bn) and Chinook helicopters (~€7 bn).

claims.claim: Sexual offences by immigrants rose 15% between 2017 and 2018 (5,258 to 6,046 cases). (translated from German)
claims.notes: The AfD omitted that Germany's sexual offences law was overhauled in late 2016 and the criminal catalogue expanded in 2017–18; figures across years are not directly comparable. Context-free comparison exaggerates apparent increase.

claims.claim: I don't believe human activity has anything significant to do with climate change. (translated from German)
claims.notes: IPCC and 99%+ of peer-reviewed climate scientists confirm anthropogenic climate change. Gauland expressed personal climate denial, contradicting scientific consensus.

claims.claim: [Doctored photo montage implying] all MPs were present for a pay-raise vote but only a handful (apart from AfD) for a pension debate. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The first photo was from the October 2017 inaugural session of the Bundestag, not a pay debate. The second photo – where Gauland is at the lectern – is from the July 2018 budget debate. Neither image is from the meetings the montage claimed.

claims.claim: The CDU-run Berlin government has raised minimum quotas for naturalisations from 20,000 last year to 40,000 this year – proof of a migration policy reversal under Merz. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The 40,000 target is an internal Berlin immigration authority operational target set since January 2025, based on staff resources and digital efficiency gains – not a political directive from Merz. The goal was publicly announced in January 2025.

claims.claim: The black-red government is giving away 9 billion euros of German taxpayers' money to Ukraine and buying Patriot missile systems for 5 billion. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Official sources only substantiate up to 5 billion euros in support since start of the new legislative period. Patriot systems are priced significantly below the 5 billion Weidel cited.

claims.claim: The AfD is the only party in Germany that protects Jewish people. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Central Council of Jews in Germany and American Jewish Committee Berlin reject this claim, describing the AfD as a 'racist and antisemitic party' that poses a threat to Jewish life. Multiple Jewish organisations issued joint statements against the AfD.

claims.claim: The IS claimed responsibility for the knife attack in a bus in Siegen. (translated from German)
claims.notes: No credible IS claim of the Siegen attack was published. Correctiv found no evidence to support Weidel's claim during the TV debate on 16 February 2025.

claims.claim: Alice Weidel supports ending the Pendlerpauschale [commuter tax allowance]. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The Weidel quote was taken out of context from an old statement. Her current position does not support ending the commuter deduction. Correctiv confirmed the attribution was stale and decontextualised.

claims.claim: Friedrich Merz has broken all his election promises – including the Heizungsgesetz [heating law] abolition and the migration policy reversal. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Several Merz pledges (GEG reform, migration border measures, naturalisation pause) were still being legislated as of July 2025. Blanket claim of all promises broken was premature and unsubstantiated.

claims.claim: Sexual offences are rising, especially because of migrants. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AfD MPs omitted that a 2017 reform of the sexual offences law (§177 StGB) broadened the criminal definition, causing the statistical increase regardless of any migrant factor. The Correctiv review of full PKS methodology shows this as a recurring AfD misuse of statistics.

claims.claim: A video clip shows Robert Habeck's cut-together admission of 350,000 open arrest warrants in Germany, implying state failure. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The 26-second clip was taken out of context and edited. Correctiv confirmed AFP findings that the broader statement was about EU-wide figures, not a domestic admission of state failure.

claims.claim: Ukrainian refugees can retire ten years earlier than Germans and receive a pension without having contributed to the system [a claim circulated by local AfD politician Hasenkamp]. (translated from German)
claims.notes: BMAS and Deutsche Rentenversicherung confirmed the standard retirement age applies to all, including Ukrainians. Pensions require 5+ years of German contributions. No special retirement age exists.

claims.claim: Half of Bürgergeld recipients are foreigners who have never paid into the social system; the other half are largely Germans with a migration background (Doppelpass). (translated from German)
claims.notes: About 47% of Bürgergeld recipients are non-German nationals; among German recipients, the majority (~36%) have no migration background according to BA data. Dual-citizenship data is not recorded.

claims.claim: Adolf Hitler was a communist-socialist type. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Broad coverage during Bundestagswahl campaign
claims.notes: Multiple historians confirmed this contradicts all established historical analysis. Hitler persecuted and murdered communists and social democrats after 1933. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: potential relativisation of Nazi crimes.

claims.claim: Only a small number of refugees integrate into the German labour market. (translated from German)
claims.notes: By September 2018, 289,000 refugees from main origin countries were in social-insurance-contributing employment, with a further 72,000 in minor employment. The claim ignores substantial integration progress.

claims.claim: Sahra Wagenknecht will switch to the AfD after stepping down from BSW leadership. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Wagenknecht confirmed she will not join the AfD. No basis in any sourced statement. | DATE REVIEW: fact-check article date differs from claim date — verify before publication.

claims.claim: The AfD as the strongest party is legally entitled to the Thuringian parliament presidency. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The constitution and Thuringia Constitutional Court confirmed the strongest party may only nominate a candidate; election remains a free vote of all deputies. CDU candidate König was subsequently elected.

claims.claim: The ZDF Hamburg demonstration photo has hundreds of demonstrators placed 'on' the Alster – a Photoshop lie to exaggerate crowd size. (translated from German)
claims.notes: dpa, Fraunhofer Institute expert, and AFP all confirmed the image is not manipulated. The visual anomaly is purely a product of the camera angle and the topography of the Jungfernstieg waterfront area.

claims.claim: Not a single penny from the Sondervermögen has been invested in the Bundeswehr. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Bundeswehr procurement office confirmed: '100 billion euros from the Sondervermögen practically fully committed in contracts.' Defence ministry: 87% bound, remainder already planned.

claims.claim: Between 250,000 and 300,000 people in Germany live without right of residence and would have to be deported immediately. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Federal government data showed 202,880 persons required to leave Germany as of December 2024, not 250,000–300,000. Chrupalla conflated different legal categories. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Terrifying number of COVID-19 vaccine deaths: in the first nine months of the year there had already been 1,028 deaths as a result of Corona vaccinations. (translated from German)
claims.notes: PEI (Paul-Ehrlich-Institut) data cited were reported adverse-event suspicions, not confirmed causal deaths. Saxony health ministry confirmed the '19 deaths' claim was incorrect. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Failed mass experiment: Incidence WITH vaccinations is five times higher than a year ago — proof that vaccines are failing. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Comparison used incidence=0 (Aug 2020) vs incidence=56 (Aug 2021), a period when no vaccines existed in the earlier year, making the comparison meaningless as a measure of vaccine efficacy. (translated from German)

claims.claim: mRNA vaccinations could have killed 17 million people worldwide. The vaccines were 'never tested'. (translated from German)
claims.notes: WHO, EMA and multiple studies confirmed vaccines are safe and effective. AFP previously debunked the 17-million deaths claim in 2023. Clinical trials included people of all ages, genders and ethnicities. (translated from German)

claims.claim: AfD leader Alice Weidel said China is the only country that can force the United States to 'sit down and negotiate'.
claims.notes: Original livestream (August 2024 AfD rally in Thuringia) showed Weidel was criticising Afghan deportations as a gimmick, not praising China. China not mentioned in the speech. Former Chinese consul general amplified the false clip.

claims.claim: Current Bundestagswahl poll shows AfD at 28%, SPD at 25%, CDU at 16% — the AfD is leading. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Shared widely to exaggerate AfD support before Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: The poll was from the Brandenburg state election (September 2024), shared with source and date cropped out. Actual January 2025 Bundestagswahl polls showed CDU 31.5%, AfD 19.5%, SPD 16.5%. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The AfD 'Einzelfallticker' reveals the true extent of crimes committed by migrants that the mainstream media conceals. (translated from German)
claims.notes: ARD analysis of 171 Einzelfallticker entries found nearly half had no indication of a non-German suspect. Sociologists noted the ticker is unrepresentative propaganda, not statistics, and falsely implies nationality causes criminality. (translated from German)

claims.claim: According to the Federal Police annual report, people with a migration background commit knife attacks six times more often than Germans. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Federal Police spokesperson confirmed the statistic does not track migration background; only German/non-German is recorded, and Federal Police data covers only train stations, not all of Germany. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Statistics show that foreigners are particularly criminal — they commit a disproportionate share of crimes relative to their population share. (translated from German)
claims.notes: BKA and criminologists confirm the PKS crime statistic inflates apparent non-German suspect rates by including tourists, transit travellers and short-term visitors in the non-German suspect total but not in the comparison population base. (translated from German)

claims.claim: An increase in sexual offences in Germany is attributable to migrants. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AfD MPs omitted that changes to the criminal code (Strafrechtsreform) distorted the 2025 PKS figures for sexual offences, making year-on-year comparisons invalid. BKA confirmed migration background is not recorded in PKS. (translated from German)

claims.claim: CDU candidate Friedrich Merz has a secret 10-point plan that includes measures harming ordinary Germans. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Reached ~15 million impressions on TikTok before Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: Correctiv identified 201 TikTok videos with Bundestagswahl misinformation reaching ~15 million views. TikTok failed to remove most even after notification. Part of broader disinformation campaign benefiting AfD. (translated from German)

claims.claim: In Munich the AfD was not selectable with the Erststimme — this indicates election manipulation. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Election integrity narrative spread before Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: Correctiv confirmed the AfD was on all valid Munich ballots; Munich has multiple Wahlkreise and AfD appeared on all applicable ballot papers. (translated from German)

claims.claim: In Magdeburg over 3,000 ballots with AfD votes were found in the Elbe river — this is election fraud. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Election fraud narrative to delegitimise results unfavourable to AfD
claims.notes: Correctiv found no credible evidence for this claim. State electoral authorities and police found no ballots in the Elbe. Part of a broader post-election disinformation campaign by pro-AfD networks. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The AfD poll showing 51% support is from a recent IGMN Sonntagsfrage survey. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Artificially inflated AfD support perception near Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: IGMN does not appear to be a recognised polling institute. Actual Bundestagswahl result for AfD was approximately 20.8%. The supposed 51% figure had no verifiable source. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Germany has the highest energy prices in the world. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Repeated claim during Bundestagswahl campaign
claims.notes: IEA and Eurostat data show several countries — Denmark, Belgium, Ireland — have higher household electricity prices than Germany. The claim of world-highest is unsupported. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The German Schuldenbremse prohibits taking on new debt under any circumstances. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundestagswahl campaign claim
claims.notes: ARD legal and economic correspondents confirmed the Schuldenbremse allows up to 0.35% of GDP in structural new debt annually, plus exemptions for extraordinary emergencies. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The AfD's Einzelfallticker is performing the role of objective enlightener after the media has largely kept silent or obfuscated the true extent of migrant crime. (translated from German)
claims.notes: ARD analysis showed nearly 50% of AfD's own Einzelfallticker entries had no indication of a non-German suspect. Sociologists (CeMAS) described the ticker as propaganda, not objective crime reporting. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The rise in Germany's birth rate is driven by migrants and not by ethnic Germans (biodeutsche Babys), indicating a demographic replacement in progress. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv showed nearly 60% of births to foreign-national mothers were to mothers from non-Muslim-majority countries. Only 8% of all German births in 2015 were to mothers from 'culturally foreign' countries. The birth rate among German-nationality women was also rising. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The majority of murderers in Germany have a migration background. (translated from German)
claims.notes: PKS does not record migration background; it records nationality. BKA explicitly states migration background data is not collected in PKS. The 'majority of murderers' claim originated in 2019 online posts and was directly contradicted by PKS methodology notes. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Friedrich Merz signed a secret migration agreement with Kenya to bring 1.9 million Kenyan workers to Germany. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Part of Russia-linked election interference operation targeting Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: Correctiv identified Storm-1516 as a Russia-linked disinformation campaign creating ~100 fake news websites before the German federal election. One of several fabricated stories benefiting AfD. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Höcke claimed the phrase 'Alles für Deutschland' (Everything for Germany) is not a banned SA slogan and he was unaware of its criminal-code prohibition. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Federal appellate conviction upheld September 2025
claims.notes: BGH confirmed Höcke's conviction for knowingly using banned SA slogan 'Alles für Deutschland'. Two convictions: Halle 2024 (fine) and Merseburg 2025. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: An incidence rate below 100 is 'never achievable' — restrictions are permanent. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Malsack-Winkemann was later arrested December 2022 as part of the Reichsbürger coup plot. COVID claims sought to build distrust in public health measures. ARD noted incidence targets had been achieved in multiple countries. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Bundespräsident Steinmeier threatened to annul the Bundestagswahl if the 'wrong' parties won. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Part of coordinated AI influence operation before Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder documented a network of right-wing AI-generated personas amplifying false claims about election annulment. Steinmeier made no such threat. Same AI influencer accounts spread Merz 10-point-plan fabrication. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Only 0.5% of Syrians are entitled to asylum — deport bogus refugees consistently. (translated from German)
claims.notes: The 0.5% figure refers only to full asylum under Grundgesetz Art.16a; nearly 88% of Syrians in Germany held some form of legal protection at end of 2023. The post omitted four categories of legal protection. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Germany has the highest insolvency rate in 25 years. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundestagswahl 2025 campaign debate
claims.notes: Creditreform data showed insolvency rate at 72 per 10,000 companies, far below the peak of 135 per 10,000 in 2003. Total corporate insolvencies expected at 22,400, not a record. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Human influence on climate change is not dominant. Solar activity is a better explanation for global warming than CO2 emissions. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Max-Planck-Institut für Sonnensystemforschung and climate researcher Mojib Latif confirmed solar activity cannot explain temperature increases over the past 50 years. Near-unanimous scientific consensus attributes warming to CO2 emissions. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Human-caused climate change 'cannot be proven scientifically'; climate change has always existed and will always exist. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Swiss researchers demonstrated no comparable simultaneous global temperature increase in historical record. 99.94% of peer-reviewed climate science papers (54,195 studies 1991-2015) confirmed anthropogenic climate change. The AfD 'study' cited broken links and reader letters as sources. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Climate change is a natural phenomenon. Human CO2 emissions do not contribute significantly to climate change. More than 500 scientists signed a letter saying 'there is no climate emergency'. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder clarified the 'no climate emergency' letter does not reject anthropogenic climate change; Hilse misrepresented its content. WMO chief Taalas does not support the AfD position. Scientific consensus that humans are primary driver of current warming is overwhelming. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The tagesschau manipulates its weather maps for ideological reasons to make heatwaves appear more extreme than they really are. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder confirmed the maps compared show different meteorological phenomena with different scales — not manipulation. AfD branches shared the claim widely but it was based on comparing incompatible map types. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A photo of the anti-AfD demonstration in Hamburg was manipulated to make the crowd appear much larger than it really was. (translated from German)
claims.notes: DPA confirmed the image was an original unaltered dpa photo. Martin Steinebach (Fraunhofer) confirmed the apparent optical effect is caused by perspective, not manipulation. Multiple other photos and videos confirmed the scale of the demonstration. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — living named individual.

claims.claim: The Bundestagswahl 2021 results showed systematic election fraud; disinformation about wahlbetrug was spread across AfD supporter networks. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Delegitimised Bundestagswahl 2021 results in AfD/Querdenker circles
claims.notes: Researchers found election fraud disinformation was confined to AfD-supporter and conspiracy-ideology networks and had limited reach outside those communities. The Berlin Abgeordnetenhaus election had genuine administrative problems but no evidence of systematic fraud in the Bundestagswahl. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The Bundesrat voted for the AfD in winter 2025. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread false impression of institutional AfD support
claims.notes: Bundesrat spokesperson confirmed no such vote took place and the Bundesrat cannot elect parties under the Grundgesetz. AFP audio analysis tools confirmed the video voiceover was 99-100% AI-generated. Political science professor Uwe Jun confirmed the claim false. (translated from German)

claims.claim: According to Chemnitz police, 56 rapes were committed by migrants and 4 by unknown perpetrators — migrants dominate sexual violence statistics. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: the Chemnitz rape statistics cited by AfD Saxon politician Maximilian Krah were 'freely invented' (frei erfunden). Academic analysis of 2018 AfD press releases found 95% focused exclusively on foreign suspects, systematically omitting German perpetrators in their crime framing. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A video shows genuine Bundestagswahl ballots from Leipzig Wahlkreis 1 with no AfD line — proof the AfD was removed from the ballot to rig the election. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Election fraud narrative before and on Bundestagswahl 2025 election day
claims.notes: Leipzig city spokesperson, LKA Sachsen, and Staatsanwaltschaft Leipzig all confirmed the ballot papers in the video were 'obvious forgeries'. The genuine Leipzig Wahlkreis 1 ballot listed AfD and candidate Christian Kriegel in first position. (translated from German)

claims.claim: In North Rhine-Westphalia the Staatsschutz (state security) investigates citizens who display German flags. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder confirmed the claim was false. The specific NRW case cited involved a different legal issue unrelated to displaying the German flag per se. No Staatsschutz guidelines prohibit displaying the German national flag. (translated from German)

claims.claim: AfD voters should sign their ballots to prevent election fraud and show support for Alice Weidel. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Targeted AfD voters to invalidate their own ballots before Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: §39 Bundeswahlgesetz: any ballot with an addition is invalid; a signature counts as an addition violating vote secrecy. AFP confirmed the same sharepic had circulated since 2019 pre-elections. AfD's own magazine warned members not to sign. The fake was primarily spread by satire channels to sabotage AfD votes. (translated from German)

claims.claim: What we are experiencing on German streets is jihad — a religious war is being waged against the German population. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundesverfassungsschutz cited this as evidence of islamfeindliche (anti-Muslim) agitation
claims.notes: Bundesverfassungsschutz report (May 2025) specifically cited Weidel's Brandenburg speech as evidence of anti-Muslim agitation and classified it as islamfeindlich. Criminologists and security experts disputed the characterisation of crime incidents as religiously motivated jihad. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: Remigration only means the orderly deportation of foreigners without right of residence — not the expulsion of German citizens with migration background. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Used to moderate AfD's public image post-Potsdam meeting revelations
claims.notes: Correctiv analysis of 1,100+ AfD Bundestag member social media posts found the term 'Remigration' deliberately left vague to normalise it. Höcke's 2018 book explicitly includes 'non-assimilated citizens'; courts in Münster and the Bundesverwaltungsgericht ruled the concept unconstitutional when applied to citizens. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A large-scale 'Remigration project' will be necessary that will not be possible without 'well-tempered cruelty' towards non-integrable migrants. (translated from German)
claims.platform: Book (Nie zweimal in denselben Fluss, Manuscriptum Verlag)
claims.impact: Provided ideological blueprint for Potsdam meeting Remigration plans (2023)
claims.notes: Correctiv documented how Höcke's 2018 book formulated the Remigration concept that was later presented at the Potsdam meeting. Courts found AfD's use of Remigration unconstitutional when applied to naturalised citizens. Höcke explicitly targets Staatsbürger with migration background in the book. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: CDU politicians Friedrich Merz and Norbert Röttgen, and Foreign Minister Annalena Baerbock, are 'vassals of America'. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Part of AfD pro-Russia narrative demonising NATO-supporting politicians
claims.notes: Bundesverfassungsschutz report cited Chrupalla's Nuremberg speech as evidence of demokratiefeindliche statements. The 'vassals of America' framing is a standard Kremlin messaging trope. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A tagesschau news report shows that Friedrich Merz promised to legalise drugs and introduce a universal basic income if elected chancellor. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread false information about CDU policy to discredit Merz before Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder confirmed the broadcast was AI-generated fake using a cloned tagesschau visual identity. Part of a broader campaign of fake news websites and deepfake 'news broadcasts' identified as Storm-1516 Russian disinformation infrastructure. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A comparison table shows the positions of CDU, SPD, FDP, Linke and Greens on migration, gender, and other issues — the AfD is the only party with sensible policies. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Viral before Bundestagswahl 2025 — misrepresented all non-AfD parties
claims.notes: Correctiv: more than half the listed positions for CDU, SPD, FDP, Linke and Greens were false. Only AfD positions were mostly accurately represented. The table was designed to make all other parties look extreme and AfD look moderate. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A large pro-AfD demonstration was held in Cologne the day before the Bundestagswahl 2025 — showing massive public support for the party. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Artificially boosted AfD public support narrative before Bundestagswahl
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the video showed a demonstration against right-wing extremism from a year earlier, not a pro-AfD rally. Reverse image search and metadata confirmed the footage was from January 2024 anti-AfD protests. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A photomontage shows an AfD banner displayed across the Bundestag building, implying widespread popular support for the party's values. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv confirmed the Bundestag image was a digital montage; no such banner was displayed on the actual building. Used to create false impression of mainstream acceptability of AfD messaging. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The Greens' migration policy amounts to 'Umvolkung' (ethnic replacement of the German people). (translated from German)
claims.impact: Cited by Bundesverfassungsschutz as evidence of völkisch-nationalist ideology
claims.notes: Bundesverfassungsschutz classified Krah's use of 'Umvolkung' as evidence of a 'menschenwürdewidriger völkisch-abstammungsmäßiger Volksbegriff' (a human-dignity-violating ethnic concept of 'the people'). The term 'Umvolkung' originates in Nazi racial ideology. (translated from German)

claims.claim: According to a DPA report, Friedrich Merz has agreed to give the SPD the interior ministry as part of coalition talks. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Post-election disinformation to shape coalition narrative
claims.notes: Correctiv confirmed the DPA report Nius referenced never existed. DPA denied issuing any such report. The fabricated story was designed to influence public perception of CDU-SPD coalition talks immediately after Bundestagswahl 2025. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Alice Weidel is the leading and most popular candidate to run Germany ahead of the Bundestagswahl. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Broadcast to 100M+ users, misrepresenting state of German election polls
claims.notes: ARD DeutschlandTrend (January 2025): CDU/CSU 31%, AfD ~20%. In candidate satisfaction, Weidel ranked 5th (20% satisfied), behind Habeck (28%), Merz (25%), Wagenknecht and Lindner (both 21%). Neither Weidel nor AfD were leading in any measured category. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A Tagesschau news segment falsely reported that the AfD lost the election. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Post-election disinformation targeting AfD supporters
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck confirmed the clip showing a tagesschau segment declaring AfD lost was an AI-generated deepfake. Part of the broader network of fake tagesschau news videos used to spread disinformation around the Bundestagswahl 2025. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Millions of people in Germany receive Bürgergeld without working — this is a systemic failure that rewards idleness over employment. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder noted that the majority of Bürgergeld recipients are in work (Aufstocker) or are not currently employable due to disability, caring responsibilities, or other barriers. The claim that recipients are simply idle was not supported by BA statistics. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The Holocaust Memorial in Berlin is a 'monument of shame' (Denkmal der Schande) — Germany is the only country in the world that plants a monument of shame in the heart of its capital city. Germans must embrace a '180 degree turn' in their culture of memory. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Triggered debate about Holocaust remembrance and led to temporary AfD disciplinary proceedings against Höcke
claims.notes: Höcke was later expelled from the AfD candidates list temporarily for the speech. Historical experts noted his phrase inverted the intended meaning of the memorial (which commemorates German guilt) and framed German historical responsibility as a source of shame to be overcome. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: 'It feels like 1989 again' — the current situation in Germany resembles the collapse of the GDR; ordinary Germans are resisting an authoritarian regime like East Germans did. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder indexed Höcke's 1989 comparison as part of AfD election-fraud and grievance rhetoric. Historians and political scientists noted the comparison is historically inaccurate: the FRG is a functioning democracy with free elections, independent courts and press freedom, unlike the GDR. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The WHO Pandemic Treaty will allow the WHO to override national laws and impose mandatory vaccinations on European populations. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread widely before European Parliament elections June 2024
claims.notes: WHO confirmed the draft Pandemic Agreement explicitly preserves national sovereignty and does not require mandatory vaccinations. AFP's fact-checks of Anderson's pandemic treaty claims confirmed both the mRNA death toll claim and the treaty claim were false. (translated from German)

claims.claim: An AI-generated campaign video shows SPD Health Minister Karl Lauterbach being arrested by police — a vision of holding politicians accountable for ruining Germany. (translated from German)
claims.platform: Social media (YouTube/Facebook — official AfD Brandenburg channel)
claims.impact: Used official AfD Brandenburg channel to spread AI-generated political attack content
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder documented the AI video as containing racist stereotypes and depicting Lauterbach being arrested — described as incitement. Lauterbach called it 'KI-Fakes'. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Cardinal Marx said: 'Whoever votes for the AfD goes to hell.' (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread to discredit Catholic Church and portray it as politically hostile to AfD before 2019 EU election
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder (page index 16) confirmed the quote attributed to Kardinal Marx has circulated for years but was never actually said. The claim has been debunked multiple times since 2017. (translated from German)

claims.claim: There is an increasing number of Islamist-motivated attacks on dogs in Germany, which the mainstream media is concealing. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder (page index 16 listing) confirmed no evidence exists for the claim of Islamist-motivated attacks on dogs in Germany. Described as 'Belege dafür existieren nicht'. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Germany is experiencing 'Umvolkung' (ethnic replacement) — quotes by German politicians proving this were shared at a PEGIDA rally. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder (page index 15) noted AfD Bundestag MP Heßenkemper spread 'diverse Fake-Zitate' (various fake quotes) at a PEGIDA rally and used the term 'Umvolkung', while also calling Green politicians 'Öko-Faschisten'. The quotes were not attributable to the people named. (translated from German)

claims.claim: He did not know the phrase 'Alles für Deutschland' was a banned SA slogan when he used it; it is a common everyday phrase ('Allerweltsspruch'). (translated from German)
claims.platform: TV debate (Welt TV — Höcke vs Voigt)
claims.impact: Höcke repeatedly used claim during 2024 Thuringia election campaign
claims.notes: Both the Halle (2024) and Merseburg (2025) courts, confirmed by the BGH in September 2025, found Höcke knowingly used the banned SA slogan at AfD events. Telekom confirmed they had previously warned Alice Weidel for the same claim (September 2023). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: A social media profile of a refugee shows him boasting about claiming benefits while insulting Germans — proving migrants despise the country that hosts them. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder (page index 15) documented fake social media profiles claiming to be refugees or migrants, used to stir anti-immigrant sentiment. One fake profile posed as a refugee boasting about benefits. Investigators found these were coordinated fake accounts with stolen profile photos. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The Correctiv investigation into the Potsdam meeting was false; no deportation plans were discussed, and German citizens were never mentioned as targets of remigration. (translated from German)
claims.impact: AfD denial triggered mass protests across Germany in January 2024
claims.notes: Correctiv confirmed its reporting was accurate. The Bundesverwaltungsgericht in Leipzig characterised the Sellner Remigration concept (presented at the meeting) as targeting citizens and unconstitutional (verfassungswidrig). Gerrit Huy (AfD MP present) confirmed she had been pursuing remigration plans for years. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Germany has a serious crime problem driven by migration — crime has risen dramatically since 2015. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: PKS 2024 shows crime statistics did not show a dramatic upward trend attributable to migration; criminologists note that PKS does not record migration background and increases in some categories reflect reporting changes, not underlying crime waves. (translated from German)

claims.claim: PKS data show foreigners are responsible for a disproportionate share of violent crime — this reflects cultural and ethnic differences in crime propensity. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: BKA explicitly states PKS records nationality (German/non-German), not migration background or ethnicity. The PKS includes non-resident foreigners (tourists, transit passengers) in suspect totals but not in population denominator, making direct comparisons statistically invalid. Sociologists reject cultural-causal explanations for PKS disparities. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The Bundestagswahl result count system allowed for ballot switching — election results in several Wahlkreise showed impossible discrepancies between Erst- and Zweitstimmen. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Election fraud narrative spread to delegitimise Bundestagswahl 2025 results
claims.notes: Correctiv debunked multiple post-Bundestagswahl fraud claims. Electoral authorities confirmed split-ticket voting (voting for different parties with the two votes) routinely produces exactly such discrepancies and is entirely normal and legal. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A Sonntagsfrage survey commissioned by IGMN placed the AfD at 51% nationally — proof the party has majority support ahead of the Bundestagswahl. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Artificially boosted AfD support perception in pre-election period
claims.notes: Correctiv: IGMN does not appear to be a recognised polling institute. The final Bundestagswahl result for AfD was approximately 20.8%. The supposed 51% poll had no verifiable source. The graphic was an anonymous fabrication with no institutional attribution. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The average pension in Germany is 1,200 euros. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Made during RTL election night broadcast ahead of Bundestagswahl 2025
claims.notes: Correctiv: German Pension Insurance data shows average net pension (Rentenzahlbetrag) for men was 1,608 euros and for women 1,237 euros. The 1,200 euro figure does not represent the average; only one in five pensions falls below that threshold. Wagenknecht conflated a low-end figure with the average. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Sahra Wagenknecht is switching from BSW to the AfD — breaking news. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: BSW press spokesperson Christian Posselt confirmed the claim was fabricated. Originated on YouTube channel 'Midas Effekt', spread by 'GameChanger' network across Facebook, YouTube and Instagram. Same accounts had previously fabricated false reports about Merz being 'voted out by his cabinet'. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The war in Ukraine has claimed over one million deaths at the front. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: Verified estimates place total military dead on both sides between 238,000 and 300,000. The combined killed-and-wounded figure may have surpassed one million by end-2024, but Wagenknecht cited this as deaths alone. No source provided. The post was shared approximately 300 times. (translated from German)

claims.claim: There are no right-wing extremists in the AfD; when they are found, the party acts and exclusion proceedings follow. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: The Verfassungsschutzbericht since 2022 explicitly records a right-wing extremist personnel potential within the AfD, estimated at 20,000 individuals by end-2024 out of approx. 48,000 members. The number actually excluded is unclear. Chrupalla's claim that the party consistently acts against such individuals is not supported by available data. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The AfD fell below 20 per cent in the Rheinland-Pfalz Landtag election because of postal ballot fraud. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Claimed to delegitimise Rheinland-Pfalz Landtag election results 2026
claims.notes: Correctiv: The 0.5-percentage-point difference between the initial ARD projection (20%) and the final result (19.5%) is within normal tolerance. The SPD also lost 1.1 points between projection and final count. The Landeswahlleitung confirmed no known irregularities. Postal votes were not counted separately, so no causal link to postal ballots could be established. (translated from German)

claims.claim: An analysis of Kassenärztliche Bundesvereinigung (KBV) billing data shows a drastic rise in unexplained deaths in 2021, consistent with Covid-19 vaccination side effects. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: AfD Bundestag member Martin Sichert requested the data; data analyst Tom Lausen analysed it and presented the results. Correctiv found the methodology was flawed and the data did not support the conclusion. KBV billing codes for 'unexplained deaths' do not reflect actual causes of death. Independent medical experts confirmed the analysis was not scientifically valid. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Only 17 per cent of Bürgergeld (citizens' income) recipients are Germans without a migration background — migrants dominate the welfare system. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: The official Bundesagentur für Arbeit statistic shows approximately 36 per cent of employable Bürgergeld recipients are Germans without a migration background — more than double the 17% claimed. The figure in circulation was based on a flawed Tichys Einblick graphic that used a different base population (all 5.4 million recipients) but applied migration-background data only available for the 3.9 million employable subset. Asylum seekers are excluded from Bürgergeld entirely under separate law. (translated from German)

claims.claim: It feels again like 1989 in the GDR — we live in a kind of DDR 2.0, a new dictatorship with no real freedom of opinion. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Used in 2019 Thuringia state election campaign (Vollende die Wende)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: Historian Patrice Poutrus (University of Erfurt) stated that equating the Federal Republic with the DDR is 'historically and politically not appropriate at all'. Historian Ilko-Sascha Kowalczuk called it 'a cheap propaganda lie'. Basic human rights were not a reality in the DDR; free political parties like the AfD could not have operated there. (translated from German)

claims.claim: We are living in a new Politbüro system — the other parties are like the GDR's Blockparteien, and the AfD is the new Neues Forum opposition. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: Historians reject the DDR-Federal Republic equivalence. The AfD can operate freely, contest elections and enter parliaments — the Neues Forum civil rights movement operated under existential threat from the Stasi. Gauland's comparison is described by historians as deliberate historical trivialisation. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A Bundestagsvizepräsidentin ran behind a banner reading 'Deutschland, Du mieses Stück Scheiße' — referring to Claudia Roth. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: Weidel's claim that Claudia Roth ran behind a banner with that slogan is false. The incident referenced occurred at a 2015 Hannover demonstration against an AfD party conference where Autonome groups chanted the slogan — but Roth stated she did not hear the parole and was not associated with the radical left groups at the front. ARD faktenfinder confirmed Roth's account. Weidel's on-demand AfD press office did not reply to queries about the specific occasion. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The Oktoberfest is practically empty — there are hardly any visitors — proving that terror fear is driving Germans away from public events since 2015. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Published in final days of 2017 Bundestagswahl campaign to fuel security narrative
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: Approximately 700,000 visitors attended the opening weekend, 100,000 more than 2016. Oktoberfest press office confirmed attendance was in line with the long-term average. The AfD later edited its Facebook post but maintained 'gähnende Leere' (yawning emptiness) which was directly contradicted by official figures. Campaign manager Alice Weidel refused to correct the claim at a press conference. (translated from German)

claims.claim: There is clear evidence of systematic electoral fraud at the 2021 Bundestagswahl — the AfD received zero first votes in Würzburg despite having a candidate on the ballot. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Post-election fraud narrative spread to delegitimise 2021 Bundestagswahl results
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: The AfD Direktkandidat in Würzburg was excluded from the ballot due to missing original signatures — a procedural failure by the AfD itself, not manipulation. It was therefore impossible to receive first votes for the AfD there. An AfD Thuringia state MP shared the fraud claim on Facebook. A Russia-registered anonymous fake-news page had promoted a 'systematic fraud proven' narrative days before the election with no evidence. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Thousands of ballots were deliberately invalidated in Thüringen's municipal elections to weaken the AfD and the WerteUnion — clear evidence of vote fraud. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread in context of 2024 Thüringen Landtag election campaign
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: Thüringen state election supervisor Holger Poppenhäger stated the elections were 'exemplary and ran without significant incidents'. The higher invalid vote percentage in some Ortsteil-Bürgermeister elections occurred because only one candidate was listed — many voters wrote in alternatives, invalidating their ballot under election rules. No evidence of deliberate manipulation. Claim was amplified by former AfD politician Frauke Petry and WerteUnion Thüringen head Albert H. Weiler. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A 127-page AfD study 'Facts instead of Fake News' documents the catastrophic state of Germany with hard data — objective scientific analysis. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: The presentation fails scientific standards — many source links do not work, the AfD cites reader letters and opinion comments as evidence, important context for statistics is systematically omitted, and claims are misleading or incomplete. The Tagesschau verdict: the party promised 'Facts instead of Fake News' but partly delivered the latter itself. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Former EU Commissioner Thierry Breton said the EU could declare the Bundestagswahl invalid if the AfD won. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread in the weeks before Bundestagswahl 2025 to claim EU would suppress AfD victory
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: Breton was speaking about the Digital Services Act and its potential application in cases of election manipulation on social media — not about annulling election results. He confirmed to AFP that the claim was 'Fake News'. The EU has no legal competence to nullify national elections. An AfD Bavarian state MP was among those sharing the false claim. Elon Musk originally amplified the clip on X. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A ruling by the Federal Constitutional Court means police can no longer enter rooms in asylum shelters to pick up people for deportation — deportations are now factually impossible. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: The Bundesverfassungsgericht ruling requires police to obtain a search warrant before entering shared accommodation — it does not ban deportations. Legal experts Andreas Zimmermann (University of Potsdam) and Sarah Lincoln (Gesellschaft für Freiheitsrechte) confirmed courts routinely grant such warrants promptly. The misleading framing originated with far-right Kleinstpartei Freie Sachsen and was amplified by the right-wing portal Nius. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Sahra Wagenknecht was abruptly and deliberately muted live on Welt TV because her Russia comments were unwanted — proof of media censorship. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: The circulating clip was an excerpt from a longer live interview that had been broadcast in full and without interruption. Wagenknecht herself confirmed this on X, sharing the complete uncut version of the interview. The short clip was later reused in an edited segment but was not evidence of censorship. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A video showing a large crowd in Berlin depicts farmers protesting against the government — proof of widespread popular revolt. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: The video shows the anti-right-wing extremism demonstration in Berlin on 21 January 2024 — not a farmers' protest. SPD Bundestag faction spokesperson Christian Helms confirmed this to AFP. AFP found no evidence of a large farmers' protest in Berlin on that date. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A graphic shows only 17 per cent of Bürgergeld recipients are Germans without a migration background — proof that migrants dominate the welfare state. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: The Tichys Einblick graphic applied migration-background statistics (available only for 3.95 million employable recipients) to the full 5.4 million recipient base — a methodologically invalid comparison. Official Bundesagentur für Arbeit data shows approximately 36 per cent of employable recipients are Germans without a migration background. Asylum seekers cannot receive Bürgergeld under German law. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Björn Höcke said: 'I feel intellectually superior to the people of Thuringia.' (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: The quote is completely untraceable. Neither Höcke's social media, the AfD Thuringia faction, nor any searchable press archive contain the quote. AfD Thuringia bureau director Robert Teske confirmed to Correctiv that 'the quote does not come from Björn Höcke, nor has he ever expressed himself in a similar manner.' The oldest source was an anonymous X post from 11 September 2023 whose author admitted they could not find their own source. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Angela Merkel must be put before a court after her term for violating the law on the euro bailout and refugee policy — she committed crimes. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Central 2017 Bundestagswahl campaign message; used to mobilise AfD base
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: Weidel repeatedly called for Merkel to be 'hauled before court' in the 2017 campaign, claiming she violated the law on euro rescue measures and migration. Legal experts found no basis for criminal prosecution; government decisions on both the eurozone and refugee policy were contested politically but not criminal acts. The campaign linked Merkel to Islamic terror attacks via billboard imagery ('Frau Merkel, wann werden Sie bremsen?'). BuzzFeed analysis: 7 of the 10 most-shared Merkel articles in 5 years were false. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Ukraine war propaganda: Footage circulating online is staged to manipulate Western public opinion — the videos are fake. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv 2022 annual review: All fact-checked cases where media were accused of staging Ukraine coverage turned out to be fabrications or old images placed in a false context. The staging narrative was one of the five most persistent 2022 disinformation narratives in Germany, amplified by AfD-adjacent channels. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Figures shared by AfD members show foreigners receive Bürgergeld at massively higher rates — proof the welfare state is being systematically abused by migrants. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: AfD MEP Petr Bystron and AfD Bundestag MP Nicole Höchst shared graphics conflating Bürgergeld recipients with asylum seekers. Asylum seekers are legally barred from receiving Bürgergeld. The higher share of foreigners in Bürgergeld statistics reflects Ukrainian war refugees (integrated since 2022) and recognised refugees — not asylum seekers. Correctiv notes the figures are routinely presented without this essential legal context. (translated from German)

claims.claim: There are 215,000 Syrians without a residence permit living in Germany who must be obligatorily deported. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: As of 30 April 2025, only approximately 11,000 obligatorily deportable Syrians were registered in Germany, of whom 90 per cent were tolerated (Duldung). The 224,637 figure Weidel cited covers all obligatorily deportable persons across all nationalities. A federal government response to a parliamentary question (June 2024) confirmed Syrians do not form the majority of obligatorily deportable persons in most German states. (translated from German)

claims.claim: In Chinese social media posts: Alice Weidel said in a viral video that China is the only country that can force the United States to sit down and negotiate. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: The video clip comes from an AfD rally in Thuringia on 31 August 2024 where Weidel criticised the German government's deportation of 28 Afghan nationals as a 'bad joke'. She did not mention China in the speech. The false claim spread on Chinese social media following the US-China trade war truce in May 2025. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The Deutsche Telekom formerly used the SA slogan 'Alles geben für Deutschland' as an advertising slogan — so using it is not uniquely a Nazi reference. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Made during Thüringen state election campaign April 2024; Höcke had previously been convicted for using the banned SA slogan
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder / BGH ruling: Deutsche Telekom confirmed the claim is false and threatened legal action. The slogan 'Alles für Deutschland' was a banned SA (Sturmabteilung) slogan — the Telekom never used it as advertising. Höcke said he did not know it was an SA slogan when he used it, which the court found implausible. The BGH confirmed two criminal convictions for use of a symbol of a banned organisation in September 2025. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active criminal proceedings now concluded.

claims.claim: A video shows AfD postal ballots being sorted out and destroyed in Hamburg — undeniable proof the 2025 Bundestagswahl is being stolen. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Published on election day of 2025 Bundestagswahl
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: Hamburg Senate, the Bundeswahlleiterin and Hamburg Landeswahlleiter Oliver Rudolf all confirmed the video showed forgeries. The electoral documents in the video do not match official Hamburg postal ballot specifications — wrong colour, wrong adhesive system, missing row numbering. Rudolf called it 'a perfidious attempt to delegitimise our democratic elections'. AfD candidate Jordan initially shared the video claiming 'drastic consequences' were needed, then retracted after the forgery was confirmed. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Tino Chrupalla's video of him criticising postal voting is current evidence of ballot fraud in Baden-Württemberg. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread around 2026 Baden-Württemberg state election
claims.notes: Correctiv: The Chrupalla video was old and unrelated to the current Baden-Württemberg election. Chrupalla himself had not claimed to uncover fraud in Baden-Württemberg — the claim was fabricated by social media accounts presenting the video in false context. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Markus Krall exposed postal vote fraud in the Baden-Württemberg election — this has now been proven. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread around 2026 Baden-Württemberg state election to delegitimise results
claims.notes: Correctiv Faktenforum: Markus Krall had not claimed to expose fraud. The entire Krall attribution was invented and not made by Krall himself. (translated from German)

claims.claim: TikTok is deliberately ignoring AfD content and suppressing party-friendly information before the Bundestagswahl. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Framing ahead of Bundestagswahl 2025 to cast social media as biased against AfD
claims.notes: Correctiv: Investigation found TikTok was systematically failing to act on reported disinformation before the Bundestagswahl — but the failures disadvantaged multiple parties and were not specifically directed against the AfD. AfD accounts were among the most prolific spreaders of disinformation that remained online after being reported. The evidence showed platform negligence rather than deliberate AfD suppression. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Kay-Uwe Ziegler (AfD): Every time a voter tries to select Donald Trump on a voting machine, the vote is automatically switched to Kamala Harris. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Amplification of US election fraud narrative by German AfD politician
claims.notes: Correctiv: Ziegler's Facebook post spreading the vote-switching claim was already fact-checked by Correctiv in an earlier stand-alone fact-check before this analysis was published. The post has since been deleted. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A photo shows Syrian Bürgergeld recipients back from their holiday in Syria — proof they are travelling on German welfare money. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: Reverse image search traced the photo to an old stock image of an Indonesian family in Indonesia, with no connection to Syria or Germany. The image was originally published by the far-right outlet Journalistenwatch with fabricated context. Journalistenwatch has a documented record of spreading false information previously debunked by AFP. (translated from German)

claims.claim: AI-generated influencer accounts are spreading pro-AfD content that looks like grassroots support — but are not declared as artificial. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Active during Bundestagswahl 2025 campaign
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: AI-generated right-wing influencer accounts on TikTok and Instagram presented fabricated personas as real citizens endorsing AfD positions ahead of the Bundestagswahl. The content was not labelled as AI-generated. These accounts amassed millions of views. (translated from German)

claims.claim: A video appearing to show a Tagesschau broadcast falsely reports an AfD ban — the public broadcaster is fabricating news against the AfD. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: The video was a deepfake or AI-manipulated clip using a fake Tagesschau visual template. Tagesschau confirmed it never broadcast the alleged content. The fake was designed to look like a real news broadcast and spread widely before the Bundestagswahl 2025. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Online decision helper for parties ahead of the Bundestagswahl shows that all parties except the AfD support open borders and mass migration. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Spread via TikTok ahead of Bundestagswahl 2025 to distort party positions
claims.notes: Correctiv: An anonymous TikTok-based decision helper attributed false or grossly distorted positions to non-AfD parties. Multiple parties contested the characterisations. Correctiv confirmed the tool systematically misrepresented positions on migration, security and climate to make the AfD appear as the only reasonable choice. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Official statistics prove that knife attacks in Germany have risen exponentially since 2015 — the government is hiding the true scale of migrant violence. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Used in run-up to 2024 Thüringen/Sachsen state elections
claims.notes: Correctiv: AUF1 used PKS data selectively. The PKS records knife-related crimes under broad categories that include non-violent offences. Year-on-year comparisons are distorted by changes in recording methods. The PKS does not record migration background. Independent criminologists stated that the framing of 'exponential growth attributable to migration' was not supported by the available data. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The AfD's Einzelfallticker (individual case ticker) proves a systematic connection between migration and crime in Germany. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: The Einzelfallticker selectively catalogues crimes where a suspect has a non-German name or migration background, ignoring crimes by German perpetrators. This creates a misleading impression of a causal relationship between migration and crime. Criminologists noted that PKS data show crimes are committed at higher rates in areas of social deprivation — an economic correlate, not an ethnic one. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Russia disinformation: Fake anti-Ukraine billboards in New York, fabricated documents about Zelensky's yachts and villas — amplified by AfD-adjacent channels as authentic. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: AFP's 2024 analysis documented systematic Russian-origin disinformation spread via German-language social media channels, including more than 50,000 fake X accounts identified by the German Foreign Ministry. The Operation Doppelgänger network (attributed to Russia by French intelligence) produced the fake Zelensky wealth documents and fake anti-Ukraine billboard photos, which then circulated widely in AfD-adjacent social media channels targeting German audiences. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Remigration of millions of people is a legitimate and lawful policy objective — the AfD's social media campaign around this term is democratic political discourse. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv analysis: The AfD's remigration social media content systematically promoted ethnically-based deportation narratives. The Bundesverwaltungsgericht characterised Sellner's remigration concept (presented at the Potsdam meeting) as targeting German citizens and as unconstitutional. The campaign used coded language to spread the demographic replacement narrative while maintaining a veneer of legality. (translated from German)

claims.claim: The WHO Pandemic Treaty will give the WHO power to override national governments and lock down entire countries — a global power grab that strips citizens of rights. (translated from German)
claims.notes: AFP Faktencheck: The WHO has no enforcement power over national governments. The proposed pandemic accord is a non-binding international agreement; WHO Director-General stated explicitly that no country would be required to cede sovereignty. Anderson's claim was part of a coordinated European right-wing messaging campaign against the WHO accord. (translated from German)

claims.claim: More than 60 per cent of Germany's electricity now comes from wind power — wind turbines are destabilising the grid. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv: Fraunhofer ISE data for 2024 shows wind power generated approximately 37 per cent of German electricity — not over 60 per cent. Krah's figure was roughly 60 per cent too high. The grid stability claim was also contested by energy engineers. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Germany has the highest energy costs in the world, making our industry uncompetitive. Nuclear power is CO2-neutral. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: Germany is not the most expensive energy country globally. As of February 2025, Germany ranked 19th for petrol prices, 21st for diesel and 19th for heating oil. On nuclear power, the Federal Environment Ministry and Scientists for Future confirm nuclear is not CO2-neutral — the fuel manufacturing process is energy-intensive and emits 3.5 times more CO2 per kWh than photovoltaics. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Germany has brought in almost 7 million people — the officially counted ones — since Merkel opened the borders in 2015 without democratic consent. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Made during high-profile X conversation with Elon Musk watched by millions globally
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: The 7 million figure conflates all migration (including skilled workers, EU citizens, students) with asylum migration. Destatis data confirms total net migration since 2015, but approximately 3 million asylum applications were lodged — not 7 million irregular arrivals. Germany's borders were not 'opened' — Schengen free movement already existed; a temporary border control was imposed with Austria within days. (translated from German)

claims.claim: AfD press releases consistently show accurate crime statistics — foreign suspects commit crimes disproportionate to their population share. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Used heavily in pre-election period for Thüringen and Sachsen 2024 elections
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder (analysis accompanying Göttingen University research project): AfD press releases systematically omit the demographic caveat in PKS data — namely that non-German suspects include tourists and transit passengers (not resident in Germany) in the numerator but not the denominator. This inflates the apparent foreign crime rate. PKS does not record migration background. The Göttingen research project reviewed 2,500 AfD press releases and identified systematic statistical misrepresentation. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Climate change is not caused by human activity — CO2 is not the driver of global warming, it is a natural cycle. (translated from German)
claims.platform: Campaign material / social media (European election campaign)
claims.impact: Spread during 2024 European Parliament election campaign
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: The scientific consensus from IPCC, DWD, WMO and all major German scientific institutions is that human-caused greenhouse gas emissions, primarily CO2, are the dominant cause of current global warming. AfD climate denial claims contradict this consensus and were used in their EU election campaign material. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Human-caused climate change is not proven science — climate policy is ideology-driven and is destroying Germany's economy. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundestagswahl 2025 TV debate
claims.notes: Tagesschau faktenfinder: 97+ per cent of active climate scientists agree that warming is primarily human-caused. German research institutions DWD, PIK and Fraunhofer confirm this. Weidel's 'not proven' framing contradicts peer-reviewed IPCC reports endorsed by 195 governments. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Covid-19 vaccines are causing a mass die-off — a British study proves that vaccinated people will never regain full immunity again. (translated from German)
claims.notes: Correctiv 2022 annual review: The 'never again full immunity' claim was based on a British study that was misrepresented. Lower antibody levels in vaccinated individuals reflect a milder disease course — not permanent immune impairment. This narrative was one of the five most persistent German disinformation narratives of 2022, heavily amplified through AfD-adjacent channels and messaging apps. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Germany shut down nuclear power plants while Europe's safest energy source — nuclear — is CO2-free and provides stable baseload power that renewables cannot replace. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Bundestagswahl 2025 campaign claim
claims.notes: Correctiv: Nuclear energy is not CO2-free — fuel manufacturing emits CO2 at 3.5× the rate of photovoltaics per kWh (Scientists for Future / Federal Environment Ministry). Fraunhofer-Institut energy researchers note that reinstating nuclear in Germany would not reduce electricity prices, as power pricing is determined by marginal cost (gas-fired plants) in the European energy market, not average generation cost. (translated from German)
