claims.claim: Remigration of millions with migration backgrounds is both legally possible and already planned by the AfD for implementation — denial that German citizens are targeted is dishonest. (translated from German)
claims.impact: Central remigration narrative sustained throughout 2024-2025 election cycle
claims.notes: Correctiv two-year review of Potsdam research (January 2026): AfD politicians continued to publicly deny the Potsdam meeting discussed deporting German citizens, but Gerrit Huy (AfD MP present) confirmed she had been pursuing remigration plans for years. The Bundesverwaltungsgericht in Leipzig characterised Sellner's remigration concept as targeting citizens and as unconstitutional. The AfD's public denial was contradicted by internal statements and the testimony of participants. (translated from German)

claims.claim: Only 8% of those who landed illegally got the right to asylum or international protection (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica found the actual protection share was ~40%; Meloni conflated different legal categories. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Irregular landings have created an unsustainable situation in terms of security (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Istat data showed crime had fallen sharply in Italy over the preceding decade; no causal link to migrant arrivals. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: In 2017 the EU proposed a naval blockade to stop boats leaving for Italy (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The EU proposed cooperation with Libya but never a naval blockade; the claim mischaracterises the 2017 EU-Libya deal. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Thanks to the Superbonus 110%, six castles were renovated at a total cost of one billion euros (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Actual spending on the six castles was around one million euros not one billion; Meloni overstated by a factor of 1000. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The budget law was approved in record time without a confidence vote; the last time this happened was in 2011 (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Previous governments had also passed budget laws without confidence votes after 2011; the claim is historically inaccurate. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: There were 17 billion euros of fraud linked to the Superbonus 110% (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Agenzia delle Entrate documented ~15 billion euros in fraudulent credits; most were sequestered or blocked and did not disappear. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: TG1 presence: I am last in ranking compared to my predecessors (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Meloni cited only presence time data; total coverage metric shows she ranked highest among recent prime ministers. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The UN says human trafficking has become more profitable than arms and is equalling drug trafficking (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Drug trafficking exceeds 300 billion USD; trafficking in persons is estimated at 7-32 billion USD. The UN has never made this claim in the form Meloni stated. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy is the most advanced country in Europe for PNRR disbursement rates (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Portugal and Croatia had also reached the same number of disbursement rates; Italy was not uniquely ahead. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Thanks to the EU-Tunisia deal departures on Mediterranean central routes fell 60% (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The drop in landings cannot be attributed solely to the Tunisia deal; overall landings in 2024 were still higher than in 2022. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: In 2019 it was the first time FdI obtained a result that put us safe from any electoral threshold (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: FdI had cleared the electoral threshold in the 2013 and 2018 elections as well. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy's public debt is returning to Italian hands thanks to the success of BTP Valore (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Foreign-held share of Italian debt remained stable at ~28%; BTP Valore sales did not meaningfully shift debt ownership. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The Cassation court ruled that judges cannot systematically disapply migrant detention from safe countries (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica found Meloni's characterisation of the Cassation ruling was inaccurate; the ruling did not carry the meaning she attributed. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Some judges still disapplied migrant detention even after the Cassation sentence (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Catania tribunal on 4 January 2025 had actually cited the Cassation ruling in support of its decision not against it. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: In 2022 about 4 per 100 illegal migrants were deported; today at least 35 per 100 are deported (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: In 2022 the rate was ~2.6/100; in 2024 it was ~7.2/100 — far below the 35 claimed by Meloni. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: All projects in the 9 pilot countries of the Mattei Plan have already started (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Only 5 of 17 projects had started; 11 more were planned to start in 2025. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy is the 4th country in the world for exports (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Technically true for H1 2024 by value but goods exports fell 1.1% compared to 2023; the framing omitted a declining trend. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: There are no pardons in our budget law (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The budget law contained a tregua fiscale (tax amnesty) allowing taxpayers to settle outstanding debts at reduced rates. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: After Greece no one has ever activated the ESM (European Stability Mechanism) (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Spain and Cyprus also received ESM assistance — Meloni omitted two documented cases. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The Conte left government released mafia bosses from 41-bis solitary confinement (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Cura Italia emergency decree did not apply to 41-bis inmates; releases cited by Meloni were under pre-existing rules unrelated to that decree. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED

claims.claim: In the last 10 years tax evasion was lowest when the cash transaction limit was 5000 euros (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The 2010 evasion data cited is partial and methodologically incomparable to later years; causal link between cash limit and evasion is unproven. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy grows more than the Eurozone more than France and Germany according to EU Commission estimates (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Eurostat Q2 2024 data showed Italy at +0.2% vs Eurozone/France at +0.3%; Italy actually grew less than the Eurozone average. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: If the South grows more than the national average it is thanks to this government (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Svimez analysts attributed southern Italy growth primarily to PNRR disbursements and EU cohesion funds not government action per se. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy is the 4th nation in the world for exports this year (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Italian goods export values fell 1.1% in H1 2024 vs same period 2023; the ranking claim obscures a declining trend. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: I did not promise in the electoral campaign that I would cut fuel excise taxes (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: FdI's 2022 electoral programme explicitly promised automatic reduction of VAT and excise taxes on fuels; Meloni's denial contradicts the party's own programme. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The government did not backtrack on the previous government's fuel excise tax cut (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Open Fact-check documented that the Meloni government first confirmed then significantly reduced the previous government's fuel tax cut in November 2022. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The EU is complicit in uncontrolled immigration the invasion of Europe and the ethnic replacement project wanted by big capital and international speculators (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica documented this as part of recurring ethnic replacement narrative by Meloni; demographic data does not support the framing. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: George Soros supports mass immigration and the ethnic replacement plan across the world (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Soros plan narrative is a documented antisemitic conspiracy theory; Pagella Politica contextualised its use and noted it lacks factual basis. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Uncontrolled mass immigration is destroying our European and Christian identity (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica documented this as part of Meloni's recurring great-replacement narrative; demographic data does not support the framing. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: A 2015 TV segment showed Chinese scientists manufactured a super virus from coronavirus; relevant to COVID-19 origins (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The 2015 Rai segment discussed gain-of-function research entirely unrelated to SARS-CoV-2; amplifying it as COVID-19 evidence was false context. Meloni submitted an urgent parliamentary question. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Lollobrigida's ethnic replacement remarks were a normal observation about demographics misrepresented by the left (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica documented that Meloni herself had used sostituzione etnica language at least three times on Twitter between 2016 and 2018 prior to Lollobrigida's remarks. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: In Italian prisons there are 20000 immigrants for drugs sexual violence theft and pickpocketing (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The overall number of foreign inmates was roughly correct but the crime breakdown was wrong; sexual violence was not the top offence category. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: In Italy immigrants commit about 700 crimes daily corresponding to a third of the total (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: ~700 foreigners arrested or reported daily is the figure; one-third ratio broadly correct but framing inflates crime attribution to immigration. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Until last year 200000 per year were landing; they were paid 35 euros a day each and the sector pocketed 6 billion euros (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The 200000 figure the 35-euro daily rate and the 6-billion-euro total were all either wrong or severely decontextualised. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Wisconsin had more votes than registered voters — direct proof of fraud in the US 2020 election (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Wisconsin claim was false; Wisconsin voter registration and total votes were consistent with legal provisional and same-day registration processes. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Today in the Giunta the League senators could have saved me and blocked my trial for Gregoretti (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: League senators on the committee did not have procedural power to unilaterally block the referral; rules required a majority across parties. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — relates to Gregoretti criminal proceedings.

claims.claim: Immigration is only a fifth of the content of the Security Decrees (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica calculated that immigration provisions accounted for between one quarter and one third of the Security Decrees not one fifth. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — relates to Gregoretti/Open Arms proceedings.

claims.claim: Deaths in the Mediterranean fell from 15000 to 2000 thanks to our policies (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The 15000 figure overestimates pre-Salvini deaths; actual 2015-2017 total ~10584. Moreover the death rate per arrival increased during Salvini's tenure. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED

claims.claim: Since we have been in government only two people died at sea because the fewer who leave the fewer die (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: IOM data showed hundreds of deaths in the Mediterranean in 2019 under Salvini as Interior Minister; the claim was factually false. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: 2019 when I was Interior Minister was the year with fewest deaths (754) in the Mediterranean in the last 10 years (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Fewer people crossed in 2019 but the mortality rate per crossing increased; the 754 figure also omits deaths on other routes. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: We gave Italians confidence and abolished the Fornero Law (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Fornero pension reform law was never abolished; Quota 100 was a temporary partial measure that expired in 2021. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Quota 100 means a million people will retire and young people will take their place (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Labour economists found no automatic job-substitution effect; retirement of older workers does not directly create equivalent openings for young people. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Quota 100 allowed 300000 Italians to retire early and an equal number of young people to start working (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: INPS data showed no equivalent creation of 300000 new youth jobs; early retirements did not translate into a like-for-like increase in youth employment. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The Left in Parliament decided that defending Italian borders is a crime (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Open Arms prosecution was requested by the Tribunal of Ministers of Palermo not by Parliament or the political left. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active Open Arms trial.

claims.claim: Open Arms arrived in front of Sicilian coasts on 20 August with 164 migrants on board (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Open Arms arrived around 14-15 August 2019 not 20 August; passenger count at end of standoff was 120 not 164. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active Open Arms trial.

claims.claim: During navigation we always ensured the sick pregnant women and minors disembarked from Open Arms (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Court documents and contemporaneous reporting show disembarkation of minors was blocked for multiple days. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active Open Arms trial.

claims.claim: During the Draghi government not a single NGO ship with migrants on board arrived in Italy (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Multiple NGO vessels landed migrants in Italy during the Draghi government; Facta documented the arrivals. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: No NGO ship in front of Libya means no departure no landing no deaths (May 2019 map post) (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The map showed a single frozen moment; deaths and departures were documented by IOM in adjacent days. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Roma camps don't exist anywhere else in Europe; I don't see why they should exist in Italy (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Informal Roma settlements exist in numerous EU countries including France Romania Bulgaria and Hungary. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Real refugees arrive by plane; migrants who land illegally are not real refugees (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The majority of individuals who received refugee protection in Italy at the time had arrived by sea not by air. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Either we stop the ethnic replacement project desired by the Left or soon WE will be the refugees! (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica documented the tweet as part of Salvini's recurring sostituzione etnica narrative which is unsupported by demographic evidence. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: This is another case of ethnic replacement in progress: Italians have fewer children and foreigners are offered everything (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Istat projections show immigration does not numerically replace Italian births even at double projected flows; the ethnic replacement framing lacks factual basis. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Migrants arriving are creating COVID-19 outbreaks and spreading the virus through Italy (March 2020 Facebook post) (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: EU Disinfo Lab research cited by Pagella Politica found Lega MPs responsible for 54% of political COVID disinformation in Italy in 2020; INMP data showed migrant COVID rates ~4x lower than Italian resident population. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Migrants arriving during COVID are responsible for reinfecting Italy (November 2020 Facebook post) (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: INMP data showed newly arrived migrants in 2020-21 had COVID infection rates roughly four times lower than the Italian resident population. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Repatriations are up 28% since I became Interior Minister (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Garante Nazionale data showed monthly forced repatriations slightly decreased from 2017 to 2018; the 28% figure came from unverified alternative sources. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: France has also put forward a bill modelled on Italy's to ban synthetic meat (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The French bill had different scope and was not described by its sponsors as following Italy's model. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: We cannot surrender to the idea of ethnic replacement: Italians are having fewer children so we replace them with someone else — that is not the way forward (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Istat projections show immigration does not numerically replace Italian births even at double projected flows; Istat president stated at same event that ethnic replacement framing is factually incorrect. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: There is no Italian race but there is an Italian ethnicity and culture we must defend (translated from Italian)
claims.platform: Rally speech (Stati Generali della Natalità - Rome)
claims.notes: Istat demographic data presented at the same event showed population decline would occur regardless of immigration levels; Lollobrigida's framing implies a false demographic threat. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: EU military forces are composed of only 5000 men in total while Italy alone has 9000 soldiers deployed abroad (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: EU unified military forces had not yet been formally created at the time and therefore had no fixed headcount; La Russa cited a non-existent number for a non-existent force. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: 70% of the world's rare earths are sold by China (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: China dominates rare earth processing and refining but the 70% sales figure conflates different stages of the supply chain. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy has more law enforcement per hundred inhabitants than most European countries (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Result depends heavily on which personnel categories are counted; Pagella Politica found the claim misleading without full context. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The twelfth transitional provision of the Italian Constitution only bans re-founding the specific dissolved fascist party not antifascism in general (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Constitutional Court has interpreted the twelfth provision broadly to prohibit any form of fascist re-organisation; Vannacci's reading was found misleading by Pagella Politica. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: With the Meloni government there have been fewer repatriations than under the Renzi government (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica verified this claim as correct: Meloni government repatriated 12535 in 2023-2025 vs Renzi government 15695 in 2014-2016. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Government brochure: Italy has surpassed EU average in growth tax cuts are working crime is down — at least 12 claims wrong or missing key context (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica found at least 12 errors or important omissions in FdI's official results brochure. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Record landings in January: already 841 from start of year (+15% vs last year). 183681 migrants in hotels. INVASION (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Over full-year 2017 landings had fallen 34%; the hotel figure conflated different reception categories. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Direct clashes between migrants and police began on the island of Lampedusa; illegal immigrants throwing stones at carabinieri (video September 2023)
claims.notes: AFP verified via InVID that footage actually showed a 2021 brawl outside a nightclub in Marotta central Italy — unrelated to Lampedusa. Salvini himself had confirmed the Marotta location at the time.

claims.claim: Italy's government has begun dumping migrants at the borders of Vatican City in retaliation for Pope Francis calling migration refusal a grave sin
claims.notes: AFP found the aerial photo used as evidence was from a 2013 papal audience; Vatican and Italian media confirmed no such transfers occurred.

claims.claim: Italy dumped migrants at the Vatican after Pope Francis criticism (Reuters check)
claims.notes: Reuters independently confirmed the claim false; Vatican spokesperson clarified the circulating image was from a 2013 papal general audience. Originating X account described itself as satire.

claims.claim: AI-generated video: Italian PM Meloni terminated all agreements with Israel and refused to shake Netanyahu's hand at a UN meeting
claims.notes: AFP ran clips through DeepFake-o-Meter; multiple AI detection models flagged both videos as synthetic. 27 Italy-Israel agreements remain in place; no such meeting occurred.

claims.claim: Italian Health Minister Roberto Speranza is under investigation for murder for concealing COVID-19 vaccination deaths
claims.notes: La Verità's November 2023 report was the origin of the false international amplification; Rome prosecutor had already requested dismissal by 23 November 2023. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — active legal proceedings context.

claims.claim: Giorgia Meloni said: My three salaries? Just over 72 basic incomes per month — what's the problem? (viral meme) (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Open Fact-check confirmed Meloni never made this statement; the meme was fabricated and circulated falsely attributed to her. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Photo of a young man before a communist flag identified as the magistrate who investigated Salvini (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Photo shows Said Chaibi a 19-year-old student activist — not a judge. Pro-Vannacci groups amplified the false identification. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — false identification of named private individual.

claims.claim: Photo of Matteo Salvini holding a racist sign against Neapolitans and Sicilians
claims.notes: Facta identified the image as digitally manipulated; the sign was inserted via image editing.

claims.claim: Giorgia Meloni is under investigation for vote-buying in Naples (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Facta found no judicial record of any such investigation; the claim was entirely fabricated. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — defamation risk.

claims.claim: Tweet attributed to Giorgia Meloni calling the arrest of Carabinieri in Piacenza a judicial set-up is real (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Facta verified the tweet was fabricated; the account was fake and Meloni never posted it. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Photo of Giorgia Meloni wearing a Roma Pride T-shirt
claims.notes: Facta confirmed the image was digitally altered; Meloni was wearing a different garment.

claims.claim: Giorgia Meloni called for a naval blockade off the coast of Afghanistan (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Facta confirmed Meloni made no such proposal; the claim was fabricated. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Fake news report: Giorgia Meloni earns colossal sums thanks to an Elon Musk app
claims.notes: Facta identified artificial lip movements indicating AI-generation; no such investment app arrangement exists.

claims.claim: The story of the Moroccan who accumulates Italian welfare benefits is true (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Facta found the story was a fabrication; no such individual existed. The article was later corrected for a separate factual error on the Bossi-Fini law date. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Claims on immigration and budget achievements in Gli Appunti di Giorgia episode 3 — some errors found (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica fact-checked 10 claims from Meloni's own video series; some were correct others contained errors on immigration and budget data. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy is the most advanced country in Europe for PNRR rates (parliament speech) (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Same claim fact-checked multiple times; other EU states had equivalent PNRR disbursement progress. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Real refugees arrive by plane and not by sea; those landing by boat are economic migrants not entitled to protection (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Italian Interior Ministry data showed the majority of individuals receiving refugee status at the time had arrived by sea. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Roma camps are a uniquely Italian phenomenon not found elsewhere in Europe (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Documented Roma informal camps exist in France Romania Bulgaria Hungary and other EU states. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy kept fuel price rises lower than France and Germany thanks to our fuel tax cut policy (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Open Fact-check showed the excise cut was reduced by the Meloni government from November 2022; the differential with France/Germany was partly attributable to pre-existing measures. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Footage shows migrants attacking Italian police at Lampedusa during the September 2023 migration surge
claims.notes: AFP confirmed the footage was from a 2021 brawl in Marotta; amplified widely in September 2023 during the Lampedusa spike.

claims.claim: Italy is among the countries most advanced on PNRR in Europe — we are leading (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Multiple EU states had comparable or better PNRR disbursement rates at the time. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy has recovered about 100 billion euros in three years in the fight against tax evasion (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Fact-checkers noted that the figure encompasses multiple definitional categories of recovered revenue including declared arrears; the framing overstates enforcement success. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Migrants at Lampedusa are stealing cars motorbikes and fishing boats from locals (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The mayor of Lampedusa and investigators denied the claims; no evidence supported Salvini's account. Source: Pagella Politica 2022 immigration overview. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy has introduced a Family Pride Month in June (viral claim circulating internationally)
claims.notes: EDMO and AFP confirmed no such government initiative existed; a ProVita pro-life demonstration was misrepresented. The AP and multiple international fact-checkers debunked the claim.

claims.claim: Record landings in January: 841 arrivals +15% from last year; 183681 migrants kept in hotels — INVASION organised to cancel our culture (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Over full-year 2017 landings fell 34%; the hotel figure conflated different reception categories. The tweet was selected cherry-picking. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Foreigners commit one third of all crimes in Italy — about 700 crimes every day (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: ~700 foreigners arrested or reported daily is roughly the data; one-third ratio broadly correct but framing inflates crime attributable to immigration. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Thanks to Superbonus 110% there were 17 billion euros of fraud (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Documented fraud was ~15 billion euros; most was sequestered not spent. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: In 2019 it was the first time FdI passed the electoral threshold (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: FdI also passed the threshold in 2013 and 2018 elections. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The EU army only has 5000 troops while Italy deploys 9000 abroad (second occasion citing same false figure) (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Title of Pagella Politica article notes La Russa repeated this incorrect claim on multiple occasions. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Story of a Moroccan man who collects multiple Italian welfare benefits circulates as real (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Facta found the story was a fabrication; the legal reference cited was also wrong (Bossi-Fini law date). (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: We did not promise to cut fuel excise taxes in the 2022 electoral campaign (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: FdI's 2022 programme explicitly promised automatic reduction of VAT and excise taxes on fuels. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: We must incentivise births to stop Italians from being numerically replaced by foreigners (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Istat president Blangiardo stated at the same event that even doubling projected immigration levels would not reverse Italy's population decline; the replacement framing is factually unfounded. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: All Mattei Plan projects in the 9 pilot countries have already started (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Only 5 of 17 projects had started; 11 more were planned to start in 2025. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: I always allowed vulnerable migrants to disembark from Open Arms (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Court documents show disembarkation of unaccompanied minors was blocked for multiple days during the Open Arms standoff. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED

claims.claim: I defended Italian borders and everything I did as Interior Minister was lawful (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Multiple specific statistical claims in the Senate speech were found incorrect; the broader claim of legality was contested in active proceedings. (translated from Italian). LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED

claims.claim: Italy was the first to ban synthetic meat production and has become a model for Europe (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica found that France's bill was independently drafted and not modelled on Italy's; Lollobrigida's claim (repeated by party officials including Meloni) overstated Italy's influence. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The reception system costs Italy 6 billion euros per year with migrants receiving 35 euros per day (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Both the 35-euro figure and the 6-billion total were based on inflated or misapplied statistics; official government data showed lower per-capita costs. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy does not have more tax evaders than other comparable EU countries — we are not uniquely bad (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica found multiple claims in this press conference were false or misleading including on tax evasion comparisons. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: FdI brochure: Italy has cut taxes for workers and families while maintaining budget discipline (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Pagella Politica noted the cuneo fiscale reduction was only for 2023 and did not represent permanent reform; the framing overstated the benefit. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: The Superbonus 110% led to massive fraud that cost ordinary Italians billions (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: While fraud existed, ~8.6bn of 15bn in fraudulent credits was preventively seized; Meloni's framing implied all 17bn had been lost to citizens. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Courts gave the government right on safe countries for migrant returns (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The Cassation ruling was mischaracterised; courts continued to rule against the government's safe country designations in subsequent weeks. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Italy is leading Europe in PNRR implementation (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Portugal and Croatia matched Italy on disbursement rates; the claim of unique European leadership was not supported by Commission data. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Quota 100 will create a million new jobs for young Italians replacing retiring workers (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: Labour economists documented no automatic substitution effect; the INPS later showed much lower than projected job creation from Quota 100 retirements. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: There are 20000 foreign nationals in Italian prisons for drug crimes sexual violence theft and pickpocketing (translated from Italian)
claims.notes: The overall foreign inmate number was approximately right but the crime profile was wrong; drug offences dominated not sexual violence. (translated from Italian)

claims.claim: Women murdered in Spain were, mostly, at the hands of foreigners
claims.notes: Newtral found official femicide data showed the majority of women killed in Spain are murdered by Spanish nationals. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: individual crime attribution.

claims.claim: False [gender-violence] complaints affect millions of Spaniards
claims.notes: Newtral found no data supporting the 'millions' figure; official statistics do not show mass false complaints. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: There are 86% of archived [gender-violence] complaints
claims.notes: Newtral found no official source substantiating the 86% archiving rate for gender-violence complaints. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: We want to return to the State the competencies in healthcare, education and justice
claims.notes: Newtral found that returning these competencies requires constitutional reform, not merely statutory reforms of regional statutes as Abascal implied. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Since the manada there have been 100+ more in Spain; 70% of those accused are foreigners
claims.notes: Maldita found no official data showing 70% of gang-rape accusados are foreigners; the Interior Ministry data contradicts this. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED: crime attribution.

claims.claim: Housing-aid legislation discriminates against Spaniards with arraigo
claims.notes: Maldita found the law in question did not discriminate by nationality; income criteria applied equally. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Some MENAs earn, on occasion, more than our pensioners / our widows
claims.notes: Maldita found unaccompanied minors do not personally receive cash; public funding goes to the organisations caring for them. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Madrid created 200 more places for unaccompanied minors
claims.notes: Maldita found the Community of Madrid had not created 200 new places; the figure was not supported by official data. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Members of the 'manada de Manresa' were unaccompanied minors (MENAs)
claims.notes: Maldita found the accused in the Manresa case were not unaccompanied minors but adults. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: You can come to Madrid from anywhere in the world to have an operation and that bill is not sent to your country of origin
claims.notes: Maldita found EU bilateral agreements and international conventions mean Spain can seek reimbursement from other countries in many cases. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: After jumping the Melilla fence, automatically you are given a universal healthcare card
claims.notes: Maldita found no universal health card is issued automatically; migrants must go through administrative procedures. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: A foreign unaccompanied minor receives 664 euros [per month] / a widow receives 360 euros
claims.impact: 2019 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the 664 euro figure was fabricated; unaccompanied minors do not personally receive cash. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The EU gives more money to regions that have registered a higher number of [gender-violence] complaints, thus creating an incentive for false complaints
claims.notes: Maldita found EU structural funds to Spain's regions are not tied to gender-violence complaint figures. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: More than 60 children are killed by their mothers every year
claims.notes: Maldita found official Ministry of Interior data showed far fewer children killed by their mothers; the 60+ figure was unsubstantiated. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: 30 men die per year [at hands of intimate partners], but these figures have not been given since 2005
claims.notes: Maldita found that the Interior Ministry has published male intimate-partner homicide data regularly since 2013; the 30 per year figure was not supported. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: One MENA [unaccompanied minor], 4,700 euros per month. Your grandmother, 426 euros pension per month
claims.platform: TV broadcast (El programa de Ana Rosa); campaign poster
claims.impact: 2021 Madrid regional elections campaign
claims.notes: Maldita and Newtral found 4,700 euros is the cost per placement to care organisations, not cash given to the minor; Spanish children in care also cost the same. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: One MENA [unaccompanied minor], 4,700 euros per month. Your grandmother, 426 euros pension per month
claims.platform: TV broadcast (El programa de Ana Rosa); campaign poster
claims.impact: 2021 Madrid regional elections campaign
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed 4,700 euros is the operational cost per placement paid to host organisations, not a personal allowance; costs ranged from 1,920 to 5,762 euros depending on the centre. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: We [Vox] have been the only party that has always opposed the state of alarm
claims.notes: Newtral found that Vox initially supported the first state of alarm extension before later opposing extensions. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Community of Madrid has its public housing park occupied [by squatters]
claims.impact: 2021 Madrid regional elections campaign
claims.notes: Newtral found official Community of Madrid data showed very few public housing units were squatted; the generalisation was not supported. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: A simple examination of comparative law shows that [sedition/rebellion] are punished with much harsher penalties [in other EU countries]
claims.notes: Newtral found that many EU countries have no direct equivalent offence, or that the Spanish penalties were broadly comparable with EU peers. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: El País has been convicted for lying [about Rocío Monasterio]
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the court ruling only obliged El País to include Monasterio's right of rectification; the judge explicitly did not assess the truthfulness of the original article. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Andalusia has 36% youth unemployment of under-25s. Double the rest of Spain
claims.impact: 2022 Andalusia regional elections campaign
claims.notes: Newtral found that while Andalusia's youth unemployment is high, it was not double the national rate using comparable methodology. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Outdated textbook used to claim Andalusia government approved anti-hunting content in schools]
claims.impact: 2022 Andalusia regional elections campaign
claims.notes: Newtral found the textbook in question was old and not in current use in Andalusia; Olona attributed it to the sitting government without basis. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Vox proposes free sale of weapons in Spain [after Texas Uvalde shooting]
claims.notes: EFE Verifica found Vox's actual position was about using legally-owned firearms at home in self-defence, not the free commercial sale of weapons. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In Spain there is lithium, but [lithium] prospecting is prohibited
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed with the Ministry for Ecological Transition that lithium prospecting is not prohibited; the government was actively promoting it. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Rapes have doubled in Spain since Sánchez came to power
claims.notes: Newtral found rapes had increased but had not doubled; a 2022 methodology change (merging assault categories) also affected comparability. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: If illegal immigration increases since this government arrived, they will have some responsibility
claims.notes: Newtral found that irregular arrivals were lower in 2022 than in 2018 (before the Sánchez government) using Interior Ministry data. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Socialist Party is destroying Spain's hydraulic dams. It has destroyed 133 dams in 2022
claims.notes: Newtral and experts found the 133 were mostly small obsolete sluices and weirs, not functional water-storage dams; Spain's reservoir capacity increased. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In Castilla y León they are building a dam while in the rest of Spain they are knocking them down
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed Castilla y León was constructing one new hydraulic infrastructure, but that Spain as a whole was not demolishing operative dams. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Increase in homicides 47%, Ministry of Interior. Sexual assaults increase 60%. [Implying overall crime rising in Madrid]
claims.impact: 2023 Madrid regional elections campaign
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed both specific figures are correct, but Monasterio omitted that overall conventional crime in Madrid fell 8.7% versus 2019. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: With the patriotism of Bildu, that respectable political force, you have approved the labour reform
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed EH Bildu voted against the labour reform in the definitive 3 February 2022 congressional vote; 174 deputies including Vox voted against. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The climate law is precisely the law that prevents the exploration and exploitation of our own natural and energy resources
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the climate law does not prohibit energy resource exploration as a general rule; some specific extraction projects face environmental restrictions. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Those who pay for these green policies are the most disadvantaged Spaniards, who can no longer enter city centres because they cannot buy an electric car in cities of under 50,000 inhabitants
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found low-emission zones are only required in municipalities over 50,000 inhabitants; as of June 2023 only 10 cities had active zones, and they do not restrict entry exclusively to electric vehicles. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: You have not managed to reduce, with that gender-violence law, the murders of women
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found femicides fell from 71 in 2003 (before the law) to 56 in 2019 and 49 in 2022, contradicting Abascal's claim. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Inflation under his mandate has increased 16% accumulated and in food 31% and 22% in footwear
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed the 16% and 31% figures but found footwear rose 9.21% per INE, not 22% as Abascal claimed. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [The government] dares, in a time of drought, to blow up the dams
claims.notes: Newtral found Spain was removing obsolete weirs and small barriers, not functional dams; reservoir capacity actually increased. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In Catalonia, murders and rapes have increased by 30% in 2023
claims.impact: 2024 Catalonia regional elections campaign
claims.notes: Newtral found homicides rose ~12.7–17.4% (not 30%); rapes did rise ~34% but Garriga conflated both at 30%. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: The EU bureaucrats in Brussels are ultimately responsible for annulling elections in Romania
claims.notes: Newtral found the Romanian Constitutional Court, not the EU, annulled the first-round result; the EU had no formal decision-making role in that annulment. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Photo of Abascal standing alongside Trump, Meloni and Milei is authentic]
claims.notes: Newtral found the image was a digitally manipulated composite; Abascal was not present at the depicted occasion. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: There are 33 defensores del pueblo [ombudsmen] in Spain
claims.notes: Newtral counted the actual number of national and regional ombudsman-type bodies and found it significantly lower than 33. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Photo of Abascal on tiptoe at Patriots summit is authentic]
claims.notes: EFE Verifica compared with Vox's own original photo and found shoes and foot position had been digitally altered from a separate November 2024 image. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Illegal immigration is now the top concern of Spaniards, above housing
claims.notes: Newtral found that in February 2025 CIS data, immigration ranked sixth; housing had led for three consecutive months. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [The Mannheim attacker was] a Saudi whose activity was bringing Saudis to Europe — another terrorist attack
claims.notes: Newtral found the attacker was Afghan, not Saudi; no evidence supported the characterisation of 'Saudis to Europe'. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: What is that thing that people won't be able to go and buy something with cash? [re: digital euro abolishing cash]
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the proposed digital euro would supplement, not replace, cash, and the ECB has explicitly stated cash would remain legal tender. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In 2022 the EU received 5.1 million illegal immigrants, more than double 2021, and mostly from Muslim countries
claims.notes: Newtral found Frontex's 2022 figure refers to irregular border crossings not unique individuals, and the religious characterisation was unsupported. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The new immigration regulations allow regularising migrants with criminal records
claims.notes: AFP Factual found that criminal records still bar regularisation; only police records without judicial conviction are not automatically disqualifying. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: We expect 600,000 [illegal immigrants] to enter [Spain] again this year, that's the forecast
claims.notes: Newtral found 600,000 is an estimate of total irregular immigrants already in Spain, not annual arrivals; actual 2025 irregular arrivals were 36,775 per Interior Ministry. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Abascal] promises to send 'Spanish volunteers' to fight with Israel if he governs in 2027
claims.platform: Viral social media post (fake El País article)
claims.notes: EFE Verifica found the post was a fabricated El País-style mockup; no record of Abascal making such a promise in any official channel. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The State takes a daughter from her parents. The MENAs rape her. And the solution the State gives is to suicide her [re: Noelia Castillo euthanasia case]
claims.notes: EFE Verifica found no evidence Castillo was assaulted by unaccompanied minors while in care; the assaults she described occurred after she left care at 18, and she did not name nationalities of attackers. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: In the last five years [...] we have the same number of housing, police and hospitals and fewer doctors
claims.notes: Newtral found that police and doctors per 1,000 inhabitants both increased over the period; housing and hospital stock slightly decreased per capita. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Abascal] proposed reducing pensions and ending Imserso travel programmes
claims.platform: Viral social media post (fake 'VHN PLUS' outlet)
claims.notes: EFE Verifica found no record of Abascal making these statements; the attributed outlet 'VHN PLUS' has no verifiable online presence. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Maghrebi immigrants perpetrate more than 70% of violent crimes in the Basque Country despite being only 1.7% of the population
claims.notes: AFP Factual found the claim misuses arrest data — not convictions — and does not account for population age structure or enforcement bias. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: [Video dated 30 May 2026] shows a mass crossing of migrants at the Ceuta or Melilla fence
claims.platform: Facebook post; X/Twitter post; Instagram post
claims.notes: EFE Verifica confirmed via reverse image search the footage was from the 2 March 2022 Melilla crossing; Spanish authorities confirmed no such event occurred on 30 May 2026. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Immigrant families receive more than 2,000 euros a month in public aid just for being foreign
claims.platform: X/Twitter post; Facebook post; Instagram post
claims.notes: EFE Verifica confirmed with the Ministry of Inclusion that no aids exist based solely on nationality; IMV figures in the viral image were inflated and eligibility requires legal residency and income criteria. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The power blackout in Spain and Portugal was caused by European sanctions against Russia
claims.notes: EFE Verifica found the attributed outlets (The Independent) did not publish such a statement; the claim was spread by Tsargrad TV and Matrioska/Pravda-linked accounts. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Vox campaign image in Valladolid was digitally cloned to inflate crowd numbers]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the distortions were caused by panoramic mode on a mobile phone, not digital manipulation. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Vox Valladolid acto image] — Orange is sponsoring Vox's banner on Calle Alcalá in Madrid
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed Orange did not sponsor Vox; the orange colour came from Vox's own party branding. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Vox anti-okupa law was approved in the Community of Valencia on 15 June 2023
claims.impact: 2023 Valencia regional elections context
claims.notes: Maldita found the new Valencian Parliament had not yet constituted when the claim circulated; the outgoing government was PSOE-Compromís. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Images of Vox Madrid housing-aid poster insinuating most recipients are foreign nationals]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the provisional beneficiary list was used out of context; housing aids are income-based not nationality-based. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: We have demonstrated that we can improve education, as in Castilla y León, obtaining the best results at national level
claims.notes: Newtral found Castilla y León led PISA rankings before Vox joined the regional government in 2022; the improvement predated Vox's participation. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Primero llenaremos las urnas de votos y luego las cunetas de independentistas [First we'll fill the ballot boxes with votes and then the ditches with independentists]
claims.impact: 2019 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the widely-shared claim that Abascal said this is a fabrication; no recording or verified transcript supports it. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Viral post claiming Abascal said] 'quiere acabar con los pensionistas de una puta vez' [wants to be done with pensioners]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found no source or recording confirming Abascal made this statement. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Photo of Abascal holding a megaphone upside down at Oviedo event]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the image was digitally manipulated; original photos from the same event show the megaphone correctly oriented. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Photo of Abascal kissing the tomb of Franco]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the photo originated in May 2018 as a fabricated montage and was recirculated before the 2023 elections. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Accusation against a Moroccan national as presumed killer of a taxi driver in Alcalá de Henares]
claims.notes: EFE Verifica and VerificaRTVE debunked this October 2024 accusation against the named Moroccan individual as false. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED.

claims.claim: [In Torre Pacheco] a war has broken out and if the Government does not act all of Spain will have to come and defend itself
claims.notes: EFE Verifica / VerificaRTVE investigation flagged this as a most-shared message within a broader disinformation ecosystem around the Torre Pacheco incidents. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Fake communiqué] attributing to the Ayuntamiento de Torre Pacheco a statement blaming migrants for insecurity in the municipality
claims.notes: EFE Verifica found the communiqué was fabricated; the ayuntamiento issued no such statement. Post had not been removed at time of publication. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Government's immigration policy makes Spain the main European entrance of illegal immigrants
claims.notes: EU Fact Check found Spain's share of irregular arrivals fluctuates year to year; Greece topped in 2015 and 2019, Italy in 2016–2017; no consistent 'pull effect' attributable solely to Sánchez government. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Abascal 2022 inflation in footwear: 22%]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found INE data shows footwear prices rose 9.21%, not 22% as Abascal stated; his inflation and food figures were accurate. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: People are working fewer hours and have lower wages
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found average hours per worker fell 2.9%, but average wages rose between 2019 and 2021 per the same period, making the overall claim misleading. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: If a man self-perceives as female and hits his wife, that woman is not protected by [the trans] law
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found Article 46 of the trans law explicitly preserves gender-violence law protections regardless of registered sex change; the claim is misleading. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Teresa Ribera] has demolished dams during her mandate
claims.impact: 2024 EU elections campaign
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed Spain demolished obsolete weirs and small barriers, not functional dams; the characterisation of dam demolition was inaccurate. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Cheste dam being halted deepened the DANA flood consequences in Valencia
claims.notes: Newtral found the Cheste project would not have been operational in time, and its location would not have mitigated the DANA flooding. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.actor: Álvise Pérez (Se Acabó la Fiesta MEP)
claims.claim: Sánchez agreed to bring 250,000 Palestinians and 250,000 Mauritanians after his trip to Israel and Mauritania
claims.notes: Newtral found no evidence of any such agreement from official sources, statements, or reliable media following either trip. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The government was blowing up dams in the middle of a drought [during the 2023 debate]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found reservoir capacity had not fallen due to dam removals; the removed structures were weirs not water-storage dams. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Vox 2019 election campaign spread various immigration bulos: MENA costs, healthcare for illegal immigrants, housing aid]
claims.impact: 2019 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita compiled multiple verified false claims from Abascal and Vox during the April 2019 election campaign. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Summary of false and misleading claims most repeated by politicians in 2024 — Abascal/Vox entries: dams, DANA, immigration]
claims.notes: Newtral year-end report confirmed Vox politicians repeated false claims about dam demolition and flood management throughout 2024. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Summary of top false claims 2025 — Abascal immigration 600k, Fúster immigration top concern, housing/doctors/police, EU elections Romania]
claims.notes: Newtral year-end verification report catalogued over 220 fact-checks in 2025; several attributed to Vox/Abascal on immigration and public services. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: You voted against the law on climate change [addressing PP]
claims.notes: Newtral clarified this was a Podemos MP's claim, not Abascal's — Vox voted against the climate law while PP abstained; the broader Newtral article verifies Vox's anti-climate-law voting record. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Vox electoral poster] listing provisional housing-aid beneficiaries to imply most are foreign nationals
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found the list was provisional and that housing aid is awarded on income criteria, not nationality. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Vox implied Aemet should be abolished, which national media amplified]
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found Vox's programme proposed closing regional meteorological agencies, not the national Aemet. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Abascal: 'La gente trabaja menos horas y tiene salarios más bajos']
claims.impact: 2023 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita found hours per worker fell 2.9% but total national hours and wages both rose; the selective framing was misleading. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [The EU Green Deal / Pacto Verde] prohibits the exploration and exploitation of our natural and energy resources
claims.notes: Newtral found the EU Green Deal does not contain a blanket ban on resource exploration; certain projects face environmental review. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [The EU Green Deal involves] demolishing dams [on rivers across Spain]
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the EU Biodiversity Strategy targets obsolete barriers, not functioning dams; functional reservoirs are not at risk. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [During 2019 election campaign: Vox circulated false claims about MENA welfare payments, crime by immigrants, healthcare for irregular migrants]
claims.impact: 2019 general election campaign
claims.notes: Maldita documented multiple false claims in circulation from Vox-linked sources during the November 2019 election campaign, including the 664-euro MENA figure. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Sexual assaults have multiplied by two in Spain since Sánchez arrived
claims.notes: Newtral found the claim was misleading; a 2022 legal reform merged two assault categories making direct year-on-year comparison difficult, and the actual rise was significant but far below 'doubled'. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Spain is the main destination of illegal immigration in Europe
claims.notes: Newtral noted this claim has been checked multiple times; Frontex data shows EU entry patterns fluctuate year-on-year. (Translated from Spanish.) Source URL is the Newtral Vox tag page; article-level URL not confirmed. [NOTE: source URL is an aggregate tag page — article-level URL not confirmed independently; row flagged for editorial review.]

claims.claim: PP voted against the climate change law
claims.notes: Note: this is a mainstream government-aligned figure, not Vox; included per spec's instruction to log government false claims too. Newtral found PP abstained on the final vote; only Vox voted against. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Increasingly more Spaniards are at risk of extreme poverty [under Sánchez]
claims.notes: Newtral found that all INE poverty indicators actually fell between 2018 and 2024 (AROPE from 27.3% to 25.8%); child poverty did rise but the overall extreme poverty claim is false. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Since 2019 no private employment has been created, only public employment has been created, with private employment being destroyed in Spain
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Newtral found that 534,540 new private-sector Social Security affiliates were added since 2019, representing 64% of total new employment; Abascal omitted this. Claim made in the estado de la nación debate. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Fabricated tweet attributed to Santiago Abascal: 'My total support [sic: apollo] to Israel' with fused Israel-Spain flag
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed no such tweet exists in Abascal's account or Wayback Machine archives; Vox's press office called it a fabricated montage. The fake tweet was spread by @Hespanolazo and viral accounts attacking Abascal, not by Abascal himself. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: 26 dams were demolished between 2020 and 2024 in Valencia, causing the DANA catastrophe
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed no dams, reservoirs, or weirs were demolished in the affected area between 2018 and 2024; demolition narrative was spread virally to blame Sánchez government. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The government is hiding thousands of DANA dead bodies; only 250 bodies officially counted but over 1,800 are missing
claims.notes: Maldita found no evidence of hidden bodies; viral claims about hundreds of corpses in Bonaire car park were false — authorities confirmed the car park was nearly empty. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: There were 1,000 deaths in the DANA in Valencia
claims.notes: Maldita found Aldama provided no evidence; the official death toll was 227. Aldama's claim was part of broader conspiracy framing about Pedro Sánchez covering up deaths. (Translated from Spanish.) LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — named individual making unsupported death-toll claim.

claims.claim: NGOs (Cruz Roja, Cáritas) were not working in Valencia, were blocking aid, and were keeping donations; Cáritas prioritised migrants over DANA victims
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed Cruz Roja had 250 volunteers mobilised from day one; Cáritas denied all accusations and stated it has protocols to assist all people. The narrative was amplified by Vox-aligned accounts. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The DANA was caused by the HAARP project / geoingeniería (weather engineering); the Valencia floods were artificially created
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed HAARP and weather engineering cannot cause a DANA; no evidence provided in any of the viral posts making this claim. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The DANA was artificially created to destroy crops and enable photovoltaic solar plants to be installed in the affected areas
claims.notes: Maldita found no evidence that solar plants will be installed in affected areas, and no technology can create or direct a DANA. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Pedro Sánchez said '45,000 victims' referring to the DANA — implying a massive hidden death toll
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed Sánchez was referring to UN estimates for Gaza deaths since 2023, not DANA victims; the video was taken out of context. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Dams and reservoirs were being demolished in Spain under Sánchez, and this worsened the DANA's effects
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed most demolished structures were obsolete or abandoned azudes (small weirs), not functional flood-control dams; the narrative was used to politically blame the government for the disaster. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Bodies are still appearing in car parks and garages after the DANA and the government is hiding them from the official death count
claims.notes: Newtral found no evidence of hidden bodies; authorities confirmed no dead were found in Bonaire car park. Refrigerated truck images cited as 'proof' were taken out of context. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: NGOs such as Cruz Roja were sending aid to Gaza and to migrants in the Canary Islands instead of to DANA victims in Valencia
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the claims were false or taken out of context; Cruz Roja was actively operating in Valencia from the first day of the DANA. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Valencia rain radar was not working in the days before the DANA catastrophe, contributing to the failure to warn the population
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed that AEMET said the radar worked from the morning of 28 October and was active during the DANA. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Cruz Roja workers spray-painted pro-Pedro Sánchez graffiti in Paiporta during the DANA relief operation
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the image was a fabricated montage; Cruz Roja denied any involvement. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In 2018 the Ebro river discharged into the sea in just 20 days the entire water consumption of Spain for a whole year
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Newtral found the 20-day peak flow of the Ebro in 2018 was only 2,619 hm³ — less than 8.5% of Spain's total water demand and far less than the Ebro basin's own demand. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In 2021, Spain was the country where poverty increased the most in all of Europe, according to Eurostat
claims.notes: Newtral found Spain ranked ninth in Europe for poverty increase in 2021 (2.96%), with eight countries ahead of it including Portugal, Luxembourg, and Greec; Eurostat data contradicted Espinosa's claim directly. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In Spain, 70,000 companies have disappeared since the pandemic and have not recovered
claims.notes: Newtral found the INE Dirce census showed 26,235 more companies existed by January 2022 than before the pandemic; Espinosa's figure conflated cotización account codes with actual companies, which are not equivalent. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Today we have 68,000 fewer companies than before the pandemic
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the INE Dirce shows 67,466 more companies in 2022 compared to end-2019; Espinosa's figure inverted the actual direction of change. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Today we have the number of companies at its lowest level in the entire century
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed that in 2009–2010 the registered company count was lower than in June 2020, making Espinosa's 'lowest of the century' claim false on its own stated terms. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: More than 3.9 million immigrants are living in Spain without working — 58% of the total immigrant population
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed the figure mixes incompatible INE and Social Security datasets, excludes minors and retirees, and contradicts the EPA Q3-2024 figure of 615,900 unemployed foreign nationals. La Gaceta de la Iberosfera is published by Fundación Disenso, of which Abascal is a part. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: With the regularisation agreement, half a million migrants who have entered Spain illegally will be regularised
claims.impact: 2027 election context
claims.notes: Newtral cites Frontera Sur report showing only 4.5% of immigrants arrived irregularly; the majority entered by plane or car using legal routes. Abascal equated all beneficiaries with illegal arrivals, which is misleading. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: AI-generated image showing Pedro Sánchez handing Spanish passports to Maghrebi-appearing people, with a broken 'antecedentes penales' sign — implying regularisation grants nationality and ignores criminal records
claims.impact: 2027 election context
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the regularisation grants a 1-year residence authorisation, not nationality; criminal records remain a disqualifying factor. Vox shared the AI-generated image to mislead on both points. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The approximately 500,000 irregular immigrants eligible for regularisation will be able to vote in the 2027 Spanish general elections
claims.impact: 2027 election context
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed voting in Spanish general and regional elections requires nationality, which takes years to obtain; the regularisation process only grants a residence and work authorisation. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Video of a large group running along a road shows thousands of migrants coming to Spain in February 2026 because the government 'was giving away citizenship'
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the video was real but recorded near Ceuta in November 2023 during a border incident, with no relation to the 2026 regularisation. Old footage misattributed. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: A Muslim woman says that if Vox wins the general elections she will return to her country because she does not want to work and her benefits would end
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed the video was generated with artificial intelligence and contains common AI artefacts (unnatural hands, extra fingers). (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Spanish government's extraordinary regularisation of migrants involves giving them Spanish nationality
claims.impact: 2027 election context
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed the regularisation process grants only a 1-year residence authorisation, not nationality, which requires a separate years-long naturalisation process. (Translated from Spanish.) Note: source_url confirmed for HerQles immigration reform claim; this regularisation entry uses same checker.

claims.claim: Migrants arriving in the Canary Islands are being housed in 4- and 5-star hotels while Spanish citizens are neglected
claims.notes: Newtral found the 'hotels' narrative distorted emergency accommodation measures for new arrivals; the framing of luxury treatment was false and designed to stoke anti-migrant sentiment, per Newtral's 2024 year-end disinformation review. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The EU has mechanisms to annul German elections if AfD wins — promoting narrative to discredit European democratic institutions
claims.notes: EFE Verifica found that Breton's actual statement was about applying the DSA to counter foreign election interference, not annulling elections; El Ojo is identified as part of a Russia-promoted disinformation network broadcasting in Spanish. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In Torre Pacheco a war has broken out; if the government does not act, all of the Spanish people will have to go there to defend themselves
claims.notes: EFE Verifica/VerificaRTVE investigation of 115,000 X and Telegram messages found vitoquiles was the most viral account spreading false information about Torre Pacheco; the account had been previously fact-checked for false claims about a Maghrebi suspect in the Alcalá de Henares taxi driver murder. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Video showing an attack on an elderly man was filmed in Torre Pacheco during the incidents involving North African migrants
claims.notes: EFE Verifica and VerificaRTVE confirmed the video showed an assault in Almería, not Torre Pacheco; the victim was José, an elderly Spanish man beaten by two Spaniards in May. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — crime attribution and individual identification. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: There is a government list of surnames that automatically grant the right to Spanish nationality
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed no such list exists; the Spanish Foreign Ministry issued a statement as early as 2014 denying any government surname list. Vox-linked accounts spread this false claim in the context of migration regularisation debate. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Vox wants to expel 8 million immigrants and their children from Spain
claims.notes: Maldita found the Vox spokesperson said there were 8 million immigrants 'who have not adapted to our customs' and those who committed crimes should leave; media extrapolated a blanket expulsion claim that Vox denied but did not fully retract. Context included in notes as a clarification. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Spain is the most dangerous country in Europe
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed that European crime and homicide statistics do not support Spain being the most dangerous country in Europe; the claim was documented as part of Abascal's pre-election false claims in July 2023. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [Vox spread claim that] immigrants commit the majority of crimes in Spain
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Maldita documented multiple Vox false or misleading claims on immigration-crime links in the lead-up to the July 2023 general election, finding that crime statistics were misused to imply a causal link. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [False or misleading statistics on irregular immigration presented during the RTVE debate a tres — exact figure inflated]
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Maldita's real-time fact-checking of the RTVE debate a tres found multiple misleading figures; this row covers the immigration-related claims made during the broadcast. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: [False or misleading employment statistics presented during the RTVE debate a tres — including claims about job destruction]
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Maldita's verification of employment claims from the July 2023 debate found misleading figures; this row covers the employment/economic claims. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Extraordinary regularisations of migrants in Spain allow them to vote in the general elections
claims.impact: 2027 election context
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed voting in Spanish general elections requires nationality; the regularisation only grants a temporary residence and work authorisation. Source URL is AFP Factual's Migración listing page where this item is listed. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Spanish government's migrant regularisation proposal implies 'erasing' criminal records (borrar antecedentes penales)
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed applicants must not have criminal records; the measure modifies handling of police (not criminal) records in limited permit categories but does not erase criminal records. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Irregular immigrants in Spain receive 664 euros per month in welfare payments
claims.impact: 2019 election context
claims.notes: Maldita's pre-election 2019 fact-check confirmed the 664-euro figure was false; no such universal monthly payment for irregular migrants existed under Spanish law. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Irregular immigrants receive free healthcare in Spain without contributing to the social security system
claims.impact: 2019 election context
claims.notes: Maldita documented this misleading claim from the April 2019 election campaign as part of a batch of Vox-linked false claims about immigration costs. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: 98% of rape allegations in Spain are false
claims.notes: Maldita's fact-check of Vox-linked gender violence claims found this figure to be false; official statistics on false rape reports are far lower. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The gender violence law (Ley de Violencia de Género) has sent thousands of innocent men to prison
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed the claim was false; judicial statistics do not support the mass imprisonment of innocent men under the gender violence law. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: We have increased the number of poor people in Europe — 93.2 million people are at risk of poverty in the EU
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed that while the 93.2 million figure for 2024 is correct per Eurostat, EU poverty has actually decreased since 2021 (from 95.4m); Díaz's implication of a rising trend was misleading. Note: non-Vox actor — mainstream government figure; included per spec. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: People regularised in Spain under the extraordinary plan will be able to travel freely and settle in France and other EU countries
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed the Spanish residence permit does not grant free settlement rights in other EU countries; Schengen rules allow only short stays of up to 90 days in 180. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Video of boats carrying migrants filmed in 2024 in Almería is presented as footage from April 2026 — used to imply a post-regularisation surge in arrivals
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed the video was from 2024, not 2026, and had no connection to the extraordinary regularisation announced in April 2026. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Each unaccompanied migrant minor (MENA) costs Spanish taxpayers 4,700 euros per month
claims.impact: 2019 election context
claims.notes: Maldita documented this as one of several false or misleading MENA-related claims spread by Vox during the November 2019 election campaign. The 4,700-euro figure was an inflated and misleading interpretation of per-capita residential care costs. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Government rental assistance (bono alquiler) is only available to immigrants, not to Spanish citizens
claims.impact: 2019 election context
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed the rental assistance programmes cited by Vox were not restricted to immigrants; the claim was part of a coordinated set of false welfare claims in the November 2019 campaign. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The majority of homicides in Catalonia in 2023 were committed by people of non-EU origin
claims.notes: Newtral found the Mossos d'Esquadra statistics cited by Garriga were used selectively; the overall homicide rate and the share attributable to non-EU nationals were both misrepresented. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — crime attribution by national origin. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Unemployment in Spain is at record levels
