claims.notes: Newtral found unemployment in Spain was at its lowest since 2008 at the time of Espinosa's claim, not record highs; this was part of Newtral's ongoing monitoring of Vox's economic claims in Congress. (Translated from Spanish.) Note: source URL is the Newtral tag page; article-level URL not independently confirmed.

claims.claim: Spaniards are the only [EU citizens] who have less [GDP per capita] than four years ago
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed France and Germany also lost GDP per capita over the same period, contradicting Espinosa's 'only' framing. (Translated from Spanish.) Note: source URL is the Newtral tag page; article-level URL not independently confirmed.

claims.claim: Vox's electoral programme did not include eliminating AEMET [the Spanish meteorological agency]
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed Vox's 2023 programme did not mention eliminating AEMET, and Vox denied wanting to close it; the claim circulated to falsely link Vox's political positions to the DANA disaster. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Video showing crowds chanting 'Pedro Sánchez, son of a whore' was filmed in Valencia in response to the DANA
claims.notes: Maldita confirmed the original video was from Zaragoza during the 2024 Fiestas del Pilar and the audio was manipulated and recontextualised. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The central government (Pedro Sánchez) ordered 40 tonnes of donated clothes and aid to be thrown away after the DANA
claims.notes: Maldita found this was a municipal decision, not a Moncloa order; some clothes were spoiled by mud and humidity making them unsuitable for distribution. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Valencia DANA was caused by 'geoingeniería' (weather engineering); man-made technology created and directed the extreme rain event
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed no evidence supports weather engineering as the cause of the DANA; the claim belongs to the same conspiracy cluster as the HAARP narrative. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: The Manises and Cirat-Vallat dams in Valencia overflowed and caused the DANA flooding
claims.notes: Newtral confirmed both dams did not overflow; the flooding was caused by the Rambla del Poyo watercourse, not dam failure. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Video of an African immigrant screaming that he wants free money and housing promised on arriving in the UK
claims.notes: EFE Verifica confirmed the video is AI-generated; no such incident occurred. Source URL is the EFE Verifica migration topic page where this item appears. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Vox leader Abascal promised to send Spanish 'volunteers' to fight in Israel if he governs
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed Abascal made no such promise; the claim originated from a false video/audio montage. Note: source URL is the AFP Factual Spain page; cross-reference with EFE Verifica's separate check on the same claim. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: In Spain, insulting Islam (but not other religions) is punishable by imprisonment
claims.notes: AFP Factual confirmed all religions are protected equally under Spanish law; blasphemy laws were abolished in 1988 and current penalties are fines, not prison, and apply to all religions equally. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Spain needs Vox — calls for Spaniards to 'corner the invader and deport them en masse' after Torre Pacheco incidents
claims.notes: EFE Verifica/VerificaRTVE investigation found Yaxley-Lennon's post (1.4M followers) was part of the international amplification of false narratives about Torre Pacheco; he has a conviction for defamation and a history of false claims. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — foreign actor; active UK contempt/defamation history. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: A list circulating online names the supposed aggressors in the Torre Pacheco attack and links them to North African migrants
claims.notes: EFE Verifica confirmed the list had no relation to the Torre Pacheco incident and was fabricated. LEGAL REVIEW NEEDED — named individuals, crime attribution, potential defamation. (Translated from Spanish.)

claims.claim: Spain has the highest public debt in its history under the Sánchez government
claims.impact: 2023 election context
claims.notes: Maldita documented Abascal's false or misleading economic claims in the 2023 election campaign, including debt and fiscal claims that lacked the required context. (Translated from Spanish.)
